   312	
   313	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   314	// § 4 replay — TWO-MODE per C1
   315	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   316	
   317	/// **C1 NEW**: replay mode discriminator.
   318	/// - `ChainOnly`: skeleton-stage; chain integrity only (parent_state_root +
   319	///   parent_ledger_root + ledger_root chain). NOT the I-DETHASH witness.
   320	/// - `FullTransition`: CO1.7.5+ stage; verifies signatures + re-fetches payloads
   321	///   from CAS + re-runs pure transitions + asserts state_root match. THE
   322	///   I-DETHASH witness; requires CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence).
   323	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   324	pub enum ReplayMode {
   325	    ChainOnly,
   326	    FullTransition,
   327	}
   328	
   329	#[derive(Debug)]
   330	pub enum ReplayError {
   331	    LogicalTGap { at: usize, expected: u64, got: u64 },
   332	    ParentStateMismatch { at: usize },
   333	    ParentLedgerMismatch { at: usize }, // K2 NEW
   334	    LedgerRootMismatch { at: usize },
   335	    // FullTransition-mode-only (CO1.7.5+):
   336	    BadSignature { at: usize },
   337	    CasMissing { at: usize },
   338	    StateRootMismatch { at: usize },
   339	    /// CO1.7-impl A4: dispatch_transition rejected the re-run. In stub state
   340	    /// (CO1.7.5 not yet shipped), this fires on every replay step with
   341	    /// `inner = NotYetImplemented`.
   342	    Transition {
   343	        at: usize,
   344	        inner: crate::state::typed_tx::TransitionError,
   345	    },
   346	    /// CO1.7-impl A4 v1.1 (Codex bundle Q-J / C-3): the canonical-decoded
   347	    /// `TypedTx` variant disagrees with the entry's `tx_kind` discriminator.
   348	    /// Signed envelope claims one kind; CAS payload is another.
   349	    TxKindMismatch {
   350	        at: usize,
   351	        envelope_kind: TxKind,
   352	        decoded_kind: TxKind,
   353	    },
   354	    /// CO1.7-impl A4 v1.1 (Codex bundle Q-K secondary): payload bytes
   355	    /// retrieved from CAS but `canonical_decode` failed (corruption /
   356	    /// non-canonical bytes). Distinct from `CasMissing` (lookup failure).
   357	    PayloadDecode {
   358	        at: usize,
   359	        reason: String,
   360	    },
   361	}
   362	
   363	impl std::fmt::Display for ReplayError {
   364	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   365	        match self {
   366	            Self::LogicalTGap { at, expected, got } => {
   367	                write!(f, "logical_t gap at index {at}: expected {expected}, got {got}")
   368	            }
   369	            Self::ParentStateMismatch { at } => write!(f, "parent_state_root mismatch at index {at}"),
   370	            Self::ParentLedgerMismatch { at } => write!(f, "parent_ledger_root mismatch at index {at}"),
   371	            Self::LedgerRootMismatch { at } => write!(f, "ledger_root mismatch at index {at}"),
   372	            Self::BadSignature { at } => write!(f, "system_signature verify failed at index {at}"),
   373	            Self::CasMissing { at } => write!(f, "CAS payload not retrievable at index {at}"),
   374	            Self::StateRootMismatch { at } => write!(f, "resulting_state_root divergence at index {at}"),
   375	            Self::Transition { at, inner } => write!(f, "dispatch_transition rejected at index {at}: {inner}"),
   376	            Self::TxKindMismatch { at, envelope_kind, decoded_kind } => write!(
   377	                f,
   378	                "tx_kind mismatch at index {at}: envelope claims {envelope_kind:?} but CAS payload decoded as {decoded_kind:?}"
   379	            ),
   380	            Self::PayloadDecode { at, reason } => write!(f, "payload canonical_decode failed at index {at}: {reason}"),
   381	        }
   382	    }
   383	}
   384	impl std::error::Error for ReplayError {}
   385	
   386	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   387	// CO1.7-impl A4: LedgerCasView trait + replay_full_transition
   388	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   389	
   390	/// CO1.7 spec § 4 + DIV-4 closure: narrow read-only CAS trait that replay
   391	/// needs. Decouples `replay_full_transition` from full `CasStore` (the
   392	/// production impl). Anything that can hand back the bytes for a `Cid`
   393	/// satisfies this — testing can mock it; cold-replay uses CasStore directly.
   394	pub trait LedgerCasView {
   395	    fn get_typed_payload(
   396	        &self,
   397	        cid: &crate::bottom_white::cas::schema::Cid,
   398	    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ReplayError>;
   399	}
   400	
   401	impl LedgerCasView for crate::bottom_white::cas::store::CasStore {
   402	    fn get_typed_payload(
   403	        &self,
   404	        cid: &crate::bottom_white::cas::schema::Cid,
   405	    ) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ReplayError> {
   406	        self.get(cid).map_err(|_| ReplayError::CasMissing { at: 0 })
   407	    }
   408	}
   409	
   410	/// CO1.7-impl A4 — full-mode replay (THE I-DETHASH witness).
   411	///
   412	/// Validates **every** stage spec § 4 + § 6 promises:
   413	/// 1. logical_t monotonicity
   414	/// 2. parent_state_root chain
   415	/// 3. parent_ledger_root chain (K2 transplant defense)
   416	/// 4. system_signature verifies via CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning + pinned pubkeys
   417	/// 5. CAS lookup of tx_payload_cid succeeds (CO1.4-extra cold-replay capability)
   418	/// 6. canonical_decode of payload bytes → TypedTx
   419	/// 6.5 (v1.1 C-3): decoded_typed_tx.tx_kind() MUST equal entry.tx_kind
   420	/// 7. dispatch_transition re-run produces (q_next, _signals)
   421	/// 8. q_next.state_root_t matches entry.resulting_state_root
   422	/// 9. resulting_ledger_root recomputed via append() matches stored
   423	///
   424	/// **v1.1 C-1 closure**: takes a full `genesis: &QState` (was `genesis_state_root`
   425	/// + `genesis_ledger_root` only). Caller provides the complete genesis state
   426	/// so dispatch_transition can read budget / registries / balances / task markets
   427	/// — fabricating `QState::genesis()` was dropping these fields.
   428	///
   429	/// **Stub-state caveat (CO1.7.5 unblocks)**: while `dispatch_transition`
   430	/// returns `NotYetImplemented` for every variant, replay errors at stage 7
   431	/// for any non-empty chain. Conformance tests exercising stages 1-6.5
   432	/// independently are `#[test]`-runnable now; full state_root reconstruction
   433	/// gates on CO1.7.5.
   434	pub fn replay_full_transition(
   435	    genesis: &crate::state::q_state::QState,
   436	    entries: &[LedgerEntry],
   437	    cas: &dyn LedgerCasView,
   438	    pinned_pubkeys: &crate::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::PinnedSystemPubkeys,
   439	    predicate_registry: &crate::top_white::predicates::registry::PredicateRegistry,
   440	    tool_registry: &crate::bottom_white::tools::registry::ToolRegistry,
   441	) -> Result<crate::state::q_state::QState, ReplayError> {
   442	    use crate::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::{
   443	        verify_system_signature, CanonicalMessage,
   444	    };
   445	    use crate::state::sequencer::dispatch_transition;
   446	
   447	    let mut q = genesis.clone();
   448	
   449	    for (i, entry) in entries.iter().enumerate() {
   450	        // Stage 1
   451	        let expected_logical_t = (i as u64) + 1;
   452	        if entry.logical_t != expected_logical_t {
   453	            return Err(ReplayError::LogicalTGap {
   454	                at: i,
   455	                expected: expected_logical_t,
   456	                got: entry.logical_t,
   457	            });
   458	        }
   459	        // Stage 2
   460	        if entry.parent_state_root != q.state_root_t {
   461	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentStateMismatch { at: i });
   462	        }
   463	        // Stage 3
   464	        if entry.parent_ledger_root != q.ledger_root_t {
   465	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentLedgerMismatch { at: i });
   466	        }
   467	
   468	        // Stage 4: system_signature verify (FullTransition mode only).
   469	        let signing_payload = entry.to_signing_payload();
   470	        let signing_digest = signing_payload.canonical_digest();
   471	        let canonical_msg = CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning(signing_digest.0);
   472	        if !verify_system_signature(
   473	            &entry.system_signature,
   474	            &canonical_msg,
   475	            entry.epoch,
   476	            pinned_pubkeys,
   477	        ) {
   478	            return Err(ReplayError::BadSignature { at: i });
   479	        }
   480	
   481	        // Stage 5: CAS lookup.
   482	        let payload_bytes = cas
   483	            .get_typed_payload(&entry.tx_payload_cid)
   484	            .map_err(|_| ReplayError::CasMissing { at: i })?;
   485	
   486	        // Stage 6: canonical_decode → TypedTx (v1.1 C-3-secondary: distinct
   487	        // error from CasMissing).
   488	        let typed_tx: crate::state::typed_tx::TypedTx =
   489	            canonical_decode(&payload_bytes).map_err(|e| ReplayError::PayloadDecode {
   490	                at: i,
   491	                reason: e.to_string(),
   492	            })?;
   493	
   494	        // Stage 6.5 (v1.1 C-3): tx_kind envelope vs decoded payload kind MUST match.
   495	        // Otherwise a signed envelope claiming `Work` could ride a CAS payload
   496	        // that decodes as `Verify` — sequencer would have written that
   497	        // mismatch but replay would have silently accepted it pre-v1.1.
   498	        let decoded_kind = typed_tx.tx_kind();
   499	        if decoded_kind != entry.tx_kind {
   500	            return Err(ReplayError::TxKindMismatch {
   501	                at: i,
   502	                envelope_kind: entry.tx_kind,
   503	                decoded_kind,
   504	            });
   505	        }
   506	
   507	        // Stage 7: re-run pure dispatch_transition.
   508	        let (q_next, _signals) =
   509	            dispatch_transition(&q, &typed_tx, predicate_registry, tool_registry)
   510	                .map_err(|inner| ReplayError::Transition { at: i, inner })?;
   511	
   512	        // Stage 8: state_root match.
   513	        if q_next.state_root_t != entry.resulting_state_root {
   514	            return Err(ReplayError::StateRootMismatch { at: i });
   515	        }
   516	
   517	        // Stage 9: ledger_root match (recompute via append).
   518	        let recomputed_ledger_root = append(&q.ledger_root_t, &signing_digest);
   519	        if recomputed_ledger_root != entry.resulting_ledger_root {
   520	            return Err(ReplayError::LedgerRootMismatch { at: i });
   521	        }
   522	
   523	        // Advance.
   524	        q = q_next;
   525	        q.ledger_root_t = entry.resulting_ledger_root;
   526	    }
   527	
   528	    Ok(q)
   529	}
   530	
   531	/// Skeleton-stage entry point (v1.1).
   532	///
   533	/// Validates:
   534	/// 1. logical_t monotonicity (no gaps, no duplicates)
   535	/// 2. parent_state_root chain
   536	/// 3. parent_ledger_root chain (K2 transplant defense)
   537	/// 4. resulting_ledger_root recomputed via append(prev_ledger_root, signing_digest)
   538	///
   539	/// Does NOT verify:
   540	/// - system_signature (CO1.7.5+: requires CanonicalMessage extension wired through keypair)
   541	/// - resulting_state_root (CO1.7.5+: requires dispatch_transition + CO1.4-extra CAS persistence)
   542	///
   543	/// Returns final (state_root, ledger_root) on success.
   544	pub fn replay_chain_integrity(
   545	    genesis_state_root: Hash,
   546	    genesis_ledger_root: Hash,
   547	    entries: &[LedgerEntry],
   548	) -> Result<(Hash, Hash), ReplayError> {
   549	    let mut prev_state_root = genesis_state_root;
   550	    let mut prev_ledger_root = genesis_ledger_root;
   551	
   552	    for (i, entry) in entries.iter().enumerate() {
   553	        let expected_logical_t = (i as u64) + 1;
   554	        if entry.logical_t != expected_logical_t {
   555	            return Err(ReplayError::LogicalTGap {
   556	                at: i,
   557	                expected: expected_logical_t,
   558	                got: entry.logical_t,
   559	            });
   560	        }
   561	        if entry.parent_state_root != prev_state_root {
   562	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentStateMismatch { at: i });
   563	        }
   564	        // K2 NEW: parent_ledger_root chain check
   565	        if entry.parent_ledger_root != prev_ledger_root {
   566	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentLedgerMismatch { at: i });
   567	        }
   568	        let signing_digest = entry.to_signing_payload().canonical_digest();
   569	        let recomputed = append(&prev_ledger_root, &signing_digest);
   570	        if recomputed != entry.resulting_ledger_root {
   571	            return Err(ReplayError::LedgerRootMismatch { at: i });
   572	        }
   573	        prev_state_root = entry.resulting_state_root;
   574	        prev_ledger_root = entry.resulting_ledger_root;
   575	    }
   576	
   577	    Ok((prev_state_root, prev_ledger_root))
   578	}
   579	
   580	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   581	// § 2.5 Canonical serialization (bincode v2; STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 2.5)
   582	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   583	
   584	/// `bincode::config` used for the canonical `LedgerEntry` wire format.
   585	///
   586	/// **Frozen choices** (per STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 2.5):
   587	/// - **Big-endian** byte order (network order; deterministic across platforms).
   588	/// - **Fixed-int encoding** (no varint; fixed-width for byte-stable round-trip).
   589	/// - **`BTreeMap` keys**: bincode iterates the map in serde-supplied order; we
   590	///   only ever encode `BTreeMap` (sorted by construction) so key order is lex.
   591	/// - **No padding, no implicit alignment.**
   592	fn bincode_canonical_config() -> impl bincode::config::Config {
   593	    bincode::config::standard()
   594	        .with_big_endian()
   595	        .with_fixed_int_encoding()
   596	}
   597	
   598	/// Canonical encode any serde-Serialize value to bytes (CO1.7 wire format).
   599	/// Used by `Git2LedgerWriter` for commit-message bodies and by future callers
   600	/// needing byte-stable signatures over typed payloads.
   601	pub fn canonical_encode<T: Serialize>(value: &T) -> Result<Vec<u8>, CanonicalCodecError> {
   602	    bincode::serde::encode_to_vec(value, bincode_canonical_config())
   603	        .map_err(|e| CanonicalCodecError::Encode(e.to_string()))
   604	}
   605	
   606	/// Canonical decode the inverse of `canonical_encode`. Returns the value plus
   607	/// the number of bytes consumed (entire input must be consumed for a clean decode).
   608	pub fn canonical_decode<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(
   609	    bytes: &[u8],
   610	) -> Result<T, CanonicalCodecError> {
   611	    let (value, consumed) =
   612	        bincode::serde::decode_from_slice::<T, _>(bytes, bincode_canonical_config())
   613	            .map_err(|e| CanonicalCodecError::Decode(e.to_string()))?;
   614	    if consumed != bytes.len() {
   615	        return Err(CanonicalCodecError::TrailingBytes {
   616	            consumed,
   617	            total: bytes.len(),
   618	        });
   619	    }
   620	    Ok(value)

     1	//! Constitution Landing First 2026-05-07 (HARNESS.md §3 G-009; architect
     2	//! ruling 2026-05-07): C1 immediate `HEAD_t` witness.
     3	//!
     4	//! ## Why
     5	//!
     6	//! Art. 0.4 (`Q_t` version-controlled) and the architect's G-009 Path-C-hybrid
     7	//! ruling require a derived 6-field witness over Q_t that audit can use to
     8	//! answer "where did the tape head sit at this transition?" without reading
     9	//! private state. CLAUDE.md §4.1 pins the schema:
    10	//!
    11	//! ```text
    12	//! HEAD_t = {
    13	//!   state_root,
    14	//!   l4_head,
    15	//!   l4e_head,
    16	//!   cas_root,
    17	//!   economic_state_root,
    18	//!   run_id
    19	//! }
    20	//! ```
    21	//!
    22	//! The C1 witness is **derived** from existing `QState` fields plus the L4.E
    23	//! head + CAS root + run_id passed by the caller. Keeping it derived (not
    24	//! a new persisted field) avoids touching canonical signing payload — which
    25	//! is Class-4 — while still landing the architect's required witness shape
    26	//! for FC2 replay assertions and dashboard lookups.
    27	//!
    28	//! Path-C2 production (libgit2-backed `refs/chaintape/{l4, l4e, cas}`) is the
    29	//! forward step; this module is the immediate C1 witness.
    30	//!
    31	//! `FC-trace: FC1-INV1 + FC2-INV1 + Art-0.4 + G-009`.
    32	
    33	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    34	use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};
    35	
    36	use crate::bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger::canonical_encode;
    37	use crate::state::q_state::{EconomicState, Hash, NodeId, QState};
    38	
    39	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N45 + Art-0.4: six-field C1 `HEAD_t` witness
    40	/// (architect-pinned 2026-05-07 §4.1).
    41	///
    42	/// Derived from `QState` + caller-supplied L4.E head + CAS root + run_id.
    43	/// Two field counts are CONSTITUTIONALLY meaningful:
    44	///   - 6 fields total — pinned by `head_t_witness_has_six_canonical_fields`.
    45	///   - `l4e_head` and `cas_root` are `Option` because pre-genesis / pre-CAS
    46	///     boot states have no head; downstream gates assert presence on
    47	///     post-genesis snapshots.
    48	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    49	pub struct HeadTWitness {
    50	    /// Materialized state Merkle root (from `QState.state_root_t`).
    51	    pub state_root: Hash,
    52	    /// L4 accepted-ledger head (from `QState.head_t`).
    53	    pub l4_head: NodeId,
    54	    /// L4.E rejection-ledger head (caller-supplied; `None` pre-genesis).
    55	    pub l4e_head: Option<NodeId>,
    56	    /// CAS root hash (caller-supplied; `None` when CAS index is empty).
    57	    pub cas_root: Option<Hash>,
    58	    /// `EconomicState` canonical digest (`QState.economic_state_t.canonical_digest()`).
    59	    pub economic_state_root: Hash,
    60	    /// Run identifier — caller-supplied, sourced from `genesis_payload.toml`
    61	    /// or the bootstrap run-id assignment.
    62	    pub run_id: String,
    63	}
    64	
    65	impl HeadTWitness {
    66	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N45: synthesize the witness from a Q-state plus the
    67	    /// L4.E head, CAS root, and run_id known by the caller. **Derived** view
    68	    /// — does not mutate `QState`. The `&QState` (read-only) signature is
    69	    /// the structural pin behind `dashboard_reads_head_t_derived_state`.
    70	    pub fn from_q_state(
    71	        q: &QState,
    72	        run_id: impl Into<String>,
    73	        l4e_head: Option<NodeId>,
    74	        cas_root: Option<Hash>,
    75	    ) -> Self {
    76	        Self {
    77	            state_root: q.state_root_t,
    78	            l4_head: q.head_t.clone(),
    79	            l4e_head,
    80	            cas_root,
    81	            economic_state_root: economic_state_canonical_root(&q.economic_state_t),
    82	            run_id: run_id.into(),
    83	        }
    84	    }
    85	
    86	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N45: canonical root hash of an `EconomicState`
    87	    /// (sha256 over its canonical bincode encoding). Used as the witness
    88	    /// `economic_state_root` field and exposed for downstream
    89	    /// replay-equivalence checks.
    90	    pub fn economic_state_canonical_root(econ: &EconomicState) -> Hash {
    91	        economic_state_canonical_root(econ)
    92	    }
    93	
    94	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4): reconstruct the C2 witness from `refs/chaintape/{l4,l4e,cas}` ref OIDs alone, plus caller-supplied state/economic/run_id (which MUST be reconstructed from the chain by the caller). Returns `Some` iff at least the L4 ref exists; pre-genesis returns `None`. Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.3 + SG-A3.4 replay-byte-equality.
    95	    pub fn reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs(
    96	        repo_path: &std::path::Path,
    97	        run_id: impl Into<String>,
    98	        state_root: Hash,
    99	        economic_state_root: Hash,
   100	    ) -> Result<Option<Self>, crate::bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger::LedgerWriterError>
   101	    {
   102	        use crate::bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger::Git2LedgerWriter;
   103	        let l4_oid = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_l4(repo_path)?;
   104	        let l4_oid = match l4_oid {
   105	            Some(o) => o,
   106	            None => return Ok(None),
   107	        };
   108	        let l4e_oid = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_l4e(repo_path)?;
   109	        let cas_oid = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_cas(repo_path)?;
   110	
   111	        // Map git OIDs to NodeId (40-hex string) and Hash (raw bytes).
   112	        let l4_head = NodeId(l4_oid.to_string());
   113	        let l4e_head = l4e_oid.map(|o| NodeId(o.to_string()));
   114	        // Git2 OIDs are 20 bytes (SHA-1) or 32 bytes (SHA-256 repos); hash
   115	        // them with sha256 to canonicalize into the 32-byte Hash shape.
   116	        // This is a derived view; the canonical chain-side OID lives in the
   117	        // ref itself.
   118	        let cas_root = cas_oid.map(|o| {
   119	            let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
   120	            hasher.update(b"chaintape/cas/oid");
   121	            hasher.update(o.as_bytes());
   122	            Hash(hasher.finalize().into())
   123	        });
   124	
   125	        Ok(Some(Self {
   126	            state_root,
   127	            l4_head,
   128	            l4e_head,
   129	            cas_root,
   130	            economic_state_root,
   131	            run_id: run_id.into(),
   132	        }))
   133	    }
   134	
   135	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N45: canonical hash of the witness (sha256 of
   136	    /// canonical-encoded bytes). Two witnesses with identical 6-field
   137	    /// contents MUST produce identical canonical hashes — this is what
   138	    /// `head_t_reconstructs_from_replay` pins.
   139	    pub fn canonical_hash(&self) -> Hash {
   140	        // We hash a stable concatenation of the canonical fields rather than
   141	        // depending on an external canonical-encoder; this keeps the C1
   142	        // witness self-contained and lets gates assert hash stability
   143	        // without crossing module boundaries.
   144	        let mut h = Sha256::new();
   145	        h.update(b"HEAD_t/v1");
   146	        h.update(self.state_root.0);
   147	        h.update(self.l4_head.0.as_bytes());
   148	        match &self.l4e_head {
   149	            Some(n) => {
   150	                h.update([1u8]);
   151	                h.update(n.0.as_bytes());
   152	            }
   153	            None => h.update([0u8]),
   154	        }
   155	        match &self.cas_root {
   156	            Some(r) => {
   157	                h.update([1u8]);
   158	                h.update(r.0);
   159	            }
   160	            None => h.update([0u8]),
   161	        }
   162	        h.update(self.economic_state_root.0);
   163	        h.update(self.run_id.as_bytes());
   164	        Hash(h.finalize().into())
   165	    }
   166	}
   167	
   168	/// Free helper — sha256 over canonical bincode of an EconomicState.
   169	/// Falls back to all-zeros only on a codec error (which would indicate a
   170	/// catastrophic schema bug; surface it via panic in tests rather than mask).
   171	fn economic_state_canonical_root(econ: &EconomicState) -> Hash {
   172	    let bytes = canonical_encode(econ).expect(
   173	        "EconomicState canonical_encode is infallible for serde-Serialize types — \
   174	         a failure here means the schema is broken and the witness cannot be trusted",
   175	    );
   176	    let mut h = Sha256::new();
   177	    h.update(b"EconomicState/v1");
   178	    h.update(&bytes);
   179	    Hash(h.finalize().into())
   180	}
   181	
   182	#[cfg(test)]
   183	mod tests {
   184	    use super::*;
   185	
   186	    fn fixture_q_with_head(head_oid: &str) -> QState {
   187	        let mut q = QState::genesis();
   188	        q.head_t = NodeId(head_oid.into());
   189	        q.state_root_t = Hash([0x11; 32]);
   190	        q
   191	    }
   192	
   193	    #[test]
   194	    fn six_field_count() {
   195	        let q = QState::genesis();
   196	        let w = HeadTWitness::from_q_state(&q, "run-1", None, None);
   197	        let v = serde_json::to_value(&w).expect("serialize");
   198	        let obj = v.as_object().expect("witness is a JSON object");
   199	        assert_eq!(
   200	            obj.len(),
   201	            6,
   202	            "HEAD_t witness must have exactly 6 canonical fields per architect §4.1; got {}",
   203	            obj.len()
   204	        );
   205	        for f in [
   206	            "state_root",
   207	            "l4_head",
   208	            "l4e_head",
   209	            "cas_root",
   210	            "economic_state_root",
   211	            "run_id",
   212	        ] {
   213	            assert!(
   214	                obj.contains_key(f),
   215	                "HEAD_t witness missing canonical field `{f}`"
   216	            );
   217	        }
   218	    }
   219	
   220	    #[test]
   221	    fn advances_on_l4_head_change() {
   222	        let q1 = fixture_q_with_head(&"a".repeat(40));
   223	        let q2 = fixture_q_with_head(&"b".repeat(40));
   224	        let w1 = HeadTWitness::from_q_state(&q1, "run-1", None, None);
   225	        let w2 = HeadTWitness::from_q_state(&q2, "run-1", None, None);
   226	        assert_ne!(w1.l4_head, w2.l4_head);
   227	        assert_ne!(w1.canonical_hash(), w2.canonical_hash());
   228	    }
   229	
   230	    #[test]
   231	    fn does_not_advance_on_l4e_only() {
   232	        let q = fixture_q_with_head(&"a".repeat(40));
   233	        let w_pre = HeadTWitness::from_q_state(&q, "run-1", None, None);
   234	        let w_post = HeadTWitness::from_q_state(
   235	            &q,
   236	            "run-1",
   237	            Some(NodeId("ff".repeat(20))),
   238	            None,
   239	        );
   240	        assert_eq!(
   241	            w_pre.l4_head, w_post.l4_head,
   242	            "l4_head must NOT advance when only L4.E events occur"
   243	        );
   244	        assert_ne!(w_pre.l4e_head, w_post.l4e_head);
   245	        assert_ne!(
   246	            w_pre.canonical_hash(),
   247	            w_post.canonical_hash(),
   248	            "canonical hash must reflect l4e_head changes"
   249	        );
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    #[test]
   253	    fn reconstructs_from_replay() {
   254	        let q = fixture_q_with_head(&"a".repeat(40));
   255	        let w_a = HeadTWitness::from_q_state(
   256	            &q,
   257	            "run-1",
   258	            Some(NodeId("ff".repeat(20))),
   259	            Some(Hash([0x22; 32])),
   260	        );
   261	        // Same inputs → same witness → same canonical hash. This is the
   262	        // replay-equivalence property dashboards rely on.
   263	        let w_b = HeadTWitness::from_q_state(
   264	            &q,
   265	            "run-1",
   266	            Some(NodeId("ff".repeat(20))),
   267	            Some(Hash([0x22; 32])),
   268	        );
   269	        assert_eq!(w_a, w_b);
   270	        assert_eq!(w_a.canonical_hash(), w_b.canonical_hash());
   271	    }
   272	}

     1	//! Stage A3 — HEAD_t C2 multi-ref ChainTape ship gates.
     2	//!
     3	//! Per `STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md` §4 Ship Gates:
     4	//!
     5	//! ```text
     6	//! SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1  L4 head ref advances on accepted transition
     7	//! SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2  L4.E head ref advances on rejected evidence
     8	//! SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.3  CAS root ref advances when CAS evidence added
     9	//! SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4  Replay reconstructs HEAD_t (six-field byte equality)
    10	//! SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5  No hidden filesystem pointer
    11	//! ```
    12	//!
    13	//! These tests are the executable face of FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1..7 +
    14	//! CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 (the three named Git refs ARE the canonical pointer).
    15	//! The C1 baseline `refs/transitions/main` is preserved as a backward-compat
    16	//! alias; every accepted transition dual-writes to `refs/chaintape/l4`.
    17	//!
    18	//! `FC-trace: FC2-INV1 + Art-0.4 + G-009 Path C C2`.
    19	
    20	use std::fs;
    21	use std::path::Path;
    22	use std::path::PathBuf;
    23	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};
    24	
    25	use git2::Repository;
    26	use turingosv4::bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger::{
    27	    append, Git2LedgerWriter, LedgerEntry, LedgerEntrySigningPayload, LedgerWriter, TxKind,
    28	    CHAINTAPE_CAS_REF, CHAINTAPE_L4E_REF, CHAINTAPE_L4_REF,
    29	};
    30	use turingosv4::bottom_white::cas::schema::Cid;
    31	use turingosv4::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::{SystemEpoch, SystemSignature};
    32	use turingosv4::state::head_t_witness::HeadTWitness;
    33	use turingosv4::state::q_state::Hash;
    34	
    35	static TEST_COUNTER: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);
    36	
    37	fn fresh_repo() -> (tempfile::TempDir, PathBuf, Git2LedgerWriter) {
    38	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().expect("tempdir");
    39	    let path = tmp.path().to_path_buf();
    40	    let writer = Git2LedgerWriter::open(&path).expect("open");
    41	    (tmp, path, writer)
    42	}
    43	
    44	/// Build a minimally-valid LedgerEntry at logical_t with deterministic fields.
    45	fn entry_at(
    46	    logical_t: u64,
    47	    parent_state_root: Hash,
    48	    parent_ledger_root: Hash,
    49	    resulting_state_root: Hash,
    50	) -> LedgerEntry {
    51	    let signing = LedgerEntrySigningPayload {
    52	        logical_t,
    53	        parent_state_root,
    54	        parent_ledger_root,
    55	        tx_kind: TxKind::Work,
    56	        tx_payload_cid: Cid([0u8; 32]),
    57	        resulting_state_root,
    58	        timestamp_logical: logical_t,
    59	        epoch: SystemEpoch::new(1),
    60	        extensions: std::collections::BTreeMap::new(),
    61	    };
    62	    let signing_digest = signing.canonical_digest();
    63	    let resulting_ledger_root = append(&parent_ledger_root, &signing_digest);
    64	    LedgerEntry {
    65	        logical_t,
    66	        parent_state_root: signing.parent_state_root,
    67	        parent_ledger_root: signing.parent_ledger_root,
    68	        tx_kind: signing.tx_kind,
    69	        tx_payload_cid: signing.tx_payload_cid,
    70	        resulting_state_root: signing.resulting_state_root,
    71	        resulting_ledger_root,
    72	        timestamp_logical: signing.timestamp_logical,
    73	        epoch: signing.epoch,
    74	        extensions: signing.extensions,
    75	        system_signature: SystemSignature::from_bytes([0u8; 64]),
    76	    }
    77	}
    78	
    79	/// Helper — synthesize a Git commit OID by writing a simple blob+tree+commit.
    80	/// Used for SG-A3.2/3 to advance the L4.E and CAS refs to deterministic OIDs
    81	/// without requiring the full L4.E or CAS subsystems to participate.
    82	fn synth_commit_oid(repo_path: &Path, marker: &str) -> git2::Oid {
    83	    let repo = Repository::open(repo_path).expect("open repo");
    84	    let counter = TEST_COUNTER.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) as i64;
    85	    let blob = repo.blob(marker.as_bytes()).expect("blob");
    86	    let mut tb = repo.treebuilder(None).expect("treebuilder");
    87	    tb.insert("payload", blob, 0o100644).expect("tree insert");
    88	    let tree_oid = tb.write().expect("tree write");
    89	    let tree = repo.find_tree(tree_oid).expect("find_tree");
    90	    // Use deterministic time so OIDs are reproducible.
    91	    let time = git2::Time::new(counter, 0);
    92	    let sig = git2::Signature::new("test", "test@local", &time).expect("sig");
    93	    let commit_oid = repo
    94	        .commit(None, &sig, &sig, marker, &tree, &[])
    95	        .expect("commit");
    96	    commit_oid
    97	}
    98	
    99	/// SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1 — accepting one L4 transition advances `refs/chaintape/l4`
   100	/// and the new ref OID equals the C1 alias OID (dual-write semantics).
   101	#[test]
   102	fn sg_a3_l4_head_ref_advances_on_accepted_transition() {
   103	    let (_tmp, path, mut writer) = fresh_repo();
   104	
   105	    // Pre-state: no chaintape/l4 ref.
   106	    let pre = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_l4(&path).expect("read pre");
   107	    assert!(pre.is_none(), "fresh repo must have no chaintape/l4 ref");
   108	
   109	    // Append one entry; C1 + C2 dual-write fires.
   110	    let e = entry_at(1, Hash::ZERO, Hash::ZERO, Hash([0x11; 32]));
   111	    let _ = writer.commit(&e).expect("commit");
   112	
   113	    // Post-state: chaintape/l4 exists and equals C1 head.
   114	    let l4_post = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_l4(&path)
   115	        .expect("read l4")
   116	        .expect("l4 ref present after commit");
   117	    let c1_post = writer.head_commit_oid().expect("c1 head");
   118	    assert_eq!(
   119	        l4_post, c1_post,
   120	        "dual-write: refs/chaintape/l4 OID must equal refs/transitions/main OID"
   121	    );
   122	
   123	    // A second commit advances the ref.
   124	    let e2 = entry_at(2, Hash([0x11; 32]), e.resulting_ledger_root, Hash([0x22; 32]));
   125	    let _ = writer.commit(&e2).expect("commit-2");
   126	    let l4_post_2 = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_l4(&path)
   127	        .expect("read l4-2")
   128	        .expect("l4 ref present after commit-2");
   129	    assert_ne!(
   130	        l4_post, l4_post_2,
   131	        "refs/chaintape/l4 must advance on each accepted transition"
   132	    );
   133	    assert_eq!(
   134	        l4_post_2,
   135	        writer.head_commit_oid().expect("c1 head 2"),
   136	        "dual-write semantics must hold after 2nd commit"
   137	    );
   138	}
   139	
   140	/// SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 — `advance_chaintape_l4e_to` advances `refs/chaintape/l4e`
   141	/// to the supplied OID, idempotently overwriting any prior value.
   142	#[test]
   143	fn sg_a3_l4e_head_ref_advances_on_rejected_evidence() {
   144	    let (_tmp, path, _writer) = fresh_repo();
   145	
   146	    // Pre: no l4e ref.
   147	    let pre = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_l4e(&path).expect("read pre");
   148	    assert!(pre.is_none(), "fresh repo must have no chaintape/l4e ref");
   149	
   150	    // Synthesize a deterministic L4.E commit OID, then advance the ref.
   151	    let oid_a = synth_commit_oid(&path, "l4e_record_1");
   152	    Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_l4e_to(&path, oid_a, "L4.E append #1")
   153	        .expect("advance l4e");
   154	    let post_a = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_l4e(&path)
   155	        .expect("read l4e")
   156	        .expect("l4e ref present after advance");
   157	    assert_eq!(post_a, oid_a);
   158	
   159	    // A second advance moves the ref atomically.
   160	    let oid_b = synth_commit_oid(&path, "l4e_record_2");
   161	    Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_l4e_to(&path, oid_b, "L4.E append #2")
   162	        .expect("advance l4e #2");
   163	    let post_b = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_l4e(&path)
   164	        .expect("read l4e #2")
   165	        .expect("l4e ref present after second advance");
   166	    assert_eq!(post_b, oid_b);
   167	    assert_ne!(post_a, post_b, "l4e ref must advance on each rejected evidence record");
   168	}
   169	
   170	/// SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.3 — `advance_chaintape_cas_to` advances `refs/chaintape/cas`
   171	/// to the supplied OID per CAS write batch.
   172	#[test]
   173	fn sg_a3_cas_root_ref_advances_on_cas_write() {
   174	    let (_tmp, path, _writer) = fresh_repo();
   175	
   176	    let pre = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_cas(&path).expect("read pre");
   177	    assert!(pre.is_none(), "fresh repo must have no chaintape/cas ref");
   178	
   179	    let oid_a = synth_commit_oid(&path, "cas_batch_1");
   180	    Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_cas_to(&path, oid_a, "CAS write #1")
   181	        .expect("advance cas");
   182	    let post_a = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_cas(&path)
   183	        .expect("read cas")
   184	        .expect("cas ref present after advance");
   185	    assert_eq!(post_a, oid_a);
   186	
   187	    let oid_b = synth_commit_oid(&path, "cas_batch_2");
   188	    Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_cas_to(&path, oid_b, "CAS write #2")
   189	        .expect("advance cas #2");
   190	    let post_b = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_cas(&path)
   191	        .expect("read cas #2")
   192	        .expect("cas ref present after second advance");
   193	    assert_eq!(post_b, oid_b);
   194	    assert_ne!(post_a, post_b);
   195	}
   196	
   197	/// SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 — Replay reconstructs HEAD_t from refs alone (six-field
   198	/// byte equality) without requiring `&QState`. The L4 head, L4.E head, and
   199	/// CAS root come purely from `refs/chaintape/{l4,l4e,cas}`.
   200	#[test]
   201	fn sg_a3_replay_reconstructs_head_t_from_refs() {
   202	    let (_tmp, path, mut writer) = fresh_repo();
   203	
   204	    // Build a small history: 1 L4 commit + 1 L4.E advance + 1 CAS advance.
   205	    let e = entry_at(1, Hash::ZERO, Hash::ZERO, Hash([0x11; 32]));
   206	    let _ = writer.commit(&e).expect("commit");
   207	    let l4e_oid = synth_commit_oid(&path, "l4e_replay_test");
   208	    Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_l4e_to(&path, l4e_oid, "L4.E for replay")
   209	        .expect("advance l4e");
   210	    let cas_oid = synth_commit_oid(&path, "cas_replay_test");
   211	    Git2LedgerWriter::advance_chaintape_cas_to(&path, cas_oid, "CAS for replay")
   212	        .expect("advance cas");
   213	
   214	    // Reconstruct using a fixed state_root + economic_state_root + run_id.
   215	    // (These come from QState replay in production; we synthesize them here
   216	    // to test the ref-side reconstruction in isolation.)
   217	    let state_root = Hash([0x33; 32]);
   218	    let econ_root = Hash([0x44; 32]);
   219	    let w1 = HeadTWitness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs(
   220	        &path,
   221	        "test-run-1",
   222	        state_root,
   223	        econ_root,
   224	    )
   225	    .expect("reconstruct ok")
   226	    .expect("non-empty witness");
   227	
   228	    // Six canonical fields populated from refs.
   229	    assert_eq!(w1.state_root, state_root);
   230	    assert_eq!(
   231	        w1.l4_head.0,
   232	        writer.head_commit_oid().expect("c1 head").to_string(),
   233	        "l4_head must equal refs/chaintape/l4 OID"
   234	    );
   235	    assert!(w1.l4e_head.is_some(), "l4e_head must reflect refs/chaintape/l4e");
   236	    assert_eq!(
   237	        w1.l4e_head.as_ref().unwrap().0,
   238	        l4e_oid.to_string(),
   239	        "l4e_head must match the advanced ref"
   240	    );
   241	    assert!(w1.cas_root.is_some(), "cas_root must reflect refs/chaintape/cas");
   242	    assert_eq!(w1.economic_state_root, econ_root);
   243	    assert_eq!(w1.run_id, "test-run-1");
   244	
   245	    // Byte-equality on canonical hash for two reconstructions with identical refs.
   246	    let w2 = HeadTWitness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs(
   247	        &path,
   248	        "test-run-1",
   249	        state_root,
   250	        econ_root,
   251	    )
   252	    .expect("reconstruct ok")
   253	    .expect("non-empty witness");
   254	    assert_eq!(w1, w2, "reconstruction must be deterministic from refs");
   255	    assert_eq!(
   256	        w1.canonical_hash(),
   257	        w2.canonical_hash(),
   258	        "FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 byte-equality witness"
   259	    );
   260	
   261	    // Pre-genesis (no l4 ref) returns None.
   262	    let (_tmp_empty, empty_path, _w_empty) = fresh_repo();
   263	    let w_empty = HeadTWitness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs(
   264	        &empty_path,
   265	        "test-run-empty",
   266	        state_root,
   267	        econ_root,
   268	    )
   269	    .expect("reconstruct on empty ok");
   270	    assert!(w_empty.is_none(), "pre-genesis reconstruction must return None");
   271	}
   272	
   273	/// SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 — No hidden filesystem pointer. The three named Git
   274	/// refs ARE the canonical pointer (per CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5). This test grep-
   275	/// checks the source tree for forbidden filesystem-pointer file names.
   276	#[test]
   277	fn sg_a3_no_hidden_filesystem_pointer() {
   278	    let forbidden = [
   279	        "LATEST_HEAD_T.txt",
   280	        "CURRENT_RUN.json",
   281	        "GLOBAL_HEAD_POINTER",
   282	        "RUN_LATEST_POINTER",
   283	        "head_t_pointer.txt",
   284	    ];
   285	    // Walk src/ and check no .rs file references a forbidden pointer file.
   286	    let src = Path::new("src");
   287	    walk_and_check(src, &forbidden);
   288	    // Also ensure the matrix does not declare a forbidden pointer as canonical.
   289	    let matrix_path = "handover/alignment/CONSTITUTION_EXECUTION_MATRIX.md";
   290	    if Path::new(matrix_path).exists() {
   291	        let body = fs::read_to_string(matrix_path).expect("matrix");
   292	        for f in &forbidden {
   293	            assert!(
   294	                !body.contains(f),
   295	                "CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 violation: matrix references forbidden pointer `{f}`"
   296	            );
   297	        }
   298	    }
   299	}
   300	
   301	fn walk_and_check(dir: &Path, forbidden: &[&str]) {
   302	    if !dir.exists() {
   303	        return;
   304	    }
   305	    let entries = fs::read_dir(dir).expect("read_dir");
   306	    for entry in entries.flatten() {
   307	        let path = entry.path();
   308	        if path.is_dir() {
   309	            walk_and_check(&path, forbidden);
   310	        } else if path.extension().map(|e| e == "rs").unwrap_or(false) {
   311	            let body = match fs::read_to_string(&path) {
   312	                Ok(s) => s,
   313	                Err(_) => continue,
   314	            };
   315	            for f in forbidden {
   316	                // Ignore comments that explicitly mention forbidden names as
   317	                // banned (so this very file's forbidden-list doesn't trip).
   318	                let is_test_file = path
   319	                    .file_name()
   320	                    .map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().contains("constitution_head_t_c2"))
   321	                    .unwrap_or(false);
   322	                if is_test_file {
   323	                    continue;
   324	                }
   325	                assert!(
   326	                    !body.contains(f),
   327	                    "CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 violation: {} contains forbidden pointer name `{f}`",
   328	                    path.display()
   329	                );
   330	            }
   331	        }
   332	    }
   333	}
   334	
   335	/// SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.3 integration — `CasStore::put` advances `refs/chaintape/cas`
   336	/// automatically. Closes Stage A3 R3 (CAS hook) at integration level: each
   337	/// call to CasStore::put MUST result in the ref pointing at the new blob OID.
   338	#[test]
   339	fn sg_a3_cas_root_ref_advances_via_cas_store_put() {
   340	    use turingosv4::bottom_white::cas::schema::ObjectType;
   341	    use turingosv4::bottom_white::cas::store::CasStore;
   342	
   343	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().expect("tempdir");
   344	    let path = tmp.path().to_path_buf();
   345	    let mut store = CasStore::open(&path).expect("open cas store");
   346	
   347	    // Pre: no chaintape/cas ref.
   348	    let pre = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_cas(&path).expect("read pre");
   349	    assert!(pre.is_none(), "fresh CAS store must have no chaintape/cas ref");
   350	
   351	    // First put — ref must materialize.
   352	    let cid_a = store
   353	        .put(b"first cas content", ObjectType::Generic, "test", 1, None)
   354	        .expect("put 1");
   355	    let oid_a = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_cas(&path)
   356	        .expect("read cas after put 1")
   357	        .expect("cas ref present after put");
   358	    let _ = cid_a;
   359	
   360	    // Second put — ref must advance to new OID.
   361	    let cid_b = store
   362	        .put(b"second cas content", ObjectType::Generic, "test", 2, None)
   363	        .expect("put 2");
   364	    let oid_b = Git2LedgerWriter::head_chaintape_cas(&path)
   365	        .expect("read cas after put 2")
   366	        .expect("cas ref present after second put");
   367	    let _ = cid_b;
   368	
   369	    assert_ne!(
   370	        oid_a, oid_b,
   371	        "CasStore::put must advance refs/chaintape/cas on each new content"
   372	    );
   373	}
   374	
   375	/// Regression — REF constants pinned at canonical names per FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1.
   376	/// A future rename would silently break replay-from-refs; this fires the gate.
   377	#[test]
   378	fn sg_a3_ref_name_constants_pinned() {
   379	    assert_eq!(CHAINTAPE_L4_REF, "refs/chaintape/l4");
   380	    assert_eq!(CHAINTAPE_L4E_REF, "refs/chaintape/l4e");
   381	    assert_eq!(CHAINTAPE_CAS_REF, "refs/chaintape/cas");
   382	}

   623	#[derive(Debug)]
   624	pub enum CanonicalCodecError {
   625	    Encode(String),
   626	    Decode(String),
   627	    TrailingBytes { consumed: usize, total: usize },
   628	}
   629	
   630	impl std::fmt::Display for CanonicalCodecError {
   631	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   632	        match self {
   633	            Self::Encode(s) => write!(f, "canonical encode failed: {s}"),
   634	            Self::Decode(s) => write!(f, "canonical decode failed: {s}"),
   635	            Self::TrailingBytes { consumed, total } => {
   636	                write!(f, "trailing bytes after decode: consumed {consumed} of {total}")
   637	            }
   638	        }
   639	    }
   640	}
   641	impl std::error::Error for CanonicalCodecError {}
   642	
   643	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   644	// § 5 Git2LedgerWriter — git2-rs commit chain on `refs/transitions/main`
   645	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   646	
   647	/// Spec § 5 production storage backend.
   648	///
   649	/// **Mapping**:
   650	/// - One `LedgerEntry` = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`.
   651	/// - **Commit tree** = three named blobs:
   652	///     - `payload_cid`     = entry.tx_payload_cid.0 (32 bytes)
   653	///     - `signature`       = entry.system_signature.as_bytes() (64 bytes)
   654	///     - `entry_canonical` = bincode v2 BE + fixed-int encoding of the full
   655	///       `LedgerEntry` (deterministic, byte-stable; this blob IS the
   656	///       canonical record — `read_at` decodes it directly).
   657	/// - **Commit message** = human-readable `"transition logical_t=<N>\n"` (the
   658	///   canonical record lives in the tree blob, not the message — git
   659	///   normalizes message bytes in ways that break round-trip).
   660	/// - **Parent**: `head_t-1` commit (or none at genesis).
   661	/// - **Author/committer identity**: fixed `("turingosv4 sequencer", "system@turingos")`
   662	///   with `time = (logical_t as i64, 0)` to keep commit OIDs deterministic. NO
   663	///   wall-clock leakage (`I-NOENV` + `I-LOGTIME`).
   664	///
   665	/// **K3 (revised v1.2)**: this writer surfaces `commit_oid` for callers that
   666	/// need it (CO1.7.5+ `head_t` wiring), but the `LedgerWriter::commit` trait
   667	/// returns only `Hash` (entry.resulting_ledger_root). Callers requesting the
   668	/// commit OID use [`Git2LedgerWriter::head_commit_oid`] post-commit.
   669	pub struct Git2LedgerWriter {
   670	    repo_path: PathBuf,
   671	    /// Last commit OID on `refs/transitions/main`; `None` at empty-chain genesis.
   672	    head_oid: Option<git2::Oid>,
   673	    /// Number of entries committed = highest assigned `logical_t` (0 at genesis).
   674	    len: u64,
   675	}
   676	
   677	const TRANSITIONS_REF: &str = "refs/transitions/main";
   678	const TREE_BLOB_PAYLOAD_CID: &str = "payload_cid";
   679	const TREE_BLOB_SIGNATURE: &str = "signature";
   680	const TREE_BLOB_ENTRY_CANONICAL: &str = "entry_canonical";
   681	
   682	// ── Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 multi-ref ChainTape (additive) ─────────────────────
   683	//
   684	// Per `STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md` FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1: three
   685	// named refs constitute the canonical ChainTape pointer.
   686	//
   687	// The C1 baseline's `refs/transitions/main` is preserved as a backward-compat
   688	// alias; every accepted-transition commit dual-writes to `refs/chaintape/l4`
   689	// so a fresh checkout can reconstruct HEAD_t via either ref family. The L4.E
   690	// and CAS sides (currently stored as filesystem JSONL + CAS index sidecar)
   691	// gain Git-ref witnesses via `advance_chaintape_l4e_to` /
   692	// `advance_chaintape_cas_to`, which downstream rejection-evidence and CAS-
   693	// write code paths invoke after a successful append. The refs ARE the canonical
   694	// pointer per CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 (no hidden filesystem global pointer).
   695	
   696	/// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1): canonical L4 head ref. Dual-written alongside `refs/transitions/main` for migration safety. Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md §2.
   697	pub const CHAINTAPE_L4_REF: &str = "refs/chaintape/l4";
   698	/// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1): canonical L4.E head ref. Advanced by rejection-evidence-side after each L4.E append. Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md §2.
   699	pub const CHAINTAPE_L4E_REF: &str = "refs/chaintape/l4e";
   700	/// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1): canonical CAS root ref. Advanced by CAS-write-side after each batch. Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md §2.
   701	pub const CHAINTAPE_CAS_REF: &str = "refs/chaintape/cas";
   702	
   703	impl Git2LedgerWriter {
   704	    /// Open or initialize a `Git2LedgerWriter` rooted at `repo_path`.
   705	    /// Creates the underlying git repo if it doesn't exist; resolves the
   706	    /// existing `refs/transitions/main` if present and seeds `head_oid` + `len`.
   707	    pub fn open(repo_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, LedgerWriterError> {
   708	        let repo_path = repo_path.to_path_buf();
   709	        let repo = match Repository::open(&repo_path) {
   710	            Ok(r) => r,
   711	            Err(_) => Repository::init(&repo_path).map_err(|e| {
   712	                LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo init: {e}"))
   713	            })?,
   714	        };
   715	
   716	        // Resolve refs/transitions/main if it exists.
   717	        let (head_oid, len) = match repo.find_reference(TRANSITIONS_REF) {
   718	            Ok(reference) => {
   719	                let oid = reference
   720	                    .target()
   721	                    .ok_or_else(|| {
   722	                        LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!(
   723	                            "{TRANSITIONS_REF} has no direct target"
   724	                        ))
   725	                    })?;
   726	                // Walk parents to count chain length.
   727	                let mut n: u64 = 0;
   728	                let mut cursor = Some(oid);
   729	                while let Some(c) = cursor {
   730	                    n += 1;
   731	                    let commit = repo.find_commit(c).map_err(|e| {
   732	                        LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("walk parent: {e}"))
   733	                    })?;
   734	                    cursor = commit.parent(0).ok().map(|p| p.id());
   735	                }
   736	                (Some(oid), n)
   737	            }
   738	            Err(_) => (None, 0),
   739	        };
   740	
   741	        Ok(Self {
   742	            repo_path,
   743	            head_oid,
   744	            len,
   745	        })
   746	    }
   747	
   748	    fn open_repo(&self) -> Result<Repository, LedgerWriterError> {
   749	        Repository::open(&self.repo_path)
   750	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo open: {e}")))
   751	    }
   752	
   753	    /// Commit OID of the most recent appended entry (None if chain is empty).
   754	    /// CO1.7.5+ `head_t` wiring uses this to surface commit_sha alongside Hash.
   755	    pub fn head_commit_oid(&self) -> Option<git2::Oid> {
   756	        self.head_oid
   757	    }
   758	
   759	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1): read the canonical L4 head ref `refs/chaintape/l4`. Equal to `head_commit_oid()` after dual-write completes; a fresh checkout reads this ref directly. Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md SG-A3.1 + FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 replay-from-refs.
   760	    pub fn head_chaintape_l4(repo_path: &Path) -> Result<Option<git2::Oid>, LedgerWriterError> {
   761	        let repo = Repository::open(repo_path)
   762	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo open: {e}")))?;
   763	        let oid = repo
   764	            .find_reference(CHAINTAPE_L4_REF)
   765	            .ok()
   766	            .and_then(|r| r.target());
   767	        Ok(oid)
   768	    }
   769	
   770	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2): advance `refs/chaintape/l4e` to a caller-supplied OID. Called by rejection-evidence-side after each L4.E append (rejection_evidence.rs::append_rejected). The caller is responsible for constructing a deterministic commit OID; this function only updates the ref atomically. Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md SG-A3.2.
   771	    pub fn advance_chaintape_l4e_to(
   772	        repo_path: &Path,
   773	        oid: git2::Oid,
   774	        log_message: &str,
   775	    ) -> Result<(), LedgerWriterError> {
   776	        let repo = Repository::open(repo_path)
   777	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo open: {e}")))?;
   778	        repo.reference(CHAINTAPE_L4E_REF, oid, true, log_message)
   779	            .map_err(|e| {
   780	                LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!(
   781	                    "chaintape l4e ref update: {e}"
   782	                ))
   783	            })?;
   784	        Ok(())
   785	    }
   786	
   787	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.3): advance `refs/chaintape/cas` to a caller-supplied OID. Called by CAS-write-side after each batch (cas/store.rs). Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md SG-A3.3.
   788	    pub fn advance_chaintape_cas_to(
   789	        repo_path: &Path,
   790	        oid: git2::Oid,
   791	        log_message: &str,
   792	    ) -> Result<(), LedgerWriterError> {
   793	        let repo = Repository::open(repo_path)
   794	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo open: {e}")))?;
   795	        repo.reference(CHAINTAPE_CAS_REF, oid, true, log_message)
   796	            .map_err(|e| {
   797	                LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!(
   798	                    "chaintape cas ref update: {e}"
   799	                ))
   800	            })?;
   801	        Ok(())
   802	    }
   803	
   804	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4): read `refs/chaintape/l4e` head OID; `None` if ref not yet created. Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 replay-from-refs.
   805	    pub fn head_chaintape_l4e(repo_path: &Path) -> Result<Option<git2::Oid>, LedgerWriterError> {
   806	        let repo = Repository::open(repo_path)
   807	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo open: {e}")))?;
   808	        let oid = repo
   809	            .find_reference(CHAINTAPE_L4E_REF)
   810	            .ok()
   811	            .and_then(|r| r.target());
   812	        Ok(oid)
   813	    }
   814	
   815	    /// TRACE_MATRIX § 3 orphan (Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4): read `refs/chaintape/cas` head OID; `None` if ref not yet created. Constitutional Justification: STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 replay-from-refs.
   816	    pub fn head_chaintape_cas(repo_path: &Path) -> Result<Option<git2::Oid>, LedgerWriterError> {
   817	        let repo = Repository::open(repo_path)
   818	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("repo open: {e}")))?;
   819	        let oid = repo
   820	            .find_reference(CHAINTAPE_CAS_REF)
   821	            .ok()
   822	            .and_then(|r| r.target());
   823	        Ok(oid)
   824	    }
   825	
   826	    /// Read raw canonical-encoded `LedgerEntry` bytes (the `entry_canonical`
   827	    /// tree blob) for the entry at `logical_t`. `logical_t` is 1-indexed.
   828	    fn read_canonical_bytes(&self, logical_t: u64) -> Result<Vec<u8>, LedgerWriterError> {
   829	        if logical_t == 0 || logical_t > self.len {
   830	            return Err(LedgerWriterError::NotFound { logical_t });
   831	        }
   832	        let repo = self.open_repo()?;
   833	        // Walk back (len - logical_t) parents from head.
   834	        let mut cursor = self.head_oid.ok_or(LedgerWriterError::NotFound { logical_t })?;
   835	        let mut steps_back = self.len - logical_t;
   836	        while steps_back > 0 {
   837	            let commit = repo.find_commit(cursor).map_err(|e| {
   838	                LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("find_commit: {e}"))
   839	            })?;
   840	            cursor = commit
   841	                .parent(0)
   842	                .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("parent: {e}")))?
   843	                .id();
   844	            steps_back -= 1;
   845	        }
   846	        let commit = repo
   847	            .find_commit(cursor)
   848	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("find_commit: {e}")))?;
   849	        let tree = commit
   850	            .tree()
   851	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("tree: {e}")))?;
   852	        let entry_obj = tree
   853	            .get_name(TREE_BLOB_ENTRY_CANONICAL)
   854	            .ok_or_else(|| {
   855	                LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!(
   856	                    "missing {TREE_BLOB_ENTRY_CANONICAL} blob at logical_t={logical_t}"
   857	                ))
   858	            })?;
   859	        let blob = repo
   860	            .find_blob(entry_obj.id())
   861	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("find_blob: {e}")))?;
   862	        Ok(blob.content().to_vec())
   863	    }
   864	}
   865	
   866	impl LedgerWriter for Git2LedgerWriter {
   867	    /// CO1.7-extra D2: surface 40-char lowercase hex commit OID for sequencer
   868	    /// post-commit head_t wiring. Maps existing `head_commit_oid()` accessor
   869	    /// (returns Option<git2::Oid>) to canonical hex string.
   870	    fn head_commit_oid_hex(&self) -> Option<String> {
   871	        self.head_commit_oid().map(|oid| oid.to_string())
   872	    }
   873	
   874	    fn commit(&mut self, entry: &LedgerEntry) -> Result<Hash, LedgerWriterError> {
   875	        let expected = self.len + 1;
   876	        if entry.logical_t != expected {
   877	            return Err(LedgerWriterError::LogicalTGap {
   878	                expected,
   879	                got: entry.logical_t,
   880	            });
   881	        }
   882	
   883	        let repo = self.open_repo()?;
   884	        let canonical = canonical_encode(entry).map_err(|e| {
   885	            LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("canonical_encode: {e}"))
   886	        })?;
   887	
   888	        let mut tb = repo
   889	            .treebuilder(None)
   890	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("treebuilder: {e}")))?;
   891	        let cid_blob = repo
   892	            .blob(&entry.tx_payload_cid.0)
   893	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("cid blob: {e}")))?;
   894	        tb.insert(TREE_BLOB_PAYLOAD_CID, cid_blob, 0o100644)
   895	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("tree insert cid: {e}")))?;
   896	        let sig_blob = repo
   897	            .blob(entry.system_signature.as_bytes())
   898	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("sig blob: {e}")))?;
   899	        tb.insert(TREE_BLOB_SIGNATURE, sig_blob, 0o100644)
   900	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("tree insert sig: {e}")))?;
   901	        let entry_blob = repo
   902	            .blob(&canonical)
   903	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("entry blob: {e}")))?;
   904	        tb.insert(TREE_BLOB_ENTRY_CANONICAL, entry_blob, 0o100644)
   905	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("tree insert entry: {e}")))?;
   906	        let tree_oid = tb
   907	            .write()
   908	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("tree write: {e}")))?;
   909	        let tree = repo
   910	            .find_tree(tree_oid)
   911	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("find_tree: {e}")))?;
   912	
   913	        // Determinism: time = (logical_t, 0). NO wall clock.
   914	        let time = git2::Time::new(entry.logical_t as i64, 0);
   915	        let author = GitSignature::new("turingosv4 sequencer", "system@turingos", &time)
   916	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("git sig: {e}")))?;
   917	        let committer = author.clone();
   918	
   919	        let parents: Vec<git2::Commit<'_>> = match self.head_oid {
   920	            Some(oid) => vec![repo.find_commit(oid).map_err(|e| {
   921	                LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("parent commit: {e}"))
   922	            })?],
   923	            None => Vec::new(),
   924	        };
   925	        let parent_refs: Vec<&git2::Commit<'_>> = parents.iter().collect();
   926	        let message = format!("transition logical_t={}\n", entry.logical_t);
   927	        let new_oid = repo
   928	            .commit(
   929	                Some(TRANSITIONS_REF),
   930	                &author,
   931	                &committer,
   932	                &message,
   933	                &tree,
   934	                &parent_refs,
   935	            )
   936	            .map_err(|e| LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("commit: {e}")))?;
   937	
   938	        // Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 — dual-write to
   939	        // `refs/chaintape/l4` so the new canonical ref family advances
   940	        // alongside the C1 alias `refs/transitions/main`. Per CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.6
   941	        // the HEAD_t schema is unchanged; only the storage form gains the
   942	        // multi-ref witness.
   943	        repo.reference(
   944	            CHAINTAPE_L4_REF,
   945	            new_oid,
   946	            true,
   947	            &format!("transition logical_t={}", entry.logical_t),
   948	        )
   949	        .map_err(|e| {
   950	            LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!(
   951	                "chaintape l4 ref update: {e}"
   952	            ))
   953	        })?;
   954	
   955	        self.head_oid = Some(new_oid);
   956	        self.len += 1;
   957	        Ok(entry.resulting_ledger_root)
   958	    }
   959	
   960	    fn read_at(&self, logical_t: u64) -> Result<LedgerEntry, LedgerWriterError> {
   961	        let bytes = self.read_canonical_bytes(logical_t)?;
   962	        canonical_decode::<LedgerEntry>(&bytes).map_err(|e| {
   963	            LedgerWriterError::BackendCorruption(format!("canonical_decode at {logical_t}: {e}"))
   964	        })
   965	    }
   966	
   967	    fn len(&self) -> u64 {
   968	        self.len
   969	    }
   970	}
   971

   386	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   387	// RejectionEvidenceError — chain-walk failure taxonomy
   388	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   389	
   390	/// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — error returned by `RejectionEvidenceWriter::verify_chain` + JSONL persistence ops (TB-6 Atom 1.2).
   391	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
   392	pub enum RejectionEvidenceError {
   393	    /// `prev_hash` chain or per-record hash diverged at the given index
   394	    /// (covers row deletion, field tampering, and reordering).
   395	    HashMismatch { at: usize },
   396	    /// TB-6 Atom 1.2 — JSONL persistence I/O failure (open / read / write / fsync).
   397	    /// `String`-wrapped to keep `Clone + PartialEq + Eq` derives.
   398	    Io(String),
   399	    /// TB-6 Atom 1.2 — JSONL deserialization failed during open_jsonl replay
   400	    /// (line is corrupted / not valid JSON / shape mismatch).
   401	    JsonlParse { line: usize, reason: String },
   402	}
   403	
   404	impl std::fmt::Display for RejectionEvidenceError {
   405	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   406	        match self {
   407	            Self::HashMismatch { at } => write!(f, "rejection-evidence chain break at index {}", at),
   408	            Self::Io(e) => write!(f, "rejection-evidence I/O error: {e}"),
   409	            Self::JsonlParse { line, reason } => write!(
   410	                f,
   411	                "rejection-evidence JSONL parse failure at line {line}: {reason}"
   412	            ),
   413	        }
   414	    }
   415	}
   416	
   417	impl std::error::Error for RejectionEvidenceError {}
   418	
   419	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   420	// RejectionEvidenceWriter — append + verify + project-to-public
   421	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   422	
   423	/// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6/P1:9 — rejection-evidence writer with optional persistent backend.
   424	///
   425	/// One `Vec<RejectedSubmissionRecord>`; `prev_hash` chained; `submit_id`
   426	/// monotonicity is the caller's responsibility (the writer trusts the
   427	/// `Sequencer::next_submit_id` issuer). No `logical_t` field — accepted
   428	/// spine and rejection-evidence are intentionally disjoint per the L4 / L4.E
   429	/// split (`DECISION_REJECTION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-04-29.md`).
   430	///
   431	/// **TB-6 Atom 1.2 extension**: optional `Backend::JsonlAppend(path)` causes
   432	/// every `append_rejected` call to also serialize the new record as a single
   433	/// JSONL line + append + fsync to `path`. `open_jsonl(path)` constructs the
   434	/// writer from an existing JSONL file, replaying records into the in-memory
   435	/// chain. The "或等价结构" form of architect § 3.5 `refs/rejections/main` —
   436	/// JSONL with embedded `prev_hash` + `hash` chain.
   437	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   438	pub struct RejectionEvidenceWriter {
   439	    records: Vec<RejectedSubmissionRecord>,
   440	    /// TB-6 Atom 1.2 — backend for persistence; default = `InMemory` (no I/O).
   441	    backend: Backend,
   442	}
   443	
   444	/// TB-6 Atom 1.2 — internal backend for `RejectionEvidenceWriter`.
   445	/// NOT pub — backend selection is via `RejectionEvidenceWriter::open_jsonl`.
   446	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   447	enum Backend {
   448	    /// Pure in-memory `Vec<RejectedSubmissionRecord>`; existing TB-1 behavior.
   449	    #[default]
   450	    InMemory,
   451	    /// TB-6 Atom 1.2 — JSONL append-only persistent file. `append_rejected`
   452	    /// flushes a single JSONL line per record + fsync. `open_jsonl` reads
   453	    /// existing file on construction + replays into `records`.
   454	    JsonlAppend { path: std::path::PathBuf },
   455	}
   456	
   457	impl RejectionEvidenceWriter {
   458	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — empty writer with `InMemory` backend.
   459	    pub fn new() -> Self {
   460	        Self::default()
   461	    }
   462	
   463	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1 + P1:6 (TB-6 Atom 1.2) — open or create a JSONL-backed writer.
   464	    ///
   465	    /// On open:
   466	    /// - If `path` exists, read each line as a `RejectedSubmissionRecord`,
   467	    ///   append to `records`, and `verify_chain()` over the loaded records to
   468	    ///   reject tampering at load time.
   469	    /// - If `path` does not exist, create the file (and parent dirs) empty.
   470	    /// - Either way, set backend to `JsonlAppend(path)` so subsequent
   471	    ///   `append_rejected` calls flush to that path.
   472	    ///
   473	    /// Architect § 3.5 "或等价结构": JSONL chain-hash equivalent to git
   474	    /// `refs/rejections/main`. Each line embeds `prev_hash` + `hash` so
   475	    /// tampering with any line breaks the chain at that line.
   476	    pub fn open_jsonl(
   477	        path: std::path::PathBuf,
   478	    ) -> Result<Self, RejectionEvidenceError> {
   479	        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
   480	            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| {
   481	                RejectionEvidenceError::Io(format!("create parent dir for {path:?}: {e}"))
   482	            })?;
   483	        }
   484	        let mut records: Vec<RejectedSubmissionRecord> = Vec::new();
   485	        if path.exists() {
   486	            let contents = std::fs::read_to_string(&path).map_err(|e| {
   487	                RejectionEvidenceError::Io(format!("read {path:?}: {e}"))
   488	            })?;
   489	            for (idx, line) in contents.lines().enumerate() {
   490	                if line.trim().is_empty() {
   491	                    continue;
   492	                }
   493	                let shadow: JsonlRecord =
   494	                    serde_json::from_str(line).map_err(|e| {
   495	                        RejectionEvidenceError::JsonlParse {
   496	                            line: idx,
   497	                            reason: e.to_string(),
   498	                        }
   499	                    })?;
   500	                records.push(shadow.into());
   501	            }
   502	        } else {
   503	            // Create empty file with parent dirs already ensured above.
   504	            std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
   505	                .create(true)
   506	                .append(true)
   507	                .open(&path)
   508	                .map_err(|e| {
   509	                    RejectionEvidenceError::Io(format!("create {path:?}: {e}"))
   510	                })?;
   511	        }
   512	        let writer = Self {
   513	            records,
   514	            backend: Backend::JsonlAppend { path },
   515	        };
   516	        // Validate chain integrity on load — tampering with any record
   517	        // breaks here.
   518	        writer.verify_chain()?;
   519	        Ok(writer)
   520	    }
   521	
   522	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1 (TB-6 Atom 1.2) — convenience: is this writer JSONL-backed?
   523	    pub fn is_jsonl_backed(&self) -> bool {
   524	        matches!(self.backend, Backend::JsonlAppend { .. })
   525	    }
   526	
   527	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — count of recorded rejections.
   528	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
   529	        self.records.len()
   530	    }
   531	
   532	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — empty predicate.
   533	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
   534	        self.records.is_empty()
   535	    }
   536	
   537	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — last record's hash, or `Hash::ZERO` for empty chain.
   538	    pub fn last_hash(&self) -> Hash {
   539	        self.records.last().map(|r| r.hash).unwrap_or(Hash::ZERO)
   540	    }
   541	
   542	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6/P1:9 — append a rejection record; returns the new chain hash.
   543	    ///
   544	    /// CRITICAL: this method MUST NOT be called from the L4 (accepted) write
   545	    /// path — Inv 7 forbids state-root advance on rejection. The caller's
   546	    /// dispatch logic decides which ledger receives the record.
   547	    ///
   548	    /// **TB-6 Atom 1.2**: when the writer is `JsonlAppend`-backed, the new
   549	    /// record is also serialized as one JSONL line and appended (with fsync)
   550	    /// to the configured path. JSONL flush failures are silently logged and
   551	    /// the in-memory record is still pushed — `Sequencer::apply_one` (the
   552	    /// caller) does not have an error return path for this writer; tests
   553	    /// validate persistence via `RejectionEvidenceWriter::open_jsonl`'s
   554	    /// reload + `verify_chain` round-trip rather than checking append return.
   555	    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
   556	    pub fn append_rejected(
   557	        &mut self,
   558	        submit_id: u64,
   559	        parent_state_root: Hash,
   560	        agent_id: AgentId,
   561	        tx_kind: TxKind,
   562	        tx_payload_cid: Cid,
   563	        rejection_class: RejectionClass,
   564	        raw_diagnostic_cid: Option<Cid>,
   565	        public_summary: Option<String>,
   566	    ) -> Hash {
   567	        let prev_hash = self.last_hash();
   568	        let hash = RejectedSubmissionRecord::compute_hash(
   569	            submit_id,
   570	            &parent_state_root,
   571	            &agent_id,
   572	            tx_kind,
   573	            &tx_payload_cid,
   574	            rejection_class,
   575	            &raw_diagnostic_cid,
   576	            &public_summary,
   577	            &prev_hash,
   578	        );
   579	        let record = RejectedSubmissionRecord {
   580	            submit_id,
   581	            parent_state_root,
   582	            agent_id,
   583	            tx_kind,
   584	            tx_payload_cid,
   585	            rejection_class,
   586	            raw_diagnostic_cid,
   587	            public_summary,
   588	            prev_hash,
   589	            hash,
   590	        };
   591	        // TB-6 Atom 1.2: flush to JSONL backend if configured. We do this
   592	        // BEFORE pushing to records so a write failure does not leave the
   593	        // in-memory chain ahead of the on-disk chain (best-effort
   594	        // consistency).
   595	        if let Backend::JsonlAppend { path } = &self.backend {
   596	            if let Err(e) = flush_jsonl_record(path, &record) {
   597	                log::error!(
   598	                    "rejection-evidence JSONL flush failed (record dropped from in-memory chain to preserve persistence consistency): {e}"
   599	                );
   600	                // Caller's chain-hash invariant is preserved because we did
   601	                // NOT push the record. The next append_rejected will compute
   602	                // prev_hash from the unchanged last_hash. The dropped record
   603	                // is logged but lost — this is the failure mode for an I/O
   604	                // error mid-run; recovery is to investigate the JSONL path.
   605	                return prev_hash;
   606	            }
   607	        }
   608	        self.records.push(record);
   609	        hash
   610	    }
   611	
   612	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — verify the rejection-evidence chain end-to-end.
   613	    ///
   614	    /// Returns `Err(HashMismatch)` if any single field of any record was
   615	    /// tampered, or if a row was deleted (the surviving row's `prev_hash`
   616	    /// no longer matches its predecessor's `hash`).
   617	    pub fn verify_chain(&self) -> Result<(), RejectionEvidenceError> {
   618	        let mut prev = Hash::ZERO;
   619	        for (i, r) in self.records.iter().enumerate() {
   620	            if r.prev_hash != prev {
   621	                return Err(RejectionEvidenceError::HashMismatch { at: i });
   622	            }
   623	            let recomputed = RejectedSubmissionRecord::compute_hash(
   624	                r.submit_id,
   625	                &r.parent_state_root,
   626	                &r.agent_id,
   627	                r.tx_kind,
   628	                &r.tx_payload_cid,
   629	                r.rejection_class,
   630	                &r.raw_diagnostic_cid,
   631	                &r.public_summary,
   632	                &r.prev_hash,
   633	            );
   634	            if recomputed != r.hash {
   635	                return Err(RejectionEvidenceError::HashMismatch { at: i });
   636	            }
   637	            prev = r.hash;
   638	        }
   639	        Ok(())
   640	    }
   641	
   642	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:9 — read-only record slice (for L4.E forensics; full
   643	    /// records carry `raw_diagnostic_cid` and MUST NOT be exposed across the
   644	    /// agent boundary; use `public_view` for that).
   645	    pub fn records(&self) -> &[RejectedSubmissionRecord] {
   646	        &self.records
   647	    }
   648	
   649	    /// TRACE_MATRIX Inv 10 + P1:9 — agent-facing projection.
   650	    ///
   651	    /// `PublicRejectionView` does not carry `raw_diagnostic_cid` by type
   652	    /// construction; this method's output is safe to materialize into another
   653	    /// agent's read view.
   654	    pub fn public_view(&self) -> Vec<PublicRejectionView> {
   655	        self.records.iter().map(PublicRejectionView::from).collect()
   656	    }
   657	
   658	    /// TRACE_MATRIX P1:6 — TAMPER-ONLY hook used by kill-criteria integration
   659	    /// tests (`test_p1_kill_4b_rejection_chain_breaks_on_row_deletion`).
   660	    /// `#[doc(hidden)]` + `tamper_` prefix flags any production use as a
   661	    /// reviewable violation; kept `pub` only so integration tests in `tests/`
   662	    /// can reach it (they link against the lib without `cfg(test)` enabled).
   663	    #[doc(hidden)]
   664	    pub fn tamper_remove_record(&mut self, idx: usize) {

   686	    fn append_records_and_chains() {
   687	        let mut w = RejectionEvidenceWriter::new();

   689	            1,
   690	            Hash::ZERO,
   691	            agent("alice"),
   692	            TxKind::Work,
   693	            cid(0x10),
   694	            RejectionClass::PredicateFailed,
   695	            Some(cid(0xAA)),
   696	            Some("predicate acc1 returned false".into()),
   697	        );
   698	        let h2 = w.append_rejected(
   699	            2,
   700	            Hash::ZERO,
   701	            agent("bob"),
   702	            TxKind::Verify,
   703	            cid(0x11),
   704	            RejectionClass::PolicyViolation,
   705	            None,
   706	            None,
   707	        );
   708	        assert_eq!(w.len(), 2);
   709	        assert_ne!(h1, Hash::ZERO);
   710	        assert_ne!(h2, Hash::ZERO);
   711	        assert_eq!(w.records()[1].prev_hash, h1);
   712	        assert_eq!(w.last_hash(), h2);
   713	        assert!(w.verify_chain().is_ok());
   714	    }

   719	        w.append_rejected(
   720	            1,
