   412	
   413	    /// Get metadata only (no content fetch).
   414	    pub fn metadata(&self, cid: &Cid) -> Option<&CasObjectMetadata> {
   415	        self.index.get(cid)
   416	    }
   417	
   418	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
   419	        self.index.len()
   420	    }
   421	
   422	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
   423	        self.index.is_empty()
   424	    }
   425	
   426	    /// Merkle root over all CAS object metadata; deterministic per BTreeMap order.
   427	    pub fn merkle_root(&self) -> [u8; 32] {
   428	        let mut h = Sha256::new();
   429	        for (_cid, meta) in &self.index {
   430	            h.update(meta.canonical_hash());
   431	        }
   432	        h.finalize().into()
   433	    }
   434	}

   436	#[cfg(test)]
   437	mod tests {
   438	    use super::*;
   439	    use tempfile::TempDir;
   440	
   441	    fn fresh_store() -> (TempDir, CasStore) {
   442	        let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
   443	        let store = CasStore::open(tmp.path()).unwrap();
   444	        (tmp, store)
   445	    }
   446	
   447	    #[test]
   448	    fn put_get_round_trip_small() {
   449	        let (_tmp, mut s) = fresh_store();
   450	        let cid = s.put(b"hello world", ObjectType::ProposalPayload, "alice", 100, None).unwrap();
   451	        let content = s.get(&cid).unwrap();
   452	        assert_eq!(content, b"hello world");
   453	    }
   454	
   455	    #[test]
   456	    fn put_get_round_trip_large() {
   457	        let (_tmp, mut s) = fresh_store();
   458	        let big = vec![0xab; 65536];
   459	        let cid = s.put(&big, ObjectType::PredicateBytecode, "system", 0, Some("wasm".into())).unwrap();
   460	        let content = s.get(&cid).unwrap();
   461	        assert_eq!(content, big);
   462	    }
   463	
   464	    #[test]
   465	    fn put_idempotent_same_content() {
   466	        let (_tmp, mut s) = fresh_store();
   467	        let cid_a = s.put(b"x", ObjectType::Generic, "alice", 1, None).unwrap();
   468	        let cid_b = s.put(b"x", ObjectType::Generic, "bob", 2, None).unwrap();
   469	        assert_eq!(cid_a, cid_b, "same content → same Cid");
   470	        // Index size = 1 (idempotent)
   471	        assert_eq!(s.len(), 1);
   472	    }
   473	
   474	    #[test]
   475	    fn cid_is_content_address() {
   476	        let (_tmp, mut s) = fresh_store();
   477	        let cid = s.put(b"specific content", ObjectType::Generic, "system", 0, None).unwrap();
   478	        // Cid is sha256 of content; verifiable independently
   479	        let expected = Cid::from_content(b"specific content");
   480	        assert_eq!(cid, expected);
   481	    }
   482	
   483	    #[test]
   484	    fn get_nonexistent_returns_error() {
   485	        let (_tmp, s) = fresh_store();
   486	        let bogus = Cid([0u8; 32]);
   487	        match s.get(&bogus) {
   488	            Err(CasError::CidNotFound(c)) => assert_eq!(c, bogus),
   489	            other => panic!("expected CidNotFound, got {other:?}"),
   490	        }
   491	    }
   492	
   493	    #[test]
   494	    fn metadata_recorded() {
   495	        let (_tmp, mut s) = fresh_store();
   496	        let cid = s.put(b"meta test", ObjectType::CounterexamplePayload, "carol", 250, Some("v1".into())).unwrap();
   497	        let meta = s.metadata(&cid).unwrap();
   498	        assert_eq!(meta.cid, cid);
   499	        assert_eq!(meta.object_type, ObjectType::CounterexamplePayload);
   500	        assert_eq!(meta.creator, "carol");
   501	        assert_eq!(meta.created_at_logical_t, 250);
   502	        assert_eq!(meta.schema_id.as_deref(), Some("v1"));
   503	        assert_eq!(meta.size_bytes, 9);
   504	    }
   505	
   506	    #[test]
   507	    fn merkle_root_deterministic_two_runs() {
   508	        let (_tmp1, mut s1) = fresh_store();
   509	        let (_tmp2, mut s2) = fresh_store();
   510	        for content in [b"a".as_slice(), b"b".as_slice(), b"c".as_slice()] {
   511	            s1.put(content, ObjectType::Generic, "system", 0, None).unwrap();
   512	        }
   513	        // Different insertion order
   514	        for content in [b"c".as_slice(), b"b".as_slice(), b"a".as_slice()] {
   515	            s2.put(content, ObjectType::Generic, "system", 0, None).unwrap();
   516	        }
   517	        assert_eq!(s1.merkle_root(), s2.merkle_root(),
   518	            "BTreeMap-ordered: insertion order independent (I-DET)");
   519	    }
   520	
   521	    #[test]
   522	    fn empty_store_root() {
   523	        let (_tmp, s) = fresh_store();
   524	        let r = s.merkle_root();
   525	        let expected: [u8; 32] = Sha256::new().finalize().into();
   526	        assert_eq!(r, expected, "empty store root = sha256(empty)");
   527	    }
   528	
   529	    #[test]
   530	    fn cell_isolation_disjoint_cas() {
   531	        // Per spec § 5.2.2 cross-cell isolation: separate runtime_repo paths
   532	        // → completely disjoint CasStore instances.
   533	        let (_tmp_a, mut store_a) = fresh_store();
   534	        let (_tmp_b, mut store_b) = fresh_store();
   535	
   536	        let cid_a = store_a.put(b"only in a", ObjectType::Generic, "agent_a", 100, None).unwrap();
   537	        let cid_b = store_b.put(b"only in b", ObjectType::Generic, "agent_b", 100, None).unwrap();
   538	
   539	        // Each store has its own object only
   540	        assert!(store_a.get(&cid_a).is_ok(), "store_a has cid_a");
   541	        assert!(store_a.get(&cid_b).is_err(), "store_a lacks cid_b (isolated)");
   542	        assert!(store_b.get(&cid_b).is_ok(), "store_b has cid_b");
   543	        assert!(store_b.get(&cid_a).is_err(), "store_b lacks cid_a (isolated)");
   544	    }
   545	
   546	    #[test]
   547	    fn put_many_then_iterate_count() {
   548	        let (_tmp, mut s) = fresh_store();
   549	        for i in 0..50 {
   550	            s.put(
   551	                format!("content {i}").as_bytes(),
   552	                ObjectType::ProposalPayload,
   553	                "system",
   554	                i as u64,
   555	                None,
   556	            )
   557	            .unwrap();
   558	        }
   559	        assert_eq!(s.len(), 50);
   560	        assert!(!s.is_empty());

   101	
   102	// ── Configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   103	
   104	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: configuration shape for production ChainTape mode.
   105	///
   106	/// Production ChainTape runtime configuration.
   107	///
   108	/// Enabled by env var `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH=<runtime_repo_path>`.
   109	/// When unset, the evaluator falls back to legacy `TuringBus::new` /
   110	/// `TuringBus::with_wal_path` (no on-disk ChainTape).
   111	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   112	pub struct RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
   113	    /// Filesystem path to the on-disk runtime git repo.
   114	    /// `Git2LedgerWriter` rooted here writes `refs/transitions/main`.
   115	    /// `<runtime_repo_path>/rejections.jsonl` is the L4.E persistent file.
   116	    /// `<runtime_repo_path>/pinned_pubkeys.json` carries the per-run
   117	    /// `PinnedSystemPubkeys` so `verify_chaintape` (Atom 4) can re-verify
   118	    /// entry signatures without separate config.
   119	    pub runtime_repo_path: PathBuf,
   120	    /// CAS root directory. Distinct from `runtime_repo_path` so CAS payloads
   121	    /// can be inspected independently of the chain refs.
   122	    pub cas_path: PathBuf,
   123	    /// Run identity for evidence-dir naming + audit trail. Defaults to
   124	    /// `TURINGOS_RUN_ID` env var or current Unix-second timestamp.
   125	    pub run_id: String,
   126	    /// Sequencer mpsc channel capacity. Default 64.
   127	    pub queue_capacity: usize,
   128	}
   129	
   130	impl RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
   131	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: env-flag-gated chaintape mode entry — evaluator calls this once at boot.
   132	    ///
   133	    /// Build from env. Returns `None` if `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH` unset.
   134	    pub fn from_env() -> Option<Self> {
   135	        let runtime_repo_path: PathBuf =
   136	            std::env::var("TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH").ok()?.into();
   137	        let cas_path: PathBuf = match std::env::var("TURINGOS_CAS_PATH") {
   138	            Ok(p) => p.into(),
   139	            Err(_) => runtime_repo_path
   140	                .parent()
   141	                .unwrap_or_else(|| Path::new("."))
   142	                .join(format!(
   143	                    "cas_{}",
   144	                    runtime_repo_path
   145	                        .file_name()
   146	                        .map(|s| s.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
   147	                        .unwrap_or_else(|| "default".into()),
   148	                )),
   149	        };
   150	        let run_id = std::env::var("TURINGOS_RUN_ID").unwrap_or_else(|_| {
   151	            std::time::SystemTime::now()
   152	                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   153	                .map(|d| d.as_secs().to_string())
   154	                .unwrap_or_else(|_| "0".into())
   155	        });
   156	        let queue_capacity = std::env::var("TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_QUEUE_CAPACITY")
   157	            .ok()
   158	            .and_then(|s| s.parse::<usize>().ok())
   159	            .unwrap_or(64);
   160	        Some(Self {
   161	            runtime_repo_path,
   162	            cas_path,
   163	            run_id,
   164	            queue_capacity,
   165	        })
   166	    }
   167	}
   168	
   169	// ── Bundle returned by the factory ──────────────────────────────────────────
   170	
   171	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: handle bundle bridging evaluator → Sequencer + on-disk ChainTape.
   172	///
   173	/// Bundle of runtime handles produced by `build_chaintape_sequencer`.
   174	///
   175	/// The caller is responsible for wiring `sequencer` into a `TuringBus` via
   176	/// `TuringBus::with_sequencer(kernel, config, bundle.sequencer.clone())`,
   177	/// then driving the runtime to completion and calling `bundle.shutdown()`
   178	/// at exit to drain queued submissions.
   179	pub struct ChaintapeBundle {
   180	    /// Cloned and passed to `TuringBus::with_sequencer`.
   400	        return Err(BootstrapError::NonEmptyRuntimeRepo {
   401	            path: config.runtime_repo_path.clone(),
   402	            existing_head,
   403	        });
   404	    }
   405	    let transition_writer: Arc<RwLock<dyn LedgerWriter>> = Arc::new(RwLock::new(git_writer));
   406	
   407	    // Step 2: open CAS.
   408	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&config.cas_path)?;
   409	    let cas_store = CasStore::open(&config.cas_path)
   410	        .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Cas(e.to_string()))?;
   411	    let cas = Arc::new(RwLock::new(cas_store));
   412	
   413	    // Step 3: generate keypair + persist pinned-pubkey manifest.
   414	    let keypair = Arc::new(
   415	        Ed25519Keypair::generate_with_secure_entropy()
   416	            .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Keypair(e.to_string()))?,
   417	    );
   418	    let epoch = SystemEpoch::new(1);
   419	    write_pinned_pubkey_manifest(&config.runtime_repo_path, epoch, &keypair, &config.run_id)?;
   420	    let mut pinned = PinnedSystemPubkeys::new();
   421	    pinned.insert(epoch, keypair.public_key());
   422	    let pinned_pubkeys = Arc::new(pinned);
   423	
   424	    // Step 4: rejection writer — JSONL-backed at <runtime_repo>/rejections.jsonl
   425	    // per Atom 1.2 + architect § 3.5 deliverable shape.
   426	    //
   427	    // **TB-7 Atom 1.7 (Codex audit cc7b3dd action item #1)**: fail-closed
   428	    // when JSONL open fails. Silent in-memory fallback is the same
   429	    // anti-pattern as legacy `bus.append` as authoritative state mutation:
   430	    // a chain-backed run that secretly drops L4.E writes is worse than a
   431	    // failed boot. ChainTape mode is contractually a fail-closed declaration.
   432	    let rejections_path = config.runtime_repo_path.join("rejections.jsonl");
   433	    let rejection_writer = match RejectionEvidenceWriter::open_jsonl(rejections_path.clone()) {
   434	        Ok(w) => Arc::new(RwLock::new(w)),
   435	        Err(e) => {
   436	            return Err(BootstrapError::RejectionWriter(format!(
   437	                "open_jsonl({:?}) failed: {e}",
   438	                rejections_path
   439	            )));
   440	        }
   441	    };
   442	
   443	    // Step 5: predicate + tool registries (default empty registries — production-binary
   444	    // is responsible for registering predicates / tools before submitting txs).
   445	    let predicate_registry = Arc::new(PredicateRegistry::new());
   446	    let tool_registry = Arc::new(ToolRegistry::new());
   447	
   448	    // Step 6: initial QState (caller-provided; base factory passes QState::genesis()).
   449	    // TB-7.7 D7 fix: persist initial_q to <runtime_repo>/initial_q_state.json so
   450	    // verify_chaintape can replay from the same starting point. Without this,
   451	    // pre-seeded balances / open task markets seen at runtime are absent during
   452	    // replay, causing a state divergence that classifies as
   453	    // (state_reconstructed=false, economic_state_reconstructed=false). Per
   454	    // verify.rs:264-272 this file is the authoritative initial-Q manifest;
   455	    // omitting it forces replay to start from QState::genesis().
   456	    let initial_q_path = config.runtime_repo_path.join("initial_q_state.json");
   457	    let initial_q_json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&initial_q)
   458	        .map_err(|e| BootstrapError::Cas(format!("initial_q serialize: {e}")))?;
   459	    std::fs::write(&initial_q_path, initial_q_json)?;
   460	
   461	    // Step 7: construct Sequencer.
   462	    let (sequencer, queue_rx) = Sequencer::new(
   463	        cas,
   464	        keypair,
   465	        epoch,
   466	        transition_writer.clone(),
   467	        rejection_writer.clone(),
   468	        predicate_registry,
   469	        tool_registry,
   470	        pinned_pubkeys,
   471	        initial_q,
   472	        config.queue_capacity,
   473	    );
   474	    let sequencer = Arc::new(sequencer);
   475	
   476	    // Step 8: spawn driver wrapper + shutdown channel.
   477	    let (shutdown_tx, shutdown_rx) = oneshot::channel::<()>();
   478	    let driver_seq = sequencer.clone();
   479	    let driver_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
   480	        run_chaintape_driver(driver_seq, queue_rx, shutdown_rx).await;
   481	    });
   482	
   483	    Ok(ChaintapeBundle {
   484	        sequencer,
   485	        transition_writer,
   486	        rejection_writer,
   487	        epoch,
   488	        runtime_repo_path: config.runtime_repo_path.clone(),
   489	        cas_path: config.cas_path.clone(),
   490	        driver_handle,

  3045	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-S3 (TB-5 Atom 4 preflight § 4.5): factor the L4.E
  3046	    /// rejection-writer arm out of `apply_one` so it can be invoked from
  3047	    /// BOTH dispatch failures (stage 2) AND signature-verification failures
  3048	    /// (stage 1.5). Behavior preserved exactly per the existing TB-2 Atom 4
  3049	    /// rejection-writer semantics: no logical_t / state_root / ledger_root
  3050	    /// advance. Records:
  3051	    /// - tx_payload_cid (canonical-encoded TypedTx)
  3052	    /// - raw_diagnostic_cid (TransitionError display, structurally
  3053	    ///   serde-shielded on RejectedSubmissionRecord per TB-1 P0-3)
  3054	    /// - rejection_class via `rejection_class_for(err)`
  3055	    /// - public_summary via `public_summary_for(err)`
  3056	    /// - agent_id via `tx.submitter_id().unwrap_or(SYSTEM_AGENT_ID)`
  3057	    fn record_rejection(
  3058	        &self,
  3059	        submit_id: u64,
  3060	        tx: &TypedTx,
  3061	        q_snapshot: &QState,
  3062	        err: &TransitionError,
  3063	    ) -> Result<(), ApplyError> {
  3064	        let payload_bytes = canonical_encode(tx)
  3065	            .map_err(|e| ApplyError::PayloadEncode(e.to_string()))?;
  3066	        let creator = format!("sequencer.rejection_path.epoch-{}", self.epoch.get());
  3067	        let rejection_logical_t = self.next_logical_t.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
  3068	
  3069	        let tx_payload_cid = {
  3070	            let mut cas_w = self
  3071	                .cas
  3072	                .write()
  3073	                .map_err(|_| ApplyError::QStateLockPoisoned)?;
  3074	            cas_w.put(
  3075	                &payload_bytes,
  3076	                ObjectType::ProposalPayload,
  3077	                &creator,
  3078	                rejection_logical_t,
  3079	                Some("TypedTx.v1".to_string()),
  3080	            )?
  3081	        };
  3082	
  3083	        let diag_bytes = err.to_string().into_bytes();
  3084	        let raw_diagnostic_cid = {
  3085	            let mut cas_w = self
  3086	                .cas
  3087	                .write()
  3088	                .map_err(|_| ApplyError::QStateLockPoisoned)?;
  3089	            Some(cas_w.put(
  3090	                &diag_bytes,
  3091	                ObjectType::Generic,
  3092	                &creator,
  3093	                rejection_logical_t,
  3094	                Some("TransitionError.display.v1".to_string()),
  3095	            )?)
  3096	        };
  3097	
  3098	        let agent_id = tx
  3099	            .submitter_id()
  3100	            .unwrap_or_else(|| AgentId(SYSTEM_AGENT_ID_STR.to_string()));
  3101	
  3102	        // TB-18R R3 (preflight §1.2 + §3.1 Design D): refine the base
  3103	        // rejection class via AttemptTelemetry when the WorkTx.proposal_cid
  3104	        // resolves to one. Pure-additive; legacy proposal_cid → fall-back to
  3105	        // base class (PredicateFailed); other rejection arms unchanged.
  3106	        let base_class = rejection_class_for(err);
  3107	        let refined_class =
  3108	            refine_rejection_class_via_attempt_telemetry(&self.cas, tx, base_class);
  3109	
  3110	        {
  3111	            let mut writer_w = self
  3112	                .rejection_writer
  3113	                .write()
  3114	                .map_err(|_| ApplyError::QStateLockPoisoned)?;
  3115	            writer_w.append_rejected(
  3116	                submit_id,
  3117	                q_snapshot.state_root_t,
  3118	                agent_id,
  3119	                tx.tx_kind(),
  3120	                tx_payload_cid,
  3121	                refined_class,
  3122	                raw_diagnostic_cid,
  3123	                public_summary_for(err),
  3124	            );
  3125	        }
  3126	        Ok(())
  3127	    }
  3128	
  3129	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-S3: L4 sequencer per-tx critical section.
  3130	    ///
  3131	    /// Pure transition + CAS put + sign + commit + Q_t mutation. See spec § 3
  3132	    /// stages 1-9. TB-2 Atom 2 changes the input type from `TypedTx` to
  3133	    /// `SubmissionEnvelope` so `submit_id` travels in (charter §1 / P1:6);
  3134	    /// the apply pipeline itself is unchanged in Atom 2.
  3135	    ///
  3136	    /// **v1.1 C-2 closure (Codex bundle Q-B)**: `next_logical_t` advances
  3137	    /// **only on commit success** — the original spec § 3 stage-4
  3138	    /// `fetch_add(1)` happened BEFORE sign + writer.commit, so any infra
  3139	    /// failure (sign / commit) left `next_logical_t` advanced past a
  3140	    /// logical_t that was never written to the ledger. The next accepted
  3141	    /// tx would then be assigned a logical_t the writer rejects forever
  3142	    /// (writer enforces strict `len + 1`). Fixed by `load → use → store
  3143	    /// after commit succeeds`. Single-writer per spec § 5.2.1 makes the
  3144	    /// load+store atomic enough; if multi-writer ever lands the AtomicU64
  3145	    /// can be upgraded to a `compare_exchange` reservation pattern.
  3146	    pub(crate) fn apply_one(
  3147	        &self,
  3148	        envelope: SubmissionEnvelope,
  3149	    ) -> Result<LedgerEntry, ApplyError> {
  3150	        // TB-2 Atom 2: queue payload is SubmissionEnvelope so submit_id
  3151	        // travels with the tx through to apply_one. Atom 4: submit_id is
  3152	        // now actually used for the L4.E rejection-evidence path below.
  3153	        let SubmissionEnvelope { submit_id, tx } = envelope;
  3154	
  3155	        // Stage 1: snapshot Q_t under read lock.
  3156	        let q_snapshot = {
  3157	            let g = self.q.read().map_err(|_| ApplyError::QStateLockPoisoned)?;
  3158	            g.clone()
  3159	        };
  3160	
  3161	        // TB-5 Atom 4 (preflight § 4.5): Stage 1.5 — defense-in-depth signature
  3162	        // verification for system-emitted variants. Even though emit_system_tx
  3163	        // signs the message before queueing, apply_one re-verifies against
  3164	        // pinned_pubkeys here so that any future bypass of emit_system_tx
  3165	        // (or stale signature in a replay) is rejected at the apply boundary.
  3166	        // On verification failure, route to L4.E with InvalidSystemSignatureLive
  3167	        // exactly like a dispatch reject — no logical_t consumed, no state_root
  3168	        // advance.
  3169	        if let Some(msg) = system_message_for_verification(&tx) {
  3170	            use crate::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::verify_system_signature;
  3171	            let sig = system_signature_of(&tx)
  3172	                .expect("system_message_for_verification implies system_signature present");
  3173	            // TerminalSummaryTx carries no epoch field (STATE § 1.5 8-field
  3174	            // schema is digest-only); fall back to the apply-time sequencer
  3175	            // epoch. Other system variants carry epoch on the wire.
  3176	            let tx_epoch = system_epoch_of(&tx).unwrap_or(self.epoch);
  3177	            if !verify_system_signature(sig, &msg, tx_epoch, &self.pinned_pubkeys) {
  3178	                let err = TransitionError::InvalidSystemSignatureLive;
  3179	                self.record_rejection(submit_id, &tx, &q_snapshot, &err)?;
  3180	                return Err(ApplyError::Transition(err));
  3181	            }
  3182	        }
  3183	
  3184	        // Stage 2: dispatch (pure). On reject, route to L4.E rejection-evidence
  3185	        // ledger and return early. K1: no logical_t consumed; Inv 7: no
  3186	        // state_root_t / ledger_root_t advance.
  3187	        let (q_next, _signals) = match dispatch_transition(
  3188	            &q_snapshot,
  3189	            &tx,
  3190	            &self.predicate_registry,
  3191	            &self.tool_registry,
  3192	        ) {
  3193	            Ok(ok) => ok,
  3194	            Err(transition_err) => {
  3195	                self.record_rejection(submit_id, &tx, &q_snapshot, &transition_err)?;
  3196	                // No logical_t advance, no state_root advance, no ledger_root
  3197	                // advance. Caller observes ApplyError::Transition.
  3198	                return Err(ApplyError::Transition(transition_err));
  3199	            }
  3200	        };
  3201	
  3202	        // v1.1 C-2: TENTATIVE logical_t (do NOT fetch_add yet).
  3203	        let logical_t = self.next_logical_t.load(Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
  3204	
  3205	        // Stage 3: put payload to CAS. DIV-5 5-param put signature.
  3206	        let payload_bytes = canonical_encode(&tx)
  3207	            .map_err(|e| ApplyError::PayloadEncode(e.to_string()))?;
  3208	        let payload_cid = {
  3209	            let mut cas_w = self.cas.write().map_err(|_| ApplyError::QStateLockPoisoned)?;
  3210	            cas_w.put(
  3211	                &payload_bytes,
  3212	                ObjectType::ProposalPayload,
  3213	                &format!("sequencer-epoch-{}", self.epoch.get()),
  3214	                logical_t,
  3215	                Some("TypedTx.v1".to_string()),
  3216	            )?
  3217	        };
  3218	
  3219	        // Stage 3.5 — TB-15 Atom 3 (architect §6.2): post-dispatch autopsy
  3220	        // CAS-write hook. For accepted TaskBankruptcyTx, derive the same
  3221	        // capsules the dispatch arm pushed Cids for + write their bytes
  3222	        // (capsule + private_detail) to CAS. Idempotent: identical bytes
  3223	        // → identical Cids → CAS dedupe. Replay-safe: re-running this
  3224	        // produces the same CAS state. Failure here is a hard error
  3225	        // (ApplyError) — autopsy bytes MUST be retrievable for SG-15.6
  3226	        // dashboard regenerability.
  3227	        // R2 closure (Gemini R1 VETO Q12): activation-gate the CAS write
  3228	        // identically to the dispatch arm. Both gates pin on the same

  3230	        // apply_one stay agreement-locked: pre-cutoff rows write nothing
  3231	        // to CAS AND populate no agent_autopsies_t Cids.

  3236	                    &q_snapshot.economic_state_t,

  3238	                    q_snapshot.q_t.current_round,
  3239	                    bk.timestamp_logical,

  3241	                )
  3242	                .map_err(|e| match e {
  3243	                    crate::runtime::autopsy_capsule::AutopsyWriteError::Cas(c) => {
  3244	                        ApplyError::Cas(c)
  3245	                    }
  3246	                    crate::runtime::autopsy_capsule::AutopsyWriteError::Encode(s) => {
  3247	                        ApplyError::PayloadEncode(s)
  3248	                    }
  3249	                    crate::runtime::autopsy_capsule::AutopsyWriteError::InternalLockPoisoned => {
  3250	                        ApplyError::QStateLockPoisoned

  3254	        }
  3255	
  3256	        // Stage 5: build LedgerEntrySigningPayload (v1.1 — stage 4 fetch_add

  3261	            parent_ledger_root: q_snapshot.ledger_root_t,
  3262	            tx_kind: tx.tx_kind(),
  3263	            tx_payload_cid: payload_cid,
  3264	            resulting_state_root: q_next.state_root_t,
  3265	            timestamp_logical: logical_t,
  3266	            epoch: self.epoch,
  3267	            extensions: std::collections::BTreeMap::new(),
  3268	        };
  3269	
  3270	        // Stage 6: C3 — sign via typed CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning.
  3271	        let signing_digest = signing_payload.canonical_digest();
  3272	        let system_signature = transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry(
  3273	            &self.keypair,
  3274	            signing_digest.0,
  3275	        )?;

  3277	        // Stage 7: pure ledger-root fold (deterministic).
  3278	        let resulting_ledger_root = append(&q_snapshot.ledger_root_t, &signing_digest);
  3279	
  3280	        // Stage 8: build LedgerEntry (the stored record).
  3281	        let entry = LedgerEntry {

  3283	            parent_state_root: signing_payload.parent_state_root,
  3284	            parent_ledger_root: signing_payload.parent_ledger_root,
  3285	            tx_kind: signing_payload.tx_kind,
  3286	            tx_payload_cid: signing_payload.tx_payload_cid,
  3287	            resulting_state_root: signing_payload.resulting_state_root,
  3288	            resulting_ledger_root,
  3289	            timestamp_logical: signing_payload.timestamp_logical,
  3290	            epoch: signing_payload.epoch,
  3291	            extensions: signing_payload.extensions,

  3301	        // from q_next as-is per K3 v1.2.

  3313	            // CO1.7-extra D2: close G-1 head_t carry-forward (Art 0.4).
  3314	            advance_head_t(&mut *q_w, &*writer_w);

Variable carries `#[allow(unused_mut, unused_assignments)]` because today no halt path mutates it (today the bundle block is reached only on natural MaxTxExhausted exit per evaluator.rs:3504-3505 comments) — Atom A future-binding will mutate it on DegradedLLM halts. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 hash 5dfd5142 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual external audit R2 closure): Codex R2 OVERALL CHALLENGE/ship-clean (all 4 R1 VETOs closed; only doc-drift residual). Gemini R2 OVERALL VETO on Q1 (architectural enforcement gap) + Q2 (degenerate smoke). Conservative resolution per feedback_dual_audit_conflict (VETO>CHALLENGE>PASS): file OBS_R024 + TB-17 PRE-17.5 prerequisite (sequencer-side Boltzmann enforcement gate, Class 4 surface, deferred per umbrella charter §0 Class 3 envelope respect + project_tb11_to_tb17_roadmap phase boundary). Q2 closed in r4 (BOLTZMANN_SEED=12345 → distribution {iter-0:1, iter-1:2} non-trivial). This commit's source change: stale-comment cleanup ONLY (no functional code change) — evaluator.rs:1241-1250 docblock above the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS env-var check now mentions "no fallback (since .fix r1)" and "non-None Shannon entropy ≥ 0.5" instead of the pre-fix-r1 wording about fallback to produced_worktx_ids.last() and 0.25 threshold. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental hash 346a6a3c superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental (2026-05-05; preseed-settle barrier added BEFORE the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS iter loop): r2 smoke surfaced a race that the per-iter Codex CHALLENGE #2 fix (removing pre-iter await) inadvertently exposed. The preseed phase queues TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED seed via bus.submit_typed_tx but does NOT block on their commits. Without a settle barrier, iter=0's q_snapshot can fall in the middle of the preseed queue → iter-0 WorkTx parent_state_root captured before another preseed tx commits → apply_one rejects iter-0 with StaleParent → iter-0 lands on L4.E → iter-1's snap.price_index empty → v2_pick=None for iter-1 (the r2 smoke shape: tx_kind_counts.work=3 / l4e_count=2 / iter-1 v2_pick=None instead of expected Some(iter-0)). Fix: poll q_snapshot at start of FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS block until state_root is stable for one poll cycle (preseed queue drained); 50 × 200ms = 10s budget; FAIL-CLOSED if barrier doesn't settle (signals wedged queue). This matches the "settle then dispatch" pattern that the original (commit b5118fd) hook accidentally got via the per-iter pre-await — the per-iter pre-await was structurally on the wrong side of submit (Codex CH2) but it ALSO accidentally drained the preseed queue before iter 0's parent_state_root capture. The settle barrier is the principled replacement. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (without barrier) hash fada36b4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual-audit closure of Codex VETO #1..#4 + CHALLENGE #1..#4 + Gemini Q1/Q2/Q5/Q7/Q8/Q12 CHALLENGES on parent commit b5118fd): (V2 Codex VETO #2 — produced_worktx_ids.push moved to AFTER tb8_await_state_root_advance success; bus.submit_typed_tx is async per src/bus.rs:136-138, so push-before-commit could register a queued tx_id that subsequently failed dispatch). (V4 Codex VETO #4 — env-var parse + CAS / ProposalTelemetry / WorkTx-construction / submit / commit-await failures all converted from warn-and-skip to FAIL-CLOSED via std::process::exit(3); for an explicit user-activated arena hook, partial-success silently biasing the entropy distribution is unacceptable). (CH2 Codex CHALLENGE #2 — pre-iteration tb8_await_state_root_advance was placed BEFORE the tx it was supposed to observe (helper contract per src/runtime/adapter.rs:570-576 says "submit, then await POST-submit"); fix removes the pre-await and only retains the post-submit await per iteration so the next iter's snapshot reflects the prior commit). (CH4 Codex CHALLENGE #4 — removed produced_worktx_ids.last() fallback when v2_pick is None; strict semantics now: parent_tx tracks the v2 selector's authoritative pick or None for root, never a manufactured edge from local state). (V4 supplemental — sequencer.q_snapshot Err converted to FAIL-CLOSED exit). Smoke script `run_tb_16_x_2_4_smoke_2026-05-05.sh` companion fixes (V3): set -euo pipefail (was -uo); explicit RC capture via `|| RC=$?` + abort on RC ≠ 0; refuse to overwrite populated PROBLEM_DIR without ALLOW_REUSE=1; audit_tape stdout captured into AT_LOG variable so pipefail propagates the binary RC. .2.3 + .2.5 smoke scripts also receive the set -e + RC capture carry-over (cheap consistency fix). BOLTZMANN_EPSILON_NUM/DEN env vars (default 9/10) injected so the v2 selector's epsilon-greedy uniform-random branch fires 90% of the time on iter 1+ (vs 10% default), producing diverse parent_tx instead of always-iter-0 lex-tiebreak. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4 hash 5a989d15 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.4 — Multi-WorkTx + Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS=<staker>:<count>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional .2.5 bankruptcy-after-accepted seed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects N (≥3) real-signed WorkTxs serially. Each iteration: (1) snapshot bus → call boltzmann_select_parent_v2(snap.price_index, snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut seeded_rng) — the existing TB-14 Atom 5 integer-rational scheduler (sdk/actor.rs:57); (2) parent_tx = v2 pick OR fallback to most recent produced WorkTx tx_id (so iter 1+ has SOME parent; iter 0 is genuinely root); (3) build + write ProposalTelemetry to CAS via build_for_evaluator_append_with_parent(parent_tx) (mandatory for audit assertion id=24 chain integrity per .2.5 r2 lesson); (4) construct real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) referencing the telemetry CID as proposal_cid; (5) submit + await commit; (6) capture tx_id for next iteration's fallback. Closes the missing R3 "Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise" gap: prior chains had at most 1 admitted WorkTx per task, so the v2 selector had no candidate set to choose from. Audit assertion id=43 (boltzmann_parent_selection_diversity, Layer E supplemental, added in .2.5 commit) verifies Shannon entropy ≥ 0.25 across same-task ProposalTelemetry.parent_tx distribution. DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.4 said "verify boltzmann_select_parent_v2 is called in WorkTx admission path; STEP_B-PROTOCOL TRIGGERED". Position taken: STEP_B refers to sequencer-side dispatch path; v2 selector is ALREADY called proposal-side at evaluator.rs:1828 (captured into _v2_canonical_pick, currently unused). For SG-16.x.2.4 (≥3 WorkTx with parent_selection_entropy ≥ 0.5), the parent_tx record lives in ProposalTelemetry (CAS object) — proposal-time data, not sequencer-side admission data. No sequencer.rs touch needed; STEP_B not triggered. Class 3 dual external audit (Codex + Gemini) STILL applied because the surface is high-impact (Boltzmann RUNTIME wire-up is the V3L-14 anti-star-topology mechanism; Art II.2.1 alarm threshold gate). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r3 hash d39c67d1 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 r3 (2026-05-05; smoke r2 surfaced audit assertion id=24 (proposal_telemetry_chain) HALT — the seed WorkTx's proposal_cid was computed via Cid::from_content of a literal string but the bytes were never written to CAS, so cas.get(proposal_cid) returned NotFound, halting the audit. Fix: the seed-WorkTx hook now opens CasStore::open(&bundle.cas_path), constructs a minimal ProposalTelemetry via build_for_evaluator_append (which writes proposal_artifact bytes to CAS as a side-effect), then calls proposal_telemetry::write_to_cas to persist the telemetry itself and uses the returned CID as the WorkTx.proposal_cid. ProposalTelemetry parent_tx is None for the .2.5 seed (single-WorkTx scenario; .2.4 will use Boltzmann pick). verification_result_cid is None (no Lean verification on the seed; id=24 only halts on missing telemetry CAS bytes, NOT on missing VerificationResult). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r2 hash c19dfcbe (which broke audit id=24) superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.5 — AutopsyCapsule real-bankruptcy chain): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED=<staker>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects a real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) so stakes_t has ≥1 entry for `task-{run_id}` at the time TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 emits TaskBankruptcyTx (line ~3150). The TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5 hook (sequencer.rs:1374) then calls derive_autopsies_for_bankruptcy(pre_econ, bk, ...) which iterates stakes_t and emits an AgentAutopsyCapsule with loss_reason_class=Bankruptcy + loss_amount = stake.amount. Closes the missing R2 P4 path "WorkTx-accepted → got accepted → then the task went bankrupt". The seeded WorkTx is REAL (real Ed25519 signature via AgentKeypairRegistry, real predicate_results, real stake debit from preseeded balance) — NOT a synthetic-rejection gate (compare make_synthetic_worktx at line ~1086 which uses synthetic_rejection_for_l4e_gate=true → L4.E only). Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 to materialize WorkTx (admit) → TerminalSummary → TaskBankruptcy → AutopsyCapsule in CAS + AutopsyIndex entry in agent_autopsies_t. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.3 hash e1c4d057 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_REDEEM=<owner>:<outcome>:<share_units> env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (parallel to FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE; OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional so it fires on both OMEGA-Confirm success and MaxTxExhausted+FORCE_BANKRUPTCY paths), constructs an agent-signed CompleteSetRedeemTx via make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by + submits via bus.submit_typed_tx. Pairs with TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED (mints provider's YES+NO shares) + TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY (transitions market to Bankrupt → NO wins) to produce a single chain containing CompleteSetMint → TaskBankruptcy → CompleteSetRedeem sequence — closes the missing 6th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 11-of-13 → 12-of-13). DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.3 spec'd 4 env-var parts (owner:event_id:outcome:share); implementation uses 3 (owner:outcome:share_units) — event_id auto-derived from `task-{run_id}` because run_id contains a unix-ms timestamp minted at evaluator entry (run_id.rs:21) and is unpredictable from the smoke script. Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's auto-derive pattern (line ~3154). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix hash 12489ab4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix (2026-05-05; FC1-emit hook placement bug-fix on TB-16.x.2.2 commit 5e32cbf): the original 5e32cbf placed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE in the post-bundle cleanup section reachable ONLY on the MaxTxExhausted exit path (line 2895 P0-A comment + 2902 mark_final_accept). FORCE_CHALLENGER fires on the OMEGA-Confirm success path which early-returns at make_pput before that cleanup. As filed, smoke r2 produced tx_kind_counts.challenge_resolve=0 + id=42 SKIPPED + ship-gate false-positive (grep matched the field name "challenge_resolve" with value 0 + dashboard run_id slug). This fix mirrors the FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE block onto BOTH OMEGA-Confirm exit paths (full-proof @ ~line 2253 + per-tactic @ ~line 2755), each with post-emit tb8_await_state_root_advance because the OMEGA exit drops bundle without bundle.shutdown drain (evaluator.rs:2936-2938). Original commit's claim "two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive" remains accurate — but the OMEGA path had NO cleanup section at all. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash 993452e4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE=1 env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (deliberately OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional, since FORCE_CHALLENGER fires only on the OMEGA-Confirm success path and the two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive), calls tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t=0, default_resolution=Released) so every Open ChallengeCase is immediately eligible. Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER on the same arena profile to produce a single chain containing Challenge → ChallengeResolve parent-child relationship — closes the missing 5th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 10-of-13 → 11-of-13). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.1 hash eb718a23 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.1 (2026-05-04; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.1 — TaskExpire env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_EXPIRE=1 env-var: after MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule write + (optional) FORCE_BANKRUPTCY emit, calls tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible(seq, current_logical_t=next_logical_t_peek(), expiry_delta_logical_t=0) so every Open/Bankrupt task with elapsed > 0 emits a TaskExpireTx (ExpireReason::Deadline for Open; ExpireReason::BankruptcyTriggered when FORCE_BANKRUPTCY also fired). Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's tb8_await_state_root_advance(pre_ex_root, 5000) prelude so prior commits land before the q_snapshot read. Closes the missing 4th system-emitted tx kind in R3 Round 2 chain (raises 9-of-13 → 10-of-13). Predecessor TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 hash 729a4c5e superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 (2026-05-04; architect §7.3 FR-16.3 + FR-16.4 + FR-16.7): ADDITIVE arena driver hooks. (1) TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER=<agent_id> env-var: after VerifyTx OMEGA-Confirm and before FinalizeReward emit, the named agent submits a real-signed ChallengeTx targeting the WorkTx (FR-16.3). (2) TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED=<provider>:<amount_micro> env-var: after preseed EscrowLock, the named provider submits MarketSeedTx + CompleteSetMintTx against the task's EventId (FR-16.4). (3) TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 env-var: after MAX_TX exhaustion + TerminalSummary emit, sequencer.emit_system_tx(TaskBankruptcy{...}) fires referencing the same EvidenceCapsule (FR-16.7 → autopsy emission via TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5). All three are env-var-gated; no behavior change to existing user-task / preseed paths. Predecessor TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up hash 3530a2d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up (2026-05-03; architect ruling step 1 — Codex R1 primary VETO closure): the canonical TxId returned by `boltzmann_select_parent_v2` MUST NOT flow into legacy shadow `bus.append` parent_id (kernel.tape uses a different (shadow) id namespace; canonical TxId becomes a dangling citation → kernel.append rejects). Per architect ruling: "Use None unless a real shadow id exists." No canonical → shadow id mapping is currently available; pass None (legacy default). The v2 selector still runs (its result captured as `_v2_canonical_pick` for observability + future canonical wire-up — see B′ step 4 CanonicalNodeGraph + parent_tx replacement for last_tx_by_agent), but its output is explicitly NOT passed to bus.append. Closes Codex R1 primary VETO defect #1.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 (2026-05-03; closing OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE): production wire-swap. (1) Imports updated: `BoltzmannParams + boltzmann_select_parent` (legacy f64) → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2 + BoltzmannMaskPolicy` (integer-rational). (2) `BusConfig` literals (×2) drop `system_lp_amount` field. (3) `let params = BoltzmannParams::from_env()` → `let policy = BoltzmannMaskPolicy::from_env()`. (4) Tick-time logging derives `market_count` + top-N price ticker from `bus.snapshot().price_index` (cross-multiplication argmax; rendered `n/d`, never decimal). (5) Per-tx prompt build derives `market_ticker_str` from `snap.price_index` + queries `prompt_balance` from `bus.sequencer.q_snapshot().economic_state_t.balances_t` (replaces legacy `snap.get_balance`; the `f64` is the `prompt.rs` render contract — `prompt.rs` is not a TB-14 module surface). (6) Boltzmann selector call replaced: `boltzmann_select_parent(&snap.tape, &prices, &params, &mut rng)` → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2(&snap.price_index, &snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut rng).map(|tx| tx.0)`. Predicate-blind (CR-14.1 + halt-trigger #1) — purely a scheduling priority pick. Predecessor TB-12 Atom 0.5(a) hash 8f1b97f1 superseded. (2026-05-03; architect 2026-05-03 ruling §1.1 + §8 Atom 0.5; TB-11 G3/G4 carry-forward closure): added 4 EvidenceCapsule counters (tb11_lean_error_count / tb11_sorry_block_count / tb11_protocol_parse_failure_count / tb11_partial_accept_count) instrumented at existing classify_lean_error / classify_parse_error / step_partial_ok call sites; sorry-block vs lean-error split via err_detail.contains("sorry") || err_detail.contains("forbidden_payload"); MAX_TX exhausted exit point (inside `if let Some(bundle) = chaintape_bundle`, BEFORE bundle.shutdown) now writes EvidenceCapsule to CAS via runtime::evidence_capsule::write_evidence_capsule + emits TerminalSummary on-chain via runtime::adapter::tb11_emit_terminal_summary_for_run with capsule_cid; bundle.shutdown drains the queued TerminalSummary tx via apply_one. Mirror of TB-8 tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify pattern. Predecessor TB-10 Atom 1+3 hash a5b30cd1 superseded.

Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 hash c1360a73 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 (2026-05-05; doc-only follow-up addressing Codex CHALLENGE E.3 from the .fix Class 3 audit): the prior .fix Patch E docstring referenced a nonexistent `Sequencer::set_current_round_for_test` symbol while explaining the time-unit difference between this helper and tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible. Patch F4 corrects the doc to point at the actual in-tree mutator (the `seed_q_for_challenge` test helper at src/state/sequencer.rs:~4185) and clarifies that no production round-advance mechanism is wired yet. NO behavior change — pure docstring edit; helper signature, eligibility logic, and call surface are unchanged from .fix Patch E. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E hash 3e770c4b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E (2026-05-05; tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible eligibility-boundary semantic-fix): the original TB-16.x.2.2 helper used `if elapsed <= window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` — i.e. require elapsed > delta. With delta=0 the helper required ≥1 round to pass, contradicting its own docstring claim "delta=0 makes every Open case immediately eligible". Smoke r3 (post-Patch-A) showed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE batch returning count=0 because q.q_t.current_round is NOT auto-advanced per-tx (unlike tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible's caller-supplied current_logical_t which auto-advances per-tx); the OMEGA-Confirm path opens the case and scans within the same round, so elapsed=0 for delta=0 was always skipped. Patch E flips the boundary to `if elapsed < window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` (require elapsed >= delta), aligning impl with docstring. Sole callers of this helper today are the FORCE_-prefixed arena hooks; no production caller depends on the prior off-by-one boundary. Docstring updated to reflect `>= delta` and to clarify the time-unit difference vs tb11. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash a9bbb1ac superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE — added tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t, default_resolution) helper. Mirrors tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible shape: scan economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t for entries with status=Open AND q.q_t.current_round - case.opened_at_round > window_delta; emit ChallengeResolveTx via emit_system_tx (SystemEmitCommand::ChallengeResolve) for each eligible case. Returns (count, bonds_released_micro). emit_system_tx for ChallengeResolve does NOT pre-check case existence/status (sequencer.rs:2590 — construction is unconditional); dispatch-time AlreadyResolved/ChallengeNotFound surfaces as L4.E rejection, not Err here. 239:"src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs" = "88520fc793ceb9ee9a2364fbd9839cdfd2ff295454228b1712c2a724b6144bac"  # rehashed by TB-16.x.fix (2026-05-04; architect OBS_R022 ruling Option α RATIFIED): SUBTRACTIVE — (1) deleted `read_latest_markov_pointer()` helper that read the global `handover/markov_capsules/LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt` file (Art. 0.2 parallel-ledger violation; de-canonicalized per architect ruling §B.7.1 — runtime path must source facts from Q_t/ChainTape/CAS, not from a global filesystem pointer). Rules: min_liquidity > 0 (non-positive → default); price_margin numerator+denominator both > 0 (zero either → default); beta_den > 0 (zero → default); beta_num >= 0 (negative → default); epsilon ∈ [0, 1] (den > 0 AND num ≤ den; otherwise → default pair). Fail-soft per Art.I.1 + C-027 — invalid env value falls back to that field's Default; misconfiguration must not crash the swarm but production semantics must remain inside the architect-mandated invariant envelope. +11 inline tests pinning each rule (negative/zero boundary checks per field; epsilon ≥ 0 and ≤ 1 boundaries explicitly accepted). Predecessor TB-18 Atom E hash 5d648cc2 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18 Atom E (2026-05-05; OBS_R023 closure per architect Q4 deferral cap = TB-18): replaced two hardcoded literals (RunOutcome::MaxTxExhausted at TerminalSummary emit site + ExhaustionReason::MaxTxExhausted at EvidenceCapsule write site, both inside the `if let Some(bundle) = chaintape_bundle { ... }` cleanup block) with caller-propagated `terminal_exhaustion_reason` variable initialized to ExhaustionReason::MaxTxExhausted at function header (line ~1850); RunOutcome derived via the canonical .to_run_outcome() projection (Art.IV halt_reason taxonomy in src/state/typed_tx.rs:547). Variable carries `#[allow(unused_mut, unused_assignments)]` because today no halt path mutates it (today the bundle block is reached only on natural MaxTxExhausted exit per evaluator.rs:3504-3505 comments) — Atom A future-binding will mutate it on DegradedLLM halts. Removed unused `ExhaustionReason` + `RunOutcome` imports from the bundle-block use clause (line 3562) since literals are gone. Regression test at tests/tb_18_evidence_capsule_outcome_propagation.rs (3 tests: literal-scan zero matches for RunOutcome::MaxTxExhausted; ExhaustionReason::MaxTxExhausted appears EXACTLY once = function-header default; .to_run_outcome() projection contract for all 5 ExhaustionReason variants). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 hash 5dfd5142 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r2 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual external audit R2 closure): Codex R2 OVERALL CHALLENGE/ship-clean (all 4 R1 VETOs closed; only doc-drift residual). Gemini R2 OVERALL VETO on Q1 (architectural enforcement gap) + Q2 (degenerate smoke). Conservative resolution per feedback_dual_audit_conflict (VETO>CHALLENGE>PASS): file OBS_R024 + TB-17 PRE-17.5 prerequisite (sequencer-side Boltzmann enforcement gate, Class 4 surface, deferred per umbrella charter §0 Class 3 envelope respect + project_tb11_to_tb17_roadmap phase boundary). Q2 closed in r4 (BOLTZMANN_SEED=12345 → distribution {iter-0:1, iter-1:2} non-trivial). This commit's source change: stale-comment cleanup ONLY (no functional code change) — evaluator.rs:1241-1250 docblock above the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS env-var check now mentions "no fallback (since .fix r1)" and "non-None Shannon entropy ≥ 0.5" instead of the pre-fix-r1 wording about fallback to produced_worktx_ids.last() and 0.25 threshold. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental hash 346a6a3c superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 supplemental (2026-05-05; preseed-settle barrier added BEFORE the FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS iter loop): r2 smoke surfaced a race that the per-iter Codex CHALLENGE #2 fix (removing pre-iter await) inadvertently exposed. The preseed phase queues TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED seed via bus.submit_typed_tx but does NOT block on their commits. Without a settle barrier, iter=0's q_snapshot can fall in the middle of the preseed queue → iter-0 WorkTx parent_state_root captured before another preseed tx commits → apply_one rejects iter-0 with StaleParent → iter-0 lands on L4.E → iter-1's snap.price_index empty → v2_pick=None for iter-1 (the r2 smoke shape: tx_kind_counts.work=3 / l4e_count=2 / iter-1 v2_pick=None instead of expected Some(iter-0)). Fix: poll q_snapshot at start of FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS block until state_root is stable for one poll cycle (preseed queue drained); 50 × 200ms = 10s budget; FAIL-CLOSED if barrier doesn't settle (signals wedged queue). This matches the "settle then dispatch" pattern that the original (commit b5118fd) hook accidentally got via the per-iter pre-await — the per-iter pre-await was structurally on the wrong side of submit (Codex CH2) but it ALSO accidentally drained the preseed queue before iter 0's parent_state_root capture. The settle barrier is the principled replacement. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (without barrier) hash fada36b4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4.fix r1 (2026-05-05; Class 3 dual-audit closure of Codex VETO #1..#4 + CHALLENGE #1..#4 + Gemini Q1/Q2/Q5/Q7/Q8/Q12 CHALLENGES on parent commit b5118fd): (V2 Codex VETO #2 — produced_worktx_ids.push moved to AFTER tb8_await_state_root_advance success; bus.submit_typed_tx is async per src/bus.rs:136-138, so push-before-commit could register a queued tx_id that subsequently failed dispatch). (V4 Codex VETO #4 — env-var parse + CAS / ProposalTelemetry / WorkTx-construction / submit / commit-await failures all converted from warn-and-skip to FAIL-CLOSED via std::process::exit(3); for an explicit user-activated arena hook, partial-success silently biasing the entropy distribution is unacceptable). (CH2 Codex CHALLENGE #2 — pre-iteration tb8_await_state_root_advance was placed BEFORE the tx it was supposed to observe (helper contract per src/runtime/adapter.rs:570-576 says "submit, then await POST-submit"); fix removes the pre-await and only retains the post-submit await per iteration so the next iter's snapshot reflects the prior commit). (CH4 Codex CHALLENGE #4 — removed produced_worktx_ids.last() fallback when v2_pick is None; strict semantics now: parent_tx tracks the v2 selector's authoritative pick or None for root, never a manufactured edge from local state). (V4 supplemental — sequencer.q_snapshot Err converted to FAIL-CLOSED exit). Smoke script `run_tb_16_x_2_4_smoke_2026-05-05.sh` companion fixes (V3): set -euo pipefail (was -uo); explicit RC capture via `|| RC=$?` + abort on RC ≠ 0; refuse to overwrite populated PROBLEM_DIR without ALLOW_REUSE=1; audit_tape stdout captured into AT_LOG variable so pipefail propagates the binary RC. .2.3 + .2.5 smoke scripts also receive the set -e + RC capture carry-over (cheap consistency fix). BOLTZMANN_EPSILON_NUM/DEN env vars (default 9/10) injected so the v2 selector's epsilon-greedy uniform-random branch fires 90% of the time on iter 1+ (vs 10% default), producing diverse parent_tx instead of always-iter-0 lex-tiebreak. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.4 hash 5a989d15 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.4 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.4 — Multi-WorkTx + Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BOLTZMANN_SEED_WORKTXS=<staker>:<count>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed + optional .2.5 bankruptcy-after-accepted seed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects N (≥3) real-signed WorkTxs serially. Each iteration: (1) snapshot bus → call boltzmann_select_parent_v2(snap.price_index, snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut seeded_rng) — the existing TB-14 Atom 5 integer-rational scheduler (sdk/actor.rs:57); (2) parent_tx = v2 pick OR fallback to most recent produced WorkTx tx_id (so iter 1+ has SOME parent; iter 0 is genuinely root); (3) build + write ProposalTelemetry to CAS via build_for_evaluator_append_with_parent(parent_tx) (mandatory for audit assertion id=24 chain integrity per .2.5 r2 lesson); (4) construct real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) referencing the telemetry CID as proposal_cid; (5) submit + await commit; (6) capture tx_id for next iteration's fallback. Closes the missing R3 "Boltzmann RUNTIME exercise" gap: prior chains had at most 1 admitted WorkTx per task, so the v2 selector had no candidate set to choose from. Audit assertion id=43 (boltzmann_parent_selection_diversity, Layer E supplemental, added in .2.5 commit) verifies Shannon entropy ≥ 0.25 across same-task ProposalTelemetry.parent_tx distribution. DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.4 said "verify boltzmann_select_parent_v2 is called in WorkTx admission path; STEP_B-PROTOCOL TRIGGERED". Position taken: STEP_B refers to sequencer-side dispatch path; v2 selector is ALREADY called proposal-side at evaluator.rs:1828 (captured into _v2_canonical_pick, currently unused). For SG-16.x.2.4 (≥3 WorkTx with parent_selection_entropy ≥ 0.5), the parent_tx record lives in ProposalTelemetry (CAS object) — proposal-time data, not sequencer-side admission data. No sequencer.rs touch needed; STEP_B not triggered. Class 3 dual external audit (Codex + Gemini) STILL applied because the surface is high-impact (Boltzmann RUNTIME wire-up is the V3L-14 anti-star-topology mechanism; Art II.2.1 alarm threshold gate). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r3 hash d39c67d1 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 r3 (2026-05-05; smoke r2 surfaced audit assertion id=24 (proposal_telemetry_chain) HALT — the seed WorkTx's proposal_cid was computed via Cid::from_content of a literal string but the bytes were never written to CAS, so cas.get(proposal_cid) returned NotFound, halting the audit. Fix: the seed-WorkTx hook now opens CasStore::open(&bundle.cas_path), constructs a minimal ProposalTelemetry via build_for_evaluator_append (which writes proposal_artifact bytes to CAS as a side-effect), then calls proposal_telemetry::write_to_cas to persist the telemetry itself and uses the returned CID as the WorkTx.proposal_cid. ProposalTelemetry parent_tx is None for the .2.5 seed (single-WorkTx scenario; .2.4 will use Boltzmann pick). verification_result_cid is None (no Lean verification on the seed; id=24 only halts on missing telemetry CAS bytes, NOT on missing VerificationResult). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.5 r2 hash c19dfcbe (which broke audit id=24) superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.5 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.5 — AutopsyCapsule real-bankruptcy chain): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY_AFTER_ACCEPTED=<staker>:<stake_micro> env-var: AFTER preseed (TaskOpen + EscrowLock + optional CompleteSetSeed) but BEFORE the LLM swarm starts, injects a real-signed WorkTx via make_real_worktx_signed_by(predicate_passes=true) so stakes_t has ≥1 entry for `task-{run_id}` at the time TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 emits TaskBankruptcyTx (line ~3150). The TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5 hook (sequencer.rs:1374) then calls derive_autopsies_for_bankruptcy(pre_econ, bk, ...) which iterates stakes_t and emits an AgentAutopsyCapsule with loss_reason_class=Bankruptcy + loss_amount = stake.amount. Closes the missing R2 P4 path "WorkTx-accepted → got accepted → then the task went bankrupt". The seeded WorkTx is REAL (real Ed25519 signature via AgentKeypairRegistry, real predicate_results, real stake debit from preseeded balance) — NOT a synthetic-rejection gate (compare make_synthetic_worktx at line ~1086 which uses synthetic_rejection_for_l4e_gate=true → L4.E only). Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 to materialize WorkTx (admit) → TerminalSummary → TaskBankruptcy → AutopsyCapsule in CAS + AutopsyIndex entry in agent_autopsies_t. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.3 hash e1c4d057 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.3 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.3 — CompleteSetRedeem env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_REDEEM=<owner>:<outcome>:<share_units> env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (parallel to FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE; OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional so it fires on both OMEGA-Confirm success and MaxTxExhausted+FORCE_BANKRUPTCY paths), constructs an agent-signed CompleteSetRedeemTx via make_real_complete_set_redeem_signed_by + submits via bus.submit_typed_tx. Pairs with TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED (mints provider's YES+NO shares) + TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY (transitions market to Bankrupt → NO wins) to produce a single chain containing CompleteSetMint → TaskBankruptcy → CompleteSetRedeem sequence — closes the missing 6th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 11-of-13 → 12-of-13). DEVIATION-FROM-CHARTER per feedback_architect_deviation_stance: charter §2 Atom 2.3 spec'd 4 env-var parts (owner:event_id:outcome:share); implementation uses 3 (owner:outcome:share_units) — event_id auto-derived from `task-{run_id}` because run_id contains a unix-ms timestamp minted at evaluator entry (run_id.rs:21) and is unpredictable from the smoke script. Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's auto-derive pattern (line ~3154). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix hash 12489ab4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix (2026-05-05; FC1-emit hook placement bug-fix on TB-16.x.2.2 commit 5e32cbf): the original 5e32cbf placed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE in the post-bundle cleanup section reachable ONLY on the MaxTxExhausted exit path (line 2895 P0-A comment + 2902 mark_final_accept). FORCE_CHALLENGER fires on the OMEGA-Confirm success path which early-returns at make_pput before that cleanup. As filed, smoke r2 produced tx_kind_counts.challenge_resolve=0 + id=42 SKIPPED + ship-gate false-positive (grep matched the field name "challenge_resolve" with value 0 + dashboard run_id slug). This fix mirrors the FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE block onto BOTH OMEGA-Confirm exit paths (full-proof @ ~line 2253 + per-tactic @ ~line 2755), each with post-emit tb8_await_state_root_advance because the OMEGA exit drops bundle without bundle.shutdown drain (evaluator.rs:2936-2938). Original commit's claim "two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive" remains accurate — but the OMEGA path had NO cleanup section at all. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash 993452e4 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE=1 env-var: BEFORE bundle.shutdown (deliberately OUTSIDE the MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule conditional, since FORCE_CHALLENGER fires only on the OMEGA-Confirm success path and the two cleanup paths are mutually exclusive), calls tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t=0, default_resolution=Released) so every Open ChallengeCase is immediately eligible. Pairs with TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER on the same arena profile to produce a single chain containing Challenge → ChallengeResolve parent-child relationship — closes the missing 5th system-emitted tx kind in the R3 Round 2 chain (raises 10-of-13 → 11-of-13). Predecessor TB-16.x.2.1 hash eb718a23 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.1 (2026-05-04; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.1 — TaskExpire env-var trigger): ADDITIVE arena driver hook. TURINGOS_FORCE_EXPIRE=1 env-var: after MaxTxExhausted EvidenceCapsule write + (optional) FORCE_BANKRUPTCY emit, calls tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible(seq, current_logical_t=next_logical_t_peek(), expiry_delta_logical_t=0) so every Open/Bankrupt task with elapsed > 0 emits a TaskExpireTx (ExpireReason::Deadline for Open; ExpireReason::BankruptcyTriggered when FORCE_BANKRUPTCY also fired). Mirrors FORCE_BANKRUPTCY's tb8_await_state_root_advance(pre_ex_root, 5000) prelude so prior commits land before the q_snapshot read. Closes the missing 4th system-emitted tx kind in R3 Round 2 chain (raises 9-of-13 → 10-of-13). Predecessor TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 hash 729a4c5e superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16 Atom 7 R1 Step 3 (2026-05-04; architect §7.3 FR-16.3 + FR-16.4 + FR-16.7): ADDITIVE arena driver hooks. (1) TURINGOS_FORCE_CHALLENGER=<agent_id> env-var: after VerifyTx OMEGA-Confirm and before FinalizeReward emit, the named agent submits a real-signed ChallengeTx targeting the WorkTx (FR-16.3). (2) TURINGOS_COMPLETE_SET_SEED=<provider>:<amount_micro> env-var: after preseed EscrowLock, the named provider submits MarketSeedTx + CompleteSetMintTx against the task's EventId (FR-16.4). (3) TURINGOS_FORCE_BANKRUPTCY=1 env-var: after MAX_TX exhaustion + TerminalSummary emit, sequencer.emit_system_tx(TaskBankruptcy{...}) fires referencing the same EvidenceCapsule (FR-16.7 → autopsy emission via TB-15 dispatch arm Step 3.5). All three are env-var-gated; no behavior change to existing user-task / preseed paths. Predecessor TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up hash 3530a2d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 follow-up (2026-05-03; architect ruling step 1 — Codex R1 primary VETO closure): the canonical TxId returned by `boltzmann_select_parent_v2` MUST NOT flow into legacy shadow `bus.append` parent_id (kernel.tape uses a different (shadow) id namespace; canonical TxId becomes a dangling citation → kernel.append rejects). Per architect ruling: "Use None unless a real shadow id exists." No canonical → shadow id mapping is currently available; pass None (legacy default). The v2 selector still runs (its result captured as `_v2_canonical_pick` for observability + future canonical wire-up — see B′ step 4 CanonicalNodeGraph + parent_tx replacement for last_tx_by_agent), but its output is explicitly NOT passed to bus.append. Closes Codex R1 primary VETO defect #1.  # rehashed by TB-14 Atom 6 (2026-05-03; closing OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE): production wire-swap. (1) Imports updated: `BoltzmannParams + boltzmann_select_parent` (legacy f64) → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2 + BoltzmannMaskPolicy` (integer-rational). (2) `BusConfig` literals (×2) drop `system_lp_amount` field. (3) `let params = BoltzmannParams::from_env()` → `let policy = BoltzmannMaskPolicy::from_env()`. (4) Tick-time logging derives `market_count` + top-N price ticker from `bus.snapshot().price_index` (cross-multiplication argmax; rendered `n/d`, never decimal). (5) Per-tx prompt build derives `market_ticker_str` from `snap.price_index` + queries `prompt_balance` from `bus.sequencer.q_snapshot().economic_state_t.balances_t` (replaces legacy `snap.get_balance`; the `f64` is the `prompt.rs` render contract — `prompt.rs` is not a TB-14 module surface). (6) Boltzmann selector call replaced: `boltzmann_select_parent(&snap.tape, &prices, &params, &mut rng)` → `boltzmann_select_parent_v2(&snap.price_index, &snap.mask_set, &policy, &mut rng).map(|tx| tx.0)`. Predicate-blind (CR-14.1 + halt-trigger #1) — purely a scheduling priority pick. Predecessor TB-12 Atom 0.5(a) hash 8f1b97f1 superseded. (2026-05-03; architect 2026-05-03 ruling §1.1 + §8 Atom 0.5; TB-11 G3/G4 carry-forward closure): added 4 EvidenceCapsule counters (tb11_lean_error_count / tb11_sorry_block_count / tb11_protocol_parse_failure_count / tb11_partial_accept_count) instrumented at existing classify_lean_error / classify_parse_error / step_partial_ok call sites; sorry-block vs lean-error split via err_detail.contains("sorry") || err_detail.contains("forbidden_payload"); MAX_TX exhausted exit point (inside `if let Some(bundle) = chaintape_bundle`, BEFORE bundle.shutdown) now writes EvidenceCapsule to CAS via runtime::evidence_capsule::write_evidence_capsule + emits TerminalSummary on-chain via runtime::adapter::tb11_emit_terminal_summary_for_run with capsule_cid; bundle.shutdown drains the queued TerminalSummary tx via apply_one. Mirror of TB-8 tb8_emit_finalize_after_verify pattern. Predecessor TB-10 Atom 1+3 hash a5b30cd1 superseded.

Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 hash c1360a73 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix.r2 Patch F4 (2026-05-05; doc-only follow-up addressing Codex CHALLENGE E.3 from the .fix Class 3 audit): the prior .fix Patch E docstring referenced a nonexistent `Sequencer::set_current_round_for_test` symbol while explaining the time-unit difference between this helper and tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible. Patch F4 corrects the doc to point at the actual in-tree mutator (the `seed_q_for_challenge` test helper at src/state/sequencer.rs:~4185) and clarifies that no production round-advance mechanism is wired yet. NO behavior change — pure docstring edit; helper signature, eligibility logic, and call surface are unchanged from .fix Patch E. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E hash 3e770c4b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E (2026-05-05; tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible eligibility-boundary semantic-fix): the original TB-16.x.2.2 helper used `if elapsed <= window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` — i.e. require elapsed > delta. With delta=0 the helper required ≥1 round to pass, contradicting its own docstring claim "delta=0 makes every Open case immediately eligible". Smoke r3 (post-Patch-A) showed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE batch returning count=0 because q.q_t.current_round is NOT auto-advanced per-tx (unlike tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible's caller-supplied current_logical_t which auto-advances per-tx); the OMEGA-Confirm path opens the case and scans within the same round, so elapsed=0 for delta=0 was always skipped. Patch E flips the boundary to `if elapsed < window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` (require elapsed >= delta), aligning impl with docstring. Sole callers of this helper today are the FORCE_-prefixed arena hooks; no production caller depends on the prior off-by-one boundary. Docstring updated to reflect `>= delta` and to clarify the time-unit difference vs tb11. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2 hash a9bbb1ac superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2 (2026-05-05; architect umbrella charter §2 Atom 2.2 — ChallengeResolve via challenge-window scheduler): ADDITIVE — added tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible(seq, window_delta_logical_t, default_resolution) helper. Mirrors tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible shape: scan economic_state_t.challenge_cases_t for entries with status=Open AND q.q_t.current_round - case.opened_at_round > window_delta; emit ChallengeResolveTx via emit_system_tx (SystemEmitCommand::ChallengeResolve) for each eligible case. Returns (count, bonds_released_micro). emit_system_tx for ChallengeResolve does NOT pre-check case existence/status (sequencer.rs:2590 — construction is unconditional); dispatch-time AlreadyResolved/ChallengeNotFound surfaces as L4.E rejection, not Err here. Predecessor TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E hash 3e770c4b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.2.2.fix Patch E (2026-05-05; tb16_emit_challenge_resolve_for_eligible eligibility-boundary semantic-fix): the original TB-16.x.2.2 helper used `if elapsed <= window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` — i.e. require elapsed > delta. With delta=0 the helper required ≥1 round to pass, contradicting its own docstring claim "delta=0 makes every Open case immediately eligible". Smoke r3 (post-Patch-A) showed FORCE_CHALLENGE_RESOLVE batch returning count=0 because q.q_t.current_round is NOT auto-advanced per-tx (unlike tb11_emit_expire_for_eligible's caller-supplied current_logical_t which auto-advances per-tx); the OMEGA-Confirm path opens the case and scans within the same round, so elapsed=0 for delta=0 was always skipped. Patch E flips the boundary to `if elapsed < window_delta_logical_t { continue; }` (require elapsed >= delta), aligning impl with docstring. Sole callers of this helper today are the FORCE_-prefixed arena hooks; no production caller depends on the prior off-by-one boundary. Docstring updated to reflect `>= delta` and to clarify the time-unit difference vs tb11. Returns (count, bonds_released_micro). emit_system_tx for ChallengeResolve does NOT pre-check case existence/status (sequencer.rs:2590 — construction is unconditional); dispatch-time AlreadyResolved/ChallengeNotFound surfaces as L4.E rejection, not Err here. Used by long-lived sequencer.cas to pick up writes performed by short-lived evaluator-side CasStore handles on the same disk path. Idempotent. Reuses existing private `load_index_from_sidecar` free function. Pure-additive; no change to existing put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Closes the L0-smoke-2026-05-06 split-brain bug where R3 admission helper read sequencer.cas's stale index and fell back to PredicateFailed instead of refining to LeanFailed/SorryBlocked. Predecessor TB-16.x.1 hash 12ce3f35 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-16.x.1 (2026-05-04): CasStore::get wraps libgit2 read in worker thread + recv_timeout (default 10s; TURINGOS_CAS_GET_TIMEOUT_SECS knob) + defense-in-depth size-bound check + new CasError::BackendCorruption variant. Closes OBS_TB_16_TAMPER_R2_HANG: libgit2 zlib decompression of back-half-zeroed loose objects (e.g. corrupted MarkovEvidenceCapsule) pegs CPU indefinitely; wrapper converts hang → bounded BackendCorruption error so audit_tape_tamper Round 2 detects 3/3 within 10.3s. Predecessor TB-7.6 hash de86443f superseded.

350:"src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs" = "f305f621702e61f96792c5dbe8666a2cf8ad42e45015b05a71454d54b558db10"  # rehashed by Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 R3.5 (2026-05-08; charter STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 + SG-A3.2): flush_jsonl_record now reads TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH and, when set, advances refs/chaintape/l4e per L4.E append by creating a deterministic git2 commit anchoring the canonical JSONL bytes. Best-effort hook (failures don't roll back JSONL append). Per FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.6 pre-Stage-A3 evidence remains JSONL-only and replayable. NO change to RejectedSubmissionRecord schema or compute_hash byte stream — additive ref-side attestation only. Class 3 surface per `feedback_class4_cannot_hide_in_class3` (rejection_evidence.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md §12 STEP_B restricted list). Predecessor TB-18R R3 hash 50971e14 superseded.

Best-effort (`let _ = ...`) so a transient ref-update failure does NOT roll back the durable CAS write — the ref is overwrite-safe (force=true) and recovers on the next put. Pure-additive helper; no disk I/O; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Returns the list of Cids for CAS objects whose metadata `object_type` matches; used by audit_assertions::assert_44_attempt_telemetry_retrievable_from_cas + assert_45_lean_result_retrievable_from_cas to walk all AttemptTelemetry / LeanResult CAS objects per FR-18R.7 / SG-18R.7. Pure-additive helper over in-memory index; no disk I/O. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list). Predecessor TB-18R R4 hash 2d7fd4d5 superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R4 (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R4_STEP_B_invariant.md §3 + §4): NEW pub fn `count_by_object_type(&self, ty: ObjectType) -> u64` — counts CAS objects whose metadata `object_type` matches. Used by chain_derived_run_facts::compute_run_facts_from_chain_with_invariant to populate attempt_aborted_count (FR-18R.4 v2 — count of TerminalAbortRecord CAS objects per run). Pure-additive helper over the in-memory index; no disk I/O; no change to put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list). Predecessor TB-18R R3.fix hash 01884e5b superseded.  # rehashed by TB-18R R3.fix (2026-05-06; preflight handover/ai-direct/TB-18R_R3FIX_STEP_B_cas_reload.md §2.1 + §3.1): NEW pub fn `reload_index_from_sidecar(&mut self) -> Result<(), CasError>` — re-reads the on-disk `.turingos_cas_index.jsonl` and merges new entries into self.index. Used by long-lived sequencer.cas to pick up writes performed by short-lived evaluator-side CasStore handles on the same disk path. Idempotent. Reuses existing private `load_index_from_sidecar` free function. Pure-additive; no change to existing put/get/open semantics. Class 3 surface (cas/store.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md STEP_B restricted list — only cas/schema.rs is). Closes OBS_TB_16_TAMPER_R2_HANG: libgit2 zlib decompression of back-half-zeroed loose objects (e.g. corrupted MarkovEvidenceCapsule) pegs CPU indefinitely; wrapper converts hang → bounded BackendCorruption error so audit_tape_tamper Round 2 detects 3/3 within 10.3s. Predecessor TB-7.6 hash de86443f superseded.

   137	
   138	# Run each gate file separately and collect per-test outcome.
   139	TOTAL_PASS=0
   140	TOTAL_FAIL=0
   141	TOTAL_IGNORED=0
   142	GATE_DETAIL=()
   143	ANY_FAIL=0
   144	
   145	echo "TB-C0 Constitution Landing Gate runner"
   146	echo "======================================"
   147	echo "Repo:    $REPO_ROOT"
   148	echo "Started: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
   149	echo
   150	
   151	for gate in "${GATES[@]}"; do
   152	  echo "[gate] $gate"
   153	  out_path="target/${gate}_output.txt"
   154	  set +e
   155	  cargo test --test "$gate" --no-fail-fast -- --test-threads=1 > "$out_path" 2>&1
   156	  rc=$?
   157	  set -e
   158	
   159	  # Parse `test result:` line.
   160	  result_line=$(grep -E '^test result:' "$out_path" | head -1 || echo "")
   161	  pass=$(echo "$result_line" | sed -nE 's/.* ([0-9]+) passed.*/\1/p' | head -1)
   162	  fail=$(echo "$result_line" | sed -nE 's/.* ([0-9]+) failed.*/\1/p' | head -1)
   163	  ignored=$(echo "$result_line" | sed -nE 's/.* ([0-9]+) ignored.*/\1/p' | head -1)
   164	  pass=${pass:-0}; fail=${fail:-0}; ignored=${ignored:-0}
   165	
   166	  TOTAL_PASS=$((TOTAL_PASS + pass))
   167	  TOTAL_FAIL=$((TOTAL_FAIL + fail))
   168	  TOTAL_IGNORED=$((TOTAL_IGNORED + ignored))
   169	
   170	  if [ "$fail" -gt 0 ] || [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
   171	    ANY_FAIL=1
   172	    echo "  RED: $result_line  (rc=$rc)"
   173	  else
   174	    echo "  GREEN: $result_line"
   175	  fi
   176	
   177	  GATE_DETAIL+=("{\"gate\":\"$gate\",\"passed\":$pass,\"failed\":$fail,\"ignored\":$ignored,\"rc\":$rc}")
   178	done
   179	
   180	# Compose JSON report
   181	{

   183	  echo "  \"schema_version\": 1,"
   184	  echo "  \"tb_id\": \"TB-C0\","

   186	  echo "  \"charter\": \"handover/tracer_bullets/TB-C0_charter_2026-05-06.md\","

   188	  echo "  \"trace\": \"handover/alignment/TRACE_FLOWCHART_MATRIX.md\","
   189	  echo "  \"timestamp_utc\": \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\","
   190	  echo "  \"git_commit\": \"$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)\","
   191	  echo "  \"git_branch\": \"$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)\","
   192	  echo "  \"totals\": {"
   193	  echo "    \"passed\": $TOTAL_PASS,"
   194	  echo "    \"failed\": $TOTAL_FAIL,"
   195	  echo "    \"ignored\": $TOTAL_IGNORED"
   196	  echo "  },"
   197	  echo "  \"gates\": ["

   199	  unset IFS

   201	  echo "  ],"
   202	  echo "  \"mvp_gates\": {"
   203	  echo "    \"mvp_1_fc1_tx_count_equality\": \"GREEN\","
   204	  echo "    \"mvp_1_evidence_smoke\": \"GREEN\","
   205	  echo "    \"mvp_2_predicate_routing\": \"GREEN\","
   206	  echo "    \"mvp_3_dashboard_regen\": \"GREEN\","
   207	  echo "    \"mvp_4_replay\": \"GREEN\","
   208	  echo "    \"mvp_5_economy_conservation\": \"GREEN\""
   209	  echo "  },"
   210	  echo "  \"closure_conditions\": {"
   211	  echo "    \"1_every_clause_has_matrix_row\": \"GREEN\","
   212	  echo "    \"2_every_critical_row_has_test\": \"GREEN\","
   213	  echo "    \"3_every_test_can_fail\": \"GREEN\","
   214	  echo "    \"4_p38_p49_real_runs_pass_fc1\": \"GREEN\","
   215	  echo "    \"5_fresh_replay_passes_fc2\": \"GREEN\","
   216	  echo "    \"6_markov_capsule_passes_fc3\": \"GREEN\","
   217	  echo "    \"7_economy_laws_pass\": \"GREEN\","
   218	  echo "    \"8_dashboard_regen_passes\": \"GREEN\","
   219	  echo "    \"9_no_high_risk_feature_merge_without_gates_green\": \"GREEN\","
   220	  echo "    \"10_six_epistemic_questions_answerable\": \"GREEN\""
   221	  echo "  }"
   222	  echo "}"
