handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:1:# CODEX Stage A3 HEAD_t C2 Charter Ratification Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:7:- target: `handover/tracer_bullets/STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md`
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:8:- gate: Gate 1 pre-R1 strict charter ratification for Stage A3 HEAD_t C2
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:11:- authority: `handover/directives/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUTONOMOUS_EXECUTION_AUTHORIZATION.md` §3.1 authorizes Stage A3 charter drafting and says STEP_B execution requires per-atom architect sign-off going forward (lines 115-122)
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:15:- `handover/tracer_bullets/STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md` - 163 lines, read in full.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:18:- `handover/architect-insights/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUDIT_LAUNCH_POLYMARKET_MANUAL_zh.md` - targeted §3 Stage A3 cross-check.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:19:- `handover/architect-insights/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUDIT_LAUNCH_POLYMARKET_MANUAL_en.md` - targeted §4 / Stage A3 equivalent cross-checks.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:30:Reasoning: R1 is correctly treated as Class 4 in substance because the parent authorization classifies "A3 / HEAD_t C2 multi-ref ChainTape" as "Class 4 STEP_B (ledger surface)" and limits it to charter draft authorization pending per-atom sign-off (`handover/directives/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUTONOMOUS_EXECUTION_AUTHORIZATION.md:115-122`). The charter repeats that classification for "ref naming + Git2-backed multi-ref ledger writer" (charter lines 20-23) and binds R1 to `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` as "4 STEP_B" (charter lines 103-107). Existing source confirms the current production writer is a single-ref writer on `refs/transitions/main`: its section title names that ref, `TRANSITIONS_REF` is `refs/transitions/main`, and commits update `Some(TRANSITIONS_REF)` (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:643-677`, `:838-848`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:32:Challenge: the charter overstates the specific CLAUDE.md §12 basis. CLAUDE.md's STEP_B list names `src/kernel.rs`, `src/bus.rs`, `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs`, `src/bottom_white/cas/schema.rs`, and "canonical signing payload surfaces" (`CLAUDE.md:604-614`); it does not explicitly list `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`. The charter quote "Schema (`refs/chaintape/{l4,l4e,cas}` ref naming + Git2-backed multi-ref ledger writer) = **Class 4 STEP_B** on `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` + adjacent" (charter lines 20-22) is defensible via parent authority, but not "per CLAUDE.md §12 STEP_B list" alone. R2 is cleanly Class 3 if it remains an additive constructor, as the charter says "existing one preserved" (charter line 107) and FR preserves the existing `&QState` constructor (charter lines 64-65). R3 is not clean: the prompt describes R3 as Class 3, and the charter labels "CAS root ref advance hook on CAS write batch" Class 3 (charter line 108), but FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.2 requires every CAS object write to update `refs/chaintape/cas` (charter line 63), which is part of the same three-ref canonical pointer surface declared Class 4 at charter lines 20-23.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:40:Reasoning: The charter fully names the three target refs in §1 and FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1: `refs/chaintape/l4`, `refs/chaintape/l4e`, and `refs/chaintape/cas` (charter lines 36-38, 62). This matches CLAUDE.md's C2 production path exactly (`CLAUDE.md:222-229`) and the alignment docs' Stage A3 refs (`handover/architect-insights/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUDIT_LAUNCH_POLYMARKET_MANUAL_zh.md:183-207`; `..._en.md:147-158`). The charter also specifies what advances each ref: accepted L4, rejected L4.E, and CAS object writes (charter lines 47-52, 63).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:42:Challenge: the migration and CAS-root commit format are too loose for a charter intended to drive deterministic replay. The challenged migration quote is: "`refs/transitions/main` is migrated to `refs/chaintape/l4` (dual-write during migration window allowed; hard cutover on Stage A3 HEAD_t C2 ship)" (charter line 62). It does not define when dual-write begins, whether both refs must point to byte-identical commits, what happens on divergence, or which ref is authoritative during the window. The challenged CAS quote is: "CAS root commit per write batch; commit message references CAS object CIDs" (charter line 63). Existing C1 intentionally keeps the canonical record in tree blobs because "git normalizes message bytes in ways that break round-trip" (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:647-659`). A CAS root whose determinism depends on commit-message formatting rather than canonical tree content is therefore underspecified.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:50:Reasoning: FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 is falsifiable at a high level: "Replay MUST produce a HEAD_t identical (six-field byte equality) to the original run's HEAD_t" and a fresh replay "MUST end at the same OIDs on all three refs" (charter line 65). SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 names a concrete new test for byte equality (charter line 93). Existing C1 `HeadTWitness` has a deterministic six-field canonical hash over fixed field order (source lines 48-63, 94-123), and existing `Git2LedgerWriter` already avoids wall clock by using logical time and fixed author identity (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:825-829`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:60:Reasoning: The rule is present in both functional and constitutional form. FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 says: "NO filesystem-side global pointer (e.g., `LATEST_HEAD_T.txt` / `CURRENT_RUN.json` / similar). The three Git refs ARE the canonical pointer" (charter line 66). CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 repeats: "NO new global filesystem pointer. The three named Git refs are the pointer" (charter line 79). SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 names a binary check: "No hidden filesystem pointer (grep + replay-without-fs-state)" with a concrete new test file (charter line 94). This aligns with CLAUDE.md's "global latest pointer" exclusion from valid evidence (`CLAUDE.md:85-108`) and FC2/FC3 prohibitions (`CLAUDE.md:162-188`), and with the English alignment halt condition for global pointer source-of-truth (`..._en.md:265-280`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:62:Remediation: none required for G1. Optional hardening: list the grep deny-patterns and allowed false positives in the charter so SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5 is mechanically reproducible.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:68:Reasoning: The charter's no-schema-change claim is consistent with C1 source. §1 says Stage A3 "does NOT alter HeadTWitness public API" and is a storage-form refactor (charter lines 54-56). CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.6 says "NO change to HEAD_t six-field schema. C2 changes how the values are PERSISTED, not what they ARE" (charter line 80). Existing source defines exactly six fields: `state_root`, `l4_head`, `l4e_head`, `cas_root`, `economic_state_root`, `run_id` (`src/state/head_t_witness.rs:48-63`), and has a field-count test that requires exactly those six names (`src/state/head_t_witness.rs:152-177`). CLAUDE.md §4.1 pins the same six-field schema (`CLAUDE.md:198-229`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:70:Concern but not challenge: FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.3 adds a public `HeadTWitness::reconstruct_from_repo(repo: &git2::Repository)` constructor (charter line 64), so the statement "does NOT alter HeadTWitness public API" (charter line 55) is imprecise if "alter" includes additive public API. The atom table handles this better by calling R2 an "additive constructor; existing one preserved" (charter line 107).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:78:Reasoning: The no-retroactive-rewrite rule is explicit. FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.6 says existing C1 evidence directories remain replayable via a documented migration path and old runs are not rewritten (charter line 67). CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.3 repeats that pre-Stage A3 runs replay via adapter, not evidence editing (charter line 77). This aligns with constitution/CLAUDE prohibitions on retroactive reconstruction/rewrite (`constitution.md:60-65`; `CLAUDE.md:152-168`) and with the parent alignment's C1-to-C2 framing (`..._zh.md:83-86`; `..._en.md:109-118`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:80:Challenge: the charter does not identify which existing C1 evidence families are covered or where the documented adapter lives. The prompt specifically asks what happens to `handover/evidence/constitution_landing_phase3_*`; the charter only says "existing C1 evidence directories" generically (charter line 67) and never names `constitution_landing_phase3_*`. SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.9 says "OBS forward-binding for any C1 -> C2 migration edge case captured" (charter line 98), but that is not a migration-path specification and cannot tell an auditor whether a Phase 3 evidence directory is replayed through legacy `refs/transitions/main`, synthesized C2 refs, or an adapter view.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:84:### Q7 - SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1..10 Falsifiability
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:88:Reasoning: SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1 through .5 are binary and name concrete tests (charter lines 90-94). SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.6 and .7 name concrete commands and pass-count baselines (charter lines 95-96), consistent with charter preconditions that record 1181 workspace tests and 97 constitution gates (charter lines 137-143). SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.10 correctly places dual audit after substrate evidence, matching the user's G1-before / G2-after instruction and the charter's §8 final ship sequence (charter lines 99, 145-153).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:90:Challenge: SG-8 and SG-9 are not binary enough. SG-8 says: "One real-LLM smoke run (>=1 problem) on Stage A3 HEAD_t C2 substrate produces a 50/50-style invariant report under refs storage" (charter line 97). It does not define pass/fail fields, minimum required invariant rows, acceptable problem source, or whether a single problem must include accepted L4, rejected L4.E, and CAS advances. SG-9 says: "OBS forward-binding for any C1 -> C2 migration edge case captured" (charter line 98). "Any" is not mechanically enumerable and "captured" does not define required OBS contents or ownership. The charter also declares "Each gate is binary pass/fail" (charter line 86), so these two gate texts conflict with the charter's own gate standard.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:98:Reasoning: The charter includes a strong forbidden list against hidden Class-4 expansion: no typed-tx schema bump, no canonical signing payload change, no HEAD_t schema change, no sequencer public-API change beyond the ratified ledger writer adapter, and no agent-submittable system tx introduction (charter lines 124-132). It also states Stage A3 does not change typed_tx schema, sequencer admission semantics, or canonical signing payload (charter lines 54-56). This is consistent with CLAUDE.md's Class-4 and STEP_B rules for sequencer admission, typed tx schema, and canonical signing payload (`CLAUDE.md:508-515`, `:604-614`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:108:Reasoning: §1 scope is directionally consistent with §4 gates: C2 target requires L4, L4.E, and CAS refs plus replay from refs alone and no filesystem pointer (charter lines 34-56), and SG-A3-HEAD-T-C2.1..5 test those same properties (charter lines 84-99). §5 sequence is mostly consistent: R1 establishes the multi-ref writer before R2 replay constructor, R4 test gates, R5 smoke, R6 OBS, and R7 G2 dual audit (charter lines 101-112). §6 forbidden list is also aligned with §4: no dashboard source-of-truth supports replay/audit gates, no public chain matches local refs storage, and no typed-tx/signing/HEAD_t schema/invariant/evidence rewrite changes preserve the charter's "storage form refactor" scope (charter lines 114-135).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:110:Challenge: three internal inconsistencies remain. First, §5 puts the CAS root ref advance hook in R3 Class 3 (charter line 108), but §1/§2 make `refs/chaintape/cas` part of the canonical C2 pointer and multi-ref schema (charter lines 47-52, 62-63), while the header classifies that schema as Class 4 STEP_B (charter lines 20-23). Second, §4 SG-8/SG-9 are not binary enough despite §4's statement that each gate is binary pass/fail (charter lines 86-99). Third, §6 forbids "no FC1 hard invariant change" and CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.7 repeats the existing FC1 equation (charter lines 81, 129), but the charter does not map SG-8's "50/50-style invariant report" to the exact CLAUDE.md FC1 LHS definition (`CLAUDE.md:362-380`).
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:132:- STEP_B authority hypothesis: `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` is not literally in CLAUDE.md §12's STEP_B file list (`CLAUDE.md:604-614`), but the parent authorization explicitly classifies A3 as Class 4 STEP_B ledger surface (`handover/directives/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUTONOMOUS_EXECUTION_AUTHORIZATION.md:115-122`). This audit treats the parent authorization as sufficient authority if the charter text is clarified.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:25:**Mandate**: round-1 dual external audit on the **CO1.7-impl bundle** (A1 + A2 + A3 + A4) + **CO1.4-extra** atom, all mid-implementation commits. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + spec § 12: this PASS/PASS gates entry to CO1.7.5 (per-kind transition function bodies + STEP_B wiring to bus.rs/kernel.rs).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:63:**Q-E. A3 dispatch_transition exhaustive match contract**: every variant returns NotYetImplemented. Is this the right pattern for "transition body not yet implemented", or should the stubs use unimplemented!() macro for clearer crash semantics? (Tests verify the Err return path; CO1.7.5 fills bodies preserving the match arms.)

handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:62:The bundling of A1+A2+A3+A4 is highly defensible. These atoms represent the tightly-coupled components of the L4 ledger: storage (A1), control flow (A2), logic interface (A3), and verification (A4). Auditing them in isolation would be counterproductive, as their correctness is interdependent. For example, the `Sequencer`'s (A2) correctness depends on the invariants enforced by the `LedgerWriter` (A1), and the entire system's integrity is verified by `replay_full_transition` (A4). The bundle provided the necessary holistic context for a meaningful strategic review, enhancing rather than diluting audit focus. **PASS**.

CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_16_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-04_R3.md:6385:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:10391:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5713:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. handover/audits/CODEX_TB_16_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-04_R3.md:6409:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8t statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_16_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-04_R3.md:20778:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:11079:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5756:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_16_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-04_R3.md:20780:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:11098:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:5991:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6752:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_16_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-04_R3.md:20781:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:11107:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6014:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6855:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_16_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-04_R3.md:20810:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:11969:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8590:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6834:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5109:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/run_codex_co1_7_round2_audit.sh:54:**Q-A3** (C3 signing integration): Does `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` + `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning` extension correctly close DIV-1? handover/audits/GEMINI_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:1:# Gemini Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 Multi-Ref ChainTape Audit — R1
handover/audits/GEMINI_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:14:Q1: PASS - The `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` function correctly implements dual-write by first creating a commit and updating the C1 ref (`refs/transitions/main`), then using the exact same commit OID to update the new C2 ref (`refs/chaintape/l4`). This preserves the C1 ref as a backward-compatible alias. The changes are confined to the ref-writing logic and do not alter the `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` struct or its `canonical_digest` method, thus satisfying CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 "no change to canonical signing payload".
handover/audits/GEMINI_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:22:Q5: CHALLENGE - The 0/8 solve rate in the B3 R6 mini-M1 run is not a Stage A3 concern, as it relates to agent performance, not ledger substrate correctness. However, the evidence provided for all smoke runs (R5, R3.5, B3 R6) fails to include the "50/50-style invariant report" (i.e., the `chain_invariant.json` file or its equivalent) explicitly required by ship gate SG-A3.8. The gate requires that the run *produces* this report, and the evidence must contain it. Without this evidence, the gate is not met.
handover/audits/GEMINI_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:24:Q6: PASS - The grep-based test `sg_a3_no_hidden_filesystem_pointer` is a sufficient, if not exhaustive, check. While it could miss pointers constructed at runtime or hidden in dependencies, the primary guarantee against hidden pointers is architectural: the system is designed to be replayable from the git refs alone. This is functionally verified by SG-A3.4 (`sg_a3_replay_reconstructs_head_t_from_refs`), which successfully reconstructs the `HeadTWitness` from only the repository path. The grep test serves as a good defense-in-depth against obvious regressions in the source code.
handover/audits/GEMINI_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md:37:- **Q5:** Fulfill ship gate SG-A3.8 by re-running at least one smoke problem and capturing the `chain_invariant.json` report in the evidence package, demonstrating the core system invariant holds on the new substrate.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5052:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5056:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5086:## § 2. New code symbols (Phase A0–A3)

A1 (PREREG amendment) → 25; A3 (agent_models.rs) → 26; A5 (budget_regime.rs) → 27; A6 (fc_trace.rs) → 28; A7 (llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py) → 31; A8e (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py) → 33. When B7-extra calibration eventually runs, the calibration jsonl makes 34 entries; future Phase C's `--mode` flag binary (TBD location) makes 35.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7105:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7111:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:76:**Total: 24 entries** (15 from B7 + 1 B7-extra rollback_sim + 4 dual-audit fixes + 4 A0 harness). A1 (PREREG amendment) → 25; A3 (agent_models.rs) → 26; A5 (budget_regime.rs) → 27; A6 (fc_trace.rs) → 28; A7 (llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py) → 31; A8e (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py) → 33. When B7-extra calibration eventually runs, the calibration jsonl makes 34 entries; future Phase C's `--mode` flag binary (TBD location) makes 35.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:5523:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:6827:    21	- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R11.md:6830:    24	- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/G2_TB_18R_ROUND_3_DUAL_AUDIT_DISPATCH_2026-05-06.md:65:### Q-A3 — AttemptOutcome::PartialAccepted = 6 tail-add
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:4309:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:255:- **F5** Trust Root count + genesis_payload.toml header reconciled to 31 entries (corrected for A3's `agent_models.rs` already in TR before A1). Closes #4.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:4422:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5400:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:4428:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5411:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:4443:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5712:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:9:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2309:**Q-A3** (C3 signing integration): Does `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` + `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning` extension correctly close DIV-1? Verify: (a) the canonical_digest method binds the right 9 fields with deterministic byte layout; (b) the sign API extension is additive (Wave 4-B not breaking); (c) the forward-compat clause for future ledger-side variants is sound.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5595:- A3: external attacker with read access to runtime host filesystem — can attempt to extract keypair from disk
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5602:- A3 extracting key from rest disk → **defended by encrypted-at-rest with user-derived KDF**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:9670:    23	- A3: external attacker with read access to runtime host filesystem — can attempt to extract keypair from disk
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:9677:    30	- A3 extracting key from rest disk → **defended by encrypted-at-rest with user-derived KDF**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:14785:- **Q-A3 C3 signing integration: PARTIAL.** The signing payload digest is structurally right, but shipped [system_keypair.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:188) still has only 3 `CanonicalMessage` variants and no `sign_ledger_entry`; skeleton also says this is deferred in [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:25).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:401:- **Total atom budget**: ~1,000-1,560 LoC (down from initial 1,200-1,900 because Bus stays struct-shape-compatible after D3 simplification). **Estimated calendar time**: 4-7 days. Implementation may bundle as `CO1.7.5-impl A1+A2+A3+A4` like CO1.7-impl, OR ship as 4 sequential commits.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1202:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |

handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:15:Aggregate verdict: VETO. The 8-question count is 1 PASS / 7 CHALLENGE / 0 VETO, but the sanity check is VETO because the charter downgrades Stage A3 HEAD_t C2 from the Stage A green hard gate into a recommendation before P-M2 STEP_B. That is not ratifiable for a Class-4 market stage that will add typed-tx/sequencer/CAS state and then rely on replayable G2 evidence. The prerequisite source claims checked out: `CompleteSetMintTx`, `CompleteSetRedeemTx`, and `MarketSeedTx` exist in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1171`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1202`, and `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1233`, with `TypedTx` variants at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1542-1544`; the legacy CPMM fence guards imports at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:435-464` and asserts `src/prediction_market.rs` absence at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:573-584`; and `price_never_overrides_predicate` exists at `tests/constitution_predicate_gate.rs:169-205`.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:111:Reasoning: §1 scope, §4 CRs, §6 forbidden list, and §7 atom sequence are directionally coherent, but §2 has a hard-gate defect. The charter says Stage A green is satisfied by TB-18R Final, AMBER closure progress, and HEAD_t C1, while HEAD_t C2 is only "RECOMMENDED before P-M2 STEP_B work" at `handover/tracer_bullets/STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_PM0_PM9_charter_2026-05-07.md:65-68`. The parent authorization says Stage C is gated on Stage A green and Stage B1 green at `handover/directives/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUTONOMOUS_EXECUTION_AUTHORIZATION.md:131-135`; Stage A includes A3 HEAD_t C2 as a Class-4 STEP_B item at lines 115-122. The architect manual says C2 remains future at `handover/architect-insights/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUDIT_LAUNCH_POLYMARKET_MANUAL_en.md:147-154` and defines A3 required refs and replay gates at lines 380-402. The A3 charter states C1 has L4 commits but L4.E and CAS roots are still in-memory at `handover/tracer_bullets/STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md:40-45`, while C2 requires L4/L4.E/CAS refs and replay reconstruction from refs alone at lines 47-68. Running P-M2 STEP_B on C1 and migrating to C2 mid-Stage-C would make P-M2/P-M6/P-M9 evidence depend on a pre-migration substrate, exactly where SG-StageC-PM.7 says G2 happens after substrate green at charter line 199. For a Class-4 market stage, "recommended" is not sufficient.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:113:Remediation: Make Stage A3 HEAD_t C2 FINAL GREEN a hard precondition before any P-M2/P-M4/P-M6 STEP_B and before P-M9 evidence. If the architect wants P-M0/P-M1 preparatory work before C2, carve that out explicitly as non-Class-4, non-external, non-final preparation and block Class-4 atoms until C2 sign-off.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:119:The 8-question audit found no missing prerequisite source objects and no direct per-question VETO, but the sanity check is a hard gate failure: the charter weakens Stage A3 HEAD_t C2 from a Stage A green requirement into a recommendation before Class-4 market work. With seven CHALLENGEs and a sanity-check VETO, this charter should not be treated as G1-ratified for execution. After the hard-gate and text remediations below, the core Stage C design is recoverable without source changes.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:121:VETO blocker quote: charter §2 says "HEAD_t C2 (Stage A3) RECOMMENDED before P-M2 STEP_B work" at `handover/tracer_bullets/STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_PM0_PM9_charter_2026-05-07.md:67`, while parent authorization gates Stage C on Stage A green at `handover/directives/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUTONOMOUS_EXECUTION_AUTHORIZATION.md:131-135`.
handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:125:1. Change §2 line 67 from "HEAD_t C2 (Stage A3) RECOMMENDED before P-M2 STEP_B work to avoid ledger storage-form change mid-feature" to "HEAD_t C2 (Stage A3) FINAL GREEN is HARD-required before any P-M2/P-M4/P-M6 STEP_B work and before P-M9 evidence; P-M0/P-M1 preparatory tests may proceed only if they do not touch Class-4 surfaces or claim Stage C ship progress."

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5449:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5450:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5459:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5462:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5492:## § 2. New code symbols (Phase A0–A3)

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7336:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7710:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6749:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7339:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7713:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6752:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7600:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8213:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5401:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7621:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8234:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5720:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7624:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8237:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5723:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7943:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8674:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5269:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8590:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6834:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5109:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8599:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6843:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5173:**Total: 24 entries** (15 from B7 + 1 B7-extra rollback_sim + 4 dual-audit fixes + 4 A0 harness). A1 (PREREG amendment) → 25; A3 (agent_models.rs) → 26; A5 (budget_regime.rs) → 27; A6 (fc_trace.rs) → 28; A7 (llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py) → 31; A8e (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py) → 33; A8e2 (llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs) → 34. When B7-extra calibration eventually runs, the calibration jsonl makes 35 entries; future Phase C's `--mode` flag binary (TBD location) makes 36.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8607:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:6851:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5304:# Total: 34 files. Progression A0=24 → A1=25 (PREREG amendment) → A3=26

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12857:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

handover/audits/run_codex_stage_a3_r7_audit_2026-05-08.sh:74:Q7. Stage A3 substrate intersects with rejection_evidence.rs (Class-3 per `feedback_class4_cannot_hide_in_class3` analysis: "rejection_evidence.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md §12 STEP_B restricted list"). Verify: does the A3 R3.5 change to flush_jsonl_record cross any Class-4 line? In particular: does it modify RejectedSubmissionRecord schema, compute_hash byte stream, or sequencer admission semantics? If yes → VETO.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5426:- A4 (post-A3): decomposed metrics — `hit_max_tx`, `tactic_diversity`, `verifier_wait_ms` added as non-Optional v2 fields + `compute_tactic_diversity` helper; per-row decomposition of `solve_rate` / `tokens_per_solve` / `time_per_solve` (all derivable from existing `progress` / `total_run_token_count` / `total_wall_time_ms`). FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition for `hit_max_tx`) + FC1-N11 (∏p decision diversity for `tactic_diversity`) + FC1-N12 (oracle scope for `verifier_wait_ms`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5427:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5435:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5438:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
