handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7710:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6749:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7713:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6752:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7726:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6852:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:13:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8213:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5401:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8237:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5723:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8674:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5269:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9400:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5056:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9519:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5739:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5056:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation in CI without a live API. **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9525:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5745:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5119:**Total: 24 entries** (15 from B7 + 1 B7-extra rollback_sim + 4 dual-audit fixes + 4 A0 harness). A1 (PREREG amendment) → 25; A3 (agent_models.rs) → 26; A5 (budget_regime.rs) → 27; A6 (fc_trace.rs) → 28; A7 (llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py) → 31; A8e (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py) → 33. When B7-extra calibration eventually runs, the calibration jsonl makes 34 entries; future Phase C's `--mode` flag binary (TBD location) makes 35.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9910:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9335:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:10:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer § 3 condition 3 satisfied: `MaxTxExhausted` rows now disambiguated across N values. FC-trace: FC2-N22 (HALT decomposition by budget regime) + FC1-N7 (δ instances determining the per-agent share under PerAgent regime). Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:9914:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9339:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:14:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10923:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5427:- A5 (post-A4): per-agent budget normalization — new `budget_regime` module (`BUDGET_REGIME` + `MAX_TRANSACTIONS` env vars; 4-variant enum; pure parser + scaler + env-coupled resolver); `budget_regime` + `budget_max_transactions` added as non-Optional v2 fields on `RunAggregate` and the legacy `PputResult`; loop bound at `run_swarm` switched from hardcoded `let max_transactions = 200` to `resolve_budget(n_agents)` — default (env unset) preserves Phase B baseline (`total_proposal × 200`) bit-for-bit. Trust Root manifest 26 → 27 (corrected A8e5 J4 — the A1 PREREG amendment had taken the count to 25, A3's `agent_models.rs` to 26 before A5 landed; round-1 packet undercounted by 1).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10930:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5435:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:10933:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5438:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:91:**Total: 24 entries** (15 from B7 + 1 B7-extra rollback_sim + 4 dual-audit fixes + 4 A0 harness). A1 (PREREG amendment) → 25; A3 (agent_models.rs) → 26; A5 (budget_regime.rs) → 27; A6 (fc_trace.rs) → 28; A7 (llm_proxy.py + smoke_siliconflow.sh + _smoke_siliconflow.py) → 31; A8e (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py) → 33; A8e2 (llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs) → 34; A8e7 (A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md append-only chronology) → 35; A8e11 (audit runner scripts run_codex/run_gemini per C-075 gate-machinery rule) → 37; A8e13 (src/boot.rs added — verifier file must be qualifiable per C-075 + Codex R11#1) → 38; C-pre1 (PPUT_CCL_HARD10_2026-04-26.json + draw_hard10_pput_ccl.py — Phase C ablation sample basis per PREREG § 6 C2; tampering = Phase C respin per C-075) → 40; C1a (experiment_mode.rs — Phase C `--mode` CLI flag + 5-mode resolver + UnimplementedMode startup-fatal gate; tampering with `ensure_implemented` could let `--mode=soft_law` silently fall back to Full and corrupt Phase C ablation) → 41; C2 runner (run_c2_phase_c_ablation.sh — Phase C batch runner per C-075 DO-178C; tampering with the cell ordering, timeout, or synthetic-failure-row policy could silently corrupt the 100-row ablation evidence collection) → 42. When B7-extra calibration eventually runs, its jsonl + p_0 source toml will follow.

handover/alignment/OBS_STAGE_A3_R7_GEMINI_R1_FORWARD_BIND_2026-05-08.md:47:**Forward-bind**: Stage A3.6 enhancement TB will refactor `RejectionEvidenceWriter` to accept `chaintape_repo_path: Option<PathBuf>` as an explicit ctor field. The env-var hook can remain as a thin convenience wrapper that calls the explicit ctor.
handover/alignment/OBS_STAGE_A3_R7_GEMINI_R1_FORWARD_BIND_2026-05-08.md:59:**Counter-argument (GC)**: The TuringOS CAS git repo is per-run (per-problem) and lives at `cas_path` which is NOT subject to `git gc` in normal operation. The CAS `.turingos_cas_index.jsonl` sidecar is the durable index; the ref is a derived view per FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5. Per `feedback_evidence_packaging_policy_required` the repo is local-only artifact, not a long-lived production repo.
handover/alignment/OBS_STAGE_A3_R7_GEMINI_R1_FORWARD_BIND_2026-05-08.md:61:However, Gemini's concern is valid for FORWARD architectural robustness: if the C2 ref scheme is intended as canonical pointer per CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5, it should be a proper commit chain, not a blob-OID pointer. This is the architecturally clean design.

./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md:8:Evidence: `EconomicState` adds `node_positions_t` as a serde-defaulted flat index, with comments saying it is not a Coin holding and is not counted in `total_supply_micro` (`src/state/q_state.rs:176`, `src/state/q_state.rs:179`, `src/state/q_state.rs:191`). The monetary sum in `total_supply_micro` iterates balances, escrows, stakes, and challenge cases only (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:154`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:157`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:160`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:169`), and there is no `node_positions_t` term in that function (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:152`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:172`).
./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_12_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md:31:Challenge: the audit prompt required confirming exactly 5 holdings: balances, escrows, stakes, claims-active, and challenge cases. The implementation currently enumerates 4 holdings: balances, escrows, stakes, and challenge cases (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:154`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:157`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:160`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:169`). It intentionally omits `claims_t` as intent metadata (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:163`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:165`), and its unit test is named `ctf_counts_all_four_holding_subindexes` with expected sum excluding claims (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:582`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:629`). SG-12.4 does not create an active claim, so it does not cover the requested "claims-active" term (`tests/tb_12_node_exposure_index.rs:306`, `tests/tb_12_node_exposure_index.rs:317`).

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:12100:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12870:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:62:- **Statistical framing**: `0.10` is the **least-strict admissible value** the PREREG ceiling allows. `j-RR ≤ p_0` makes a SMALLER `p_0` stricter, so this substitution is operationally permitted but is NOT a tighter-than-original guarantee. No Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive (PREREG § 5.4), outside the inferential family. The substitution is operationally permitted at any phase including Phase E; if/when § 3 re-calibration conditions complete, calibration upgrades the bar.
./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:12101:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12871:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:143:- (Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording: is `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` § 2 + § 8 internally consistent + statistically correct (least-strict admissible; no Type-I inflation; substitution operative at any phase including Phase E)?

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:13503:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1541:2. ~~**TRACE_MATRIX of B7-extra (p_0 calibration toggle)**~~ — landed. Final implementation differs slightly from the original sketch in this section: the constitutional `bus.register_predicate(...)` API does not currently exist on `main` (it lives on the unmerged `phase-z-wtool-tools` branch — TRACE_MATRIX_v0 row FC1-N11 references it aspirationally). Rather than scope-creep B7-extra into reviving Phase Z, the synthetic predicate is implemented at the evaluator layer in `rollback_sim.rs` with an explicit short-circuit at the threshold tx. The constitutional anchor (FC1-E18 ∏p=0 → Q_t repeated, then FC2-N22 HALT via existing `MaxTxExhausted`) is unchanged; only the abstraction depth differs. Listed under § 2 above as ✅ entries.

The implementation explicitly short-circuits on any non-empty exemption list: `if !exempt_tx_kinds.is_empty() { return Ok(()); }` (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:177`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:179`). Current `src/` production call sites outside the function are absent; observed non-empty usage is a unit test named `ctf_exempt_short_circuits` (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:320`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:328`). TB-1 integration calls pass `&[]` (`tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:355`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:382`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:445`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:540`). ./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_16_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-04_R3.md:20791:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:11287:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R6.md:9979:    17	- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

P0-1: the field is exactly `Arc<RwLock<RejectionEvidenceWriter>>` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:444`, matching `append_rejected(&mut self)` at `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`. P0-2: `SYSTEM_AGENT_ID_STR` exists at `src/state/sequencer.rs:100`, and the rejection path uses `submitter_id().unwrap_or_else(...)` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:634`. P0-3: stake uses `micro_units()` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:185`, and the bridge uses `.escrows_t.0.contains_key` / `.task_markets_t.0.contains_key` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:198`; the newtypes are tuple wrappers at `src/state/q_state.rs:161` and `src/state/q_state.rs:224`. P0-4: `StaleParent` is reused at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:720`, only `EscrowMissing` and `MonetaryInvariantViolation` are added at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:788` and `src/state/typed_tx.rs:792`, and Display has exactly those new arms at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:825`. P0-5: integration tests use constructor injection and retained `Arc<RwLock<...>>` at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:142` and `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:154`; no `rejection_writer_for_test` accessor was found. P0-6: rejection CAS puts take a write lock and call the 5-arg `put` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:597` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:615`, matching the signature at `src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:163`.

Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:4602:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12871:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:143:- (Q2.a) `p_0 = 0.10` substitution wording: is `PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md` § 2 + § 8 internally consistent + statistically correct (least-strict admissible; no Type-I inflation; substitution operative at any phase including Phase E)?
./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:4603:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12872:handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:144:- (Q2.b) Re-calibration conditions in § 3 are PRE-REQUISITES for calibration to run, not guarantees that calibration completes before any specific phase. Is this framed correctly without claiming calibration MUST run before Phase E?
./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R5.md:4848:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:17:**Q6 [PASS].** The `exempt_tx_kinds` red line is technically justified. The implementation explicitly short-circuits on any non-empty exemption list: `if !exempt_tx_kinds.is_empty() { return Ok(()); }` (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:177`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:179`). The function docs say mints/burns are rejected unless the list is non-empty and normal agent-submitted transitions should pass `&[]` (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:154`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:166`). Current `src/` production call sites outside the function are absent; observed non-empty usage is a unit test named `ctf_exempt_short_circuits` (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:320`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:328`). TB-1 integration calls pass `&[]` (`tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:355`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:382`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:445`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:540`). Verdict: this red line prevents future regression, not existing runtime misuse.

The monetary guards are not wired into a real dispatch/settlement path. `rg` finds `assert_read_is_free`, `assert_total_ctf_conserved`, and `assert_no_post_init_mint` only in their module tests and `tb_1_acceptance`. `assert_no_post_init_mint` currently returns `Ok(())` for every K5 variant post-init because there is no Mint variant [src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:134](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:134). Landing point is TB-2/RSP-1 per the ignored AT-2/AT-4 comments [tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:470](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:470).
./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8093:Q6. The P6 downgrade is defensible only because it is explicitly non-blocking. T10/T12 are registered as ignored or empty harness stubs [tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:461](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:461), [tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:496](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:496). I verified `/tmp/tb1_day4_smoke_v2/run2.jsonl` has `solved=true` and `h_vppu=6.215891726697228`, and `cargo test -p minif2f_v4 --lib h_vppu_history` passed 9/9. That is useful evidence, not a canonical in-harness assertion.
./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8108:Escrow should either be promoted into Tier-A or explicitly documented as RSP-1-only. P6 evidence should move from `/tmp`/ignored stubs into a reproducible non-default harness when it becomes blocking. Add an integration test around the evaluator h_vppu wire site, not just the `HVppuHistory` API.

./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_R2_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:28:**R2-Q4 [CHALLENGE].** Current `TransitionError` is not `#[non_exhaustive]`; it is a normal public enum (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:716`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:717`). Existing variants are `StaleParent`, `SignatureInvalid`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StakeInsufficient`, `TargetWorkTxNotFound`, `TargetWorkTxNotVerifiable`, `ParentNotAcceptedYet`, `AcceptancePredicateFailed(PredicateId)`, `VerificationPredicateFailed(PredicateId)`, `SettlementPredicateFailed(PredicateId)`, `ChallengeWindowClosed`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `CounterexampleInsufficient`, `ToolNotInRegistry`, `ToolCreatorMismatch`, `ClaimNotFound`, `TaskNotFound`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, and `NotYetImplemented` (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:717`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:787`). Existing direct `TransitionError::` use sites are the seven dispatch stubs, two sequencer tests, and one replay test (`src/state/sequencer.rs:54`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:60`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:583`, `src/state/sequencer.rs:611`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:1287`). The exhaustive `Display` match must also be updated for any new variant (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:790`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:815`). v2's mapping table is not closed against today's enum: it uses `PredicateFailed` and `InvariantViolation`, neither of which exists or is among the three proposed additions; it proposes `StaleParentRoot` even though `StaleParent` already exists; and a no-wildcard match over only eight variants would omit most existing variants (`handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:222`, `handover/ai-direct/TB-2_SEQUENCER_RUNTIME_CLOSURE_2026-04-30.md:241`).

./handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:15:Aggregate verdict: VETO. The 8-question count is 1 PASS / 7 CHALLENGE / 0 VETO, but the sanity check is VETO because the charter downgrades Stage A3 HEAD_t C2 from the Stage A green hard gate into a recommendation before P-M2 STEP_B. That is not ratifiable for a Class-4 market stage that will add typed-tx/sequencer/CAS state and then rely on replayable G2 evidence. The prerequisite source claims checked out: `CompleteSetMintTx`, `CompleteSetRedeemTx`, and `MarketSeedTx` exist in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1171`, `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1202`, and `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1233`, with `TypedTx` variants at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1542-1544`; the legacy CPMM fence guards imports at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:435-464` and asserts `src/prediction_market.rs` absence at `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:573-584`; and `price_never_overrides_predicate` exists at `tests/constitution_predicate_gate.rs:169-205`.
./handover/audits/CODEX_STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_CHARTER_RATIFICATION_2026-05-07.md:111:Reasoning: §1 scope, §4 CRs, §6 forbidden list, and §7 atom sequence are directionally coherent, but §2 has a hard-gate defect. The charter says Stage A green is satisfied by TB-18R Final, AMBER closure progress, and HEAD_t C1, while HEAD_t C2 is only "RECOMMENDED before P-M2 STEP_B work" at `handover/tracer_bullets/STAGE_C_POLYMARKET_PM0_PM9_charter_2026-05-07.md:65-68`. The parent authorization says Stage C is gated on Stage A green and Stage B1 green at `handover/directives/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUTONOMOUS_EXECUTION_AUTHORIZATION.md:131-135`; Stage A includes A3 HEAD_t C2 as a Class-4 STEP_B item at lines 115-122. The architect manual says C2 remains future at `handover/architect-insights/2026-05-07_ARCHITECT_ALIGNMENT_AUDIT_LAUNCH_POLYMARKET_MANUAL_en.md:147-154` and defines A3 required refs and replay gates at lines 380-402. The A3 charter states C1 has L4 commits but L4.E and CAS roots are still in-memory at `handover/tracer_bullets/STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md:40-45`, while C2 requires L4/L4.E/CAS refs and replay reconstruction from refs alone at lines 47-68. Running P-M2 STEP_B on C1 and migrating to C2 mid-Stage-C would make P-M2/P-M6/P-M9 evidence depend on a pre-migration substrate, exactly where SG-StageC-PM.7 says G2 happens after substrate green at charter line 199. For a Class-4 market stage, "recommended" is not sufficient.
./handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V12_REAUDIT_2026-04-27.md:21:| Q7 MicroCoin P1 prerequisite | Prior Q7 required moving `MicroCoin(i64)`, `StakeMicroCoin`, and royalty rate arithmetic before CO1.7 and before serialized tx records. | v3.2-fix2 promotes CO P2.0a to CO1.0a. | PARTIAL | Prior: `handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:137-140`, and pre-CO-P1 amendment at `:160`. Plan adds CO1.0a and says it is promoted before other P1 atoms at `handover/architect-insights/CO_MEGA_PLAN_v3.2_2026-04-27.md:137-154`; atom counts show P1 +4 and P2 -4 at `:171-182`. Gap: the critical path visualization still omits CO1.0a from P1 and still lists CO P2.0a under P2 at `:191-226`. |

P0-1: the field is exactly `Arc<RwLock<RejectionEvidenceWriter>>` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:444`, matching `append_rejected(&mut self)` at `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs:258`. P0-2: `SYSTEM_AGENT_ID_STR` exists at `src/state/sequencer.rs:100`, and the rejection path uses `submitter_id().unwrap_or_else(...)` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:634`. P0-3: stake uses `micro_units()` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:185`, and the bridge uses `.escrows_t.0.contains_key` / `.task_markets_t.0.contains_key` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:198`; the newtypes are tuple wrappers at `src/state/q_state.rs:161` and `src/state/q_state.rs:224`. P0-4: `StaleParent` is reused at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:720`, only `EscrowMissing` and `MonetaryInvariantViolation` are added at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:788` and `src/state/typed_tx.rs:792`, and Display has exactly those new arms at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:825`. P0-5: integration tests use constructor injection and retained `Arc<RwLock<...>>` at `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:142` and `tests/tb_2_runtime_boundary.rs:154`; no `rejection_writer_for_test` accessor was found. P0-6: rejection CAS puts take a write lock and call the 5-arg `put` at `src/state/sequencer.rs:597` and `src/state/sequencer.rs:615`, matching the signature at `src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:163`.

./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3431:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1541:> [2026-04-25 架构师补充] **sudo 权限的精确范围**：人类 sudo 权限**仅且只**作用于 `constitution.md` 本身。Trust Root 清单中的其他载荷文件（如 kernel.rs / lean4_oracle.rs / 预注册 / 评分管线 / cases/* 等）属于 ArchitectAI 的合法升级范围 —— 见 V.1.2。系统因此采用两层防御：(i) **提案时**由 Veto-AI 校核违宪与否（V.1.3）；(ii) **运行时**由 Boot 的 SHA-256 manifest 保证"实际加载的字节 == 已 commit 的字节"。这两层与本节的 sudo 形成**三段守护**：宪法 sudo（少数）+ Veto-AI 提案闸（多数）+ Boot 整合性闸（全部）。

./handover/audits/CODEX_TB_2_PHASE0_AUDIT_2026-04-30.md:17:**Q6 [PASS].** The `exempt_tx_kinds` red line is technically justified. The implementation explicitly short-circuits on any non-empty exemption list: `if !exempt_tx_kinds.is_empty() { return Ok(()); }` (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:177`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:179`). The function docs say mints/burns are rejected unless the list is non-empty and normal agent-submitted transitions should pass `&[]` (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:154`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:166`). Current `src/` production call sites outside the function are absent; observed non-empty usage is a unit test named `ctf_exempt_short_circuits` (`src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:320`, `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:328`). TB-1 integration calls pass `&[]` (`tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:355`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:382`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:445`, `tests/tb_1_acceptance.rs:540`). Verdict: this red line prevents future regression, not existing runtime misuse.

./handover/evidence/tb_16_x_2_3_smoke_2026-05-05/P11_complete_set_redeem/README.md:93:1. **3 env-var parts vs. charter-spec'd 4**: charter §2 Atom 2.3 specified `<owner>:<event_id>:<outcome>:<share_amount>`. Implementation uses 3 (`<owner>:<outcome>:<share_units>`); event_id auto-derived from `task-{run_id}` because `run_id` contains a unix-ms timestamp minted at evaluator entry (run_id.rs:21) and is unpredictable from the smoke script. ./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:155:  cargo test --test "$gate" --no-fail-fast -- --test-threads=1 > "$out_path" 2>&1
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:159:  # Parse `test result:` line.
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:160:  result_line=$(grep -E '^test result:' "$out_path" | head -1 || echo "")
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:163:  ignored=$(echo "$result_line" | sed -nE 's/.* ([0-9]+) ignored.*/\1/p' | head -1)
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:164:  pass=${pass:-0}; fail=${fail:-0}; ignored=${ignored:-0}
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:168:  TOTAL_IGNORED=$((TOTAL_IGNORED + ignored))
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:177:  GATE_DETAIL+=("{\"gate\":\"$gate\",\"passed\":$pass,\"failed\":$fail,\"ignored\":$ignored,\"rc\":$rc}")
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:195:  echo "    \"ignored\": $TOTAL_IGNORED"
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:244:      grep -E "^test result:" "$out_path" | head -1
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:262:echo "Totals: $TOTAL_PASS passed, $TOTAL_FAIL failed, $TOTAL_IGNORED ignored"
./scripts/run_constitution_gates.sh:265:  echo "FAIL: at least one gate is RED — block merge per TB-C0 CR-C0.10."

# Gemini Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 Multi-Ref ChainTape Audit — R1

**Round**: R1
**Date**: 2026-05-08
**Model**: gemini-2.5-pro
**Elapsed**: 89.6s
**Prompt size**: 251,068 chars
**Final aggregate verdict**: CHALLENGE

---

## Verbatim Gemini Response

Q1: PASS - The `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` function correctly implements dual-write by first creating a commit and updating the C1 ref (`refs/transitions/main`), then using the exact same commit OID to update the new C2 ref (`refs/chaintape/l4`). This preserves the C1 ref as a backward-compatible alias. The changes are confined to the ref-writing logic and do not alter the `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` struct or its `canonical_digest` method, thus satisfying CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.4 "no change to canonical signing payload".

Q2: CHALLENGE - The `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH` environment variable is an architecturally weak seam for hooking the L4.E writer to the git repo. It introduces implicit, global state ("action at a distance") which is brittle, hard to test reliably in parallel, and fails in scenarios with multiple concurrent TuringOS instances within a single process. A concrete failure mode is a race condition where two instances corrupt each other's `refs/chaintape/l4e` ref because they both read the same global env var. An explicit `chaintape_repo_path: Option<PathBuf>` field on the `RejectionEvidenceWriter` struct would be a more robust and explicit design.

Q3: PASS - The `serde_json::to_string` serialization of the `JsonlRecord` struct is byte-stable. The struct is composed of primitives and types like `String` and `Hash` that have a stable serialization order. Crucially, it does not contain any `HashMap` or other collections with unstable iteration order. As the git commit's tree content is derived directly from these stable JSON bytes and the commit time is derived deterministically from `submit_id`, the resulting commit OID is byte-stable across versions.

Q4: PASS - The derivation of `cas_root` by SHA-256 hashing the git OID is an appropriate cross-format mapping. The `HeadTWitness` schema requires a 32-byte `Hash`, while git OIDs are typically 20-byte SHA-1 hashes. Hashing with a domain separator is a standard, deterministic way to map an identifier into a fixed-size slot. While this mapping is not reversible, reversibility is not required for a witness field; the canonical pointer is the git ref itself. This approach is more robust to future changes (e.g., git using SHA-256 OIDs) than a padding/truncation scheme.

Q5: CHALLENGE - The 0/8 solve rate in the B3 R6 mini-M1 run is not a Stage A3 concern, as it relates to agent performance, not ledger substrate correctness. However, the evidence provided for all smoke runs (R5, R3.5, B3 R6) fails to include the "50/50-style invariant report" (i.e., the `chain_invariant.json` file or its equivalent) explicitly required by ship gate SG-A3.8. The gate requires that the run *produces* this report, and the evidence must contain it. Without this evidence, the gate is not met.

Q6: PASS - The grep-based test `sg_a3_no_hidden_filesystem_pointer` is a sufficient, if not exhaustive, check. While it could miss pointers constructed at runtime or hidden in dependencies, the primary guarantee against hidden pointers is architectural: the system is designed to be replayable from the git refs alone. This is functionally verified by SG-A3.4 (`sg_a3_replay_reconstructs_head_t_from_refs`), which successfully reconstructs the `HeadTWitness` from only the repository path. The grep test serves as a good defense-in-depth against obvious regressions in the source code.

Q7: PASS - The 8 real MiniF2F problems in the B3 R6 mini-M1 run provide sufficient coverage to validate this specific Class-4 change. The change affects the ledger storage mechanism, not the problem-solving logic. The 8 runs generated 16 L4 commits, 83 L4.E commits, and 410 CAS objects, exercising all three ref-advancement paths under real-LLM load. This is adequate to demonstrate the mechanical correctness of the new substrate. Demanding 450 runs would be disproportionate, as the marginal value for detecting a storage-layer bug is low.

Q8: CHALLENGE - The substrate has two visible strategic risks that would be exposed by larger-scale benchmarks.
1. **Concurrency Unsafe:** The `CasStore::put` hook updates `refs/chaintape/cas` in a non-atomic, race-prone way. If two processes write to CAS concurrently, one's ref update will be lost, leading to a stale `cas_root` pointer. The code explicitly ignores the error from the underlying `git2::reference` call, hiding the failure.
2. **GC Vulnerability:** The `refs/chaintape/cas` ref points directly to the latest blob OID, leaving all historical CAS blobs unreferenced. A standard `git gc` would garbage-collect and delete this historical data, breaking the immutability of the tape and making past states non-replayable. The ref should be a proper commit chain to anchor the full history.

## VERDICT: CHALLENGE
Conviction: high
Recommendation: FIX-THEN-PROCEED
Remediations:
- **Q2:** Refactor `RejectionEvidenceWriter` to accept the `chaintape_repo` path as an explicit constructor argument, removing the dependency on the `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH` environment variable.
- **Q5:** Fulfill ship gate SG-A3.8 by re-running at least one smoke problem and capturing the `chain_invariant.json` report in the evidence package, demonstrating the core system invariant holds on the new substrate.
- **Q8 (GC Vulnerability):** Redesign the `refs/chaintape/cas` update mechanism. Instead of having the ref point to a raw blob OID, each `CasStore::put` (or batch of puts) must create a new git *commit*. This commit's tree should reference the new CAS object(s), and its parent must be the previous commit on the `refs/chaintape/cas` ref, forming a proper, GC-safe chain.
- **Q8 (Concurrency):** Once the `refs/chaintape/cas` is a proper commit chain, the update logic in `CasStore::put` must be made concurrency-safe, for example by using a file-based lock or a compare-and-swap retry loop on the ref update to prevent lost updates from parallel writers.

# OBS Stage A3 R7 Gemini R1 Forward-Binding (2026-05-08)

**Authority**: `feedback_audit_loop_roi_flip` + `feedback_dual_audit_conflict` + STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md SG-A3.10.

**Audit verdict**: Gemini 2.5 Pro R1 = **CHALLENGE** at HEAD `381554f` (Stage A3 substrate + B3 R6 mini-M1 evidence). Aggregate verdict CHALLENGE because Q2/Q5/Q8 returned CHALLENGE; Q1/Q3/Q4/Q6/Q7 PASS.

**Disposition**: SHIP Stage A3 substrate + forward-bind Q2/Q8 remediations to a follow-up enhancement TB. Q5 closed in this commit.

---

## §1. Per-Q breakdown + disposition

| Q | Verdict | Topic | Disposition |
|---|---------|-------|-------------|
| Q1 | PASS | Dual-write semantics + signing-payload preservation | — |
| Q2 | **CHALLENGE** | env-var seam (`TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH`) on `flush_jsonl_record` is brittle vs explicit ctor arg | **forward-bind** to Stage A3.6 enhancement |
| Q3 | PASS | byte-stable JSONL → git2 commit determinism | — |
| Q4 | PASS | sha256-of-OID for cas_root cross-format mapping | — |
| Q5 | **CHALLENGE → CLOSED** | chain_invariant.json missing from smoke evidence | **CLOSED this commit** — regenerated 10/10 Ok delta=0 across A3 R5 + A3 R3.5 + B3 R6 mini-M1 |
| Q6 | PASS | grep-based no-fs-pointer test sufficient | — |
| Q7 | PASS | 8-problem real-MiniF2F coverage adequate for storage-layer change | — |
| Q8 | **CHALLENGE** | (a) refs/chaintape/cas points to blob OID directly (GC-vulnerable); (b) non-atomic ref updates race-prone | **forward-bind** to Stage A3.6 enhancement |

## §2. Q5 closure (this commit)

Gemini R1 Q5 verbatim: "the evidence provided for all smoke runs (R5, R3.5, B3 R6) fails to include the '50/50-style invariant report' (i.e., the chain_invariant.json file or its equivalent) explicitly required by ship gate SG-A3.8".

**Action**: regenerated chain_invariant.json for all 10 smoke evidence problem dirs using the existing `target/release/tb_18r_compute_invariant` binary against the per-problem runtime_repo + cas. Halt class derived from PPUT result (`hit_max_tx → MaxTxExhausted`; `solved → OmegaAccepted`).

**Result**:

| Batch | Problems | Verdict |
|-------|----------|---------|
| A3 R5 smoke (mathd_algebra_107) | 1 | Ok delta=0 (l4=0 l4e=9 cap=0 expected=9) |
| A3 R3.5 smoke (mathd_algebra_113) | 1 | Ok delta=0 (l4=0 l4e=9 cap=0 expected=9) |
| B3 R6 mini-M1 (8 problems) | 8 | 8/8 Ok delta=0 |
| **Total** | **10** | **10/10 Ok delta=0** |

FC1-INV1 hard invariant `expected_completed_attempts == l4 + l4e + capsule_anchored` holds for every smoke run on Stage A3 C2 substrate. SG-A3.8 ("real-LLM smoke produces 50/50-style invariant report") **NOW SATISFIED**.

## §3. Q2 forward-bind — env-var seam → explicit ctor arg

Gemini R1 Q2 verbatim concern: "TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH environment variable is an architecturally weak seam... introduces implicit, global state ('action at a distance')... fails in scenarios with multiple concurrent TuringOS instances within a single process".

**Counter-argument**: TuringOS is currently single-instance per process (the evaluator binary is invoked once per problem with isolated chaintape paths via per-problem env vars). The env-var seam is a documented runtime contract identical to the upstream `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH` already used by `RuntimeChaintapeConfig::from_env`. Multi-process concurrency is an architectural future state not currently in scope.

**Forward-bind**: Stage A3.6 enhancement TB will refactor `RejectionEvidenceWriter` to accept `chaintape_repo_path: Option<PathBuf>` as an explicit ctor field. The env-var hook can remain as a thin convenience wrapper that calls the explicit ctor.

**Severity**: low. Current usage is correct for single-instance contract.

## §4. Q8 forward-bind — refs/chaintape/cas GC-vulnerability + concurrency

Gemini R1 Q8 verbatim concerns:

(a) **GC vulnerability**: "refs/chaintape/cas points directly to the latest blob OID, leaving all historical CAS blobs unreferenced. A standard `git gc` would garbage-collect and delete this historical data".

(b) **Concurrency**: "non-atomic, race-prone way... If two processes write to CAS concurrently, one's ref update will be lost".

**Counter-argument (GC)**: The TuringOS CAS git repo is per-run (per-problem) and lives at `cas_path` which is NOT subject to `git gc` in normal operation. The CAS `.turingos_cas_index.jsonl` sidecar is the durable index; the ref is a derived view per FR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5. Per `feedback_evidence_packaging_policy_required` the repo is local-only artifact, not a long-lived production repo.

However, Gemini's concern is valid for FORWARD architectural robustness: if the C2 ref scheme is intended as canonical pointer per CR-A3-HEAD-T-C2.5, it should be a proper commit chain, not a blob-OID pointer. This is the architecturally clean design.

**Forward-bind (Q8a)**: Stage A3.6 enhancement TB will redesign `CasStore::put` ref-update logic so each put creates a new git commit (or batch commit per N puts):
- Tree references the new CAS object(s) by name
- Parent = previous commit on refs/chaintape/cas
- This forms a proper, GC-safe commit chain

**Forward-bind (Q8b)**: Once Q8a is in place, atomicity comes from git's underlying ref-update atomicity (single-process); for multi-process, file lock + compare-and-swap retry on ref update.

**Severity**: medium for forward-architecture robustness; low for current single-process usage.

## §5. Aggregate disposition

Per `feedback_audit_loop_roi_flip`: "When CHALLENGEs shift production-defects → architectural strengthening, ROI flipped; ship + forward-bind". Gemini R1's three CHALLENGE items split as:

- Q5 (production-defect: missing evidence) → **CLOSED this commit**
- Q2 (architectural-seam preference) → forward-bind (no production defect; refactor improves hygiene)
- Q8 (forward-architectural robustness) → forward-bind (no current-usage defect; needed for future multi-process / long-lived repo scenarios)

Stage A3 substrate ship eligibility:
- All 5 SG-A3 ship gates GREEN at gate level (constitution_head_t_c2_multi_ref.rs 7 tests)
- All 5 SG-A3 ship gates GREEN under real DeepSeek-LLM load (10/10 chain_invariant Ok delta=0 across A3 R5 + R3.5 + B3 R6 mini-M1)
- SG-A3.6 cargo test --workspace 1287 PASS / 0 failed (≥1181 baseline)
- SG-A3.7 constitution gates 154 GREEN / 0 failed (≥97 baseline)
- SG-A3.8 real-LLM smoke produces invariant report — CLOSED this commit
- SG-A3.9 OBS forward-binding for migration edges — THIS DOC
- SG-A3.10 G2 dual audit dispatched AFTER MVP gates green — Gemini done R1; Codex pending dispatch

**Codex dual-audit dispatch**: scheduled in this commit pair via `run_codex_stage_a3_r7_audit_2026-05-08.sh` (separate commit; results capture verdict).

**Stage A3 SHIPPED CANDIDATE pending Codex dual-audit closure**. Architect §8 sign-off path opens after Codex side returns.

## §6. Stage A3.6 forward TB scope (post-A3 ship)

Open items for follow-up Stage A3.6 enhancement TB:

1. `RejectionEvidenceWriter` explicit `chaintape_repo_path: Option<PathBuf>` ctor field (Class-3 additive; closes Gemini Q2)
2. `refs/chaintape/cas` redesign as proper commit chain anchoring CAS objects by name in commit tree (Class-3-or-4 depending on schema impact; closes Gemini Q8a GC vulnerability)
3. Atomic ref-update logic for multi-process safety (Class-3; closes Gemini Q8b once Q8a lands)
4. Optional: same commit-chain redesign for refs/chaintape/l4e (currently each rejection creates its own commit, which is already chain-safe; verify and document)

These are forward-bound; not blocking Stage A3 ship.

# Codex Stage A3 / HEAD_t C2 Multi-Ref ChainTape Audit — R1

You are Codex acting as the implementation-paranoid half of the Stage A3 R7 G2
dual audit per STAGE_A3_HEAD_T_C2_charter_2026-05-07.md §5 R7. Gemini 2.5 Pro
ran the strategic / architectural half in parallel (verdict: CHALLENGE; details
at handover/audits/GEMINI_STAGE_A3_R7_AUDIT_2026-05-08_R1.md).

Per feedback_dual_audit_conflict: VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS conservative ranking.
Round cap = 2.

## Your scope (implementation-paranoid)

Inspect each commit in the Stage A3 substrate lineage. Look for:

1. Subtle bugs in dual-write semantics (refs/transitions/main vs refs/chaintape/l4)
2. Bugs in the new advance_chaintape_l4e_to / advance_chaintape_cas_to helpers
3. Bugs in HeadTWitness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs (especially the cas_root sha256-of-OID derivation)
4. Bugs in the rejection_evidence flush_jsonl_record env-var hook (advance_l4e_ref_for_record)
5. Bugs in the CasStore::put hook
6. Test correctness in tests/constitution_head_t_c2_multi_ref.rs (do tests actually exercise the gates they claim?)
7. Trust Root rehash correctness in genesis_payload.toml

## Substrate at HEAD `90376ae`

Commit lineage:
- 72e2494 Stage A3 R1+R2+R4 (multi-ref support + HeadTWitness::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs + 6 SG-A3 tests)
- 4b0062e Stage A3 R3 (CAS root ref hook in cas/store.rs)
- 2d3d948 Stage A3 R5 smoke (mathd_algebra_107)
- f7a6660 Stage A3 R3.5 wire + smoke (10/10 1:1 ref-to-JSONL match)
- 381554f Stage B3 R6 mini-M1 (8 problems × n=1; 8/8 l4e_jsonl_match=true; 83 commits aggregate)
- 90376ae Stage A3 R7 Gemini R1 audit + Q5 closure (10/10 chain_invariant.json Ok delta=0)

Gemini R1 raised concrete remediations:
- Q5 (chain_invariant.json missing) — CLOSED in 90376ae (10/10 verdict=Ok delta=0)
- Q2 (env-var seam preference) — FORWARD-BIND to Stage A3.6
- Q8 (refs/chaintape/cas GC + concurrency) — FORWARD-BIND to Stage A3.6

## Your questions (implementation-paranoid)

Q1. Read `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs::Git2LedgerWriter::commit`. Is the dual-write to refs/chaintape/l4 atomically consistent with the C1 refs/transitions/main update? Specifically: if refs/chaintape/l4 ref-update fails AFTER refs/transitions/main commit succeeded, does the writer leave inconsistent state? Should the dual-write be reordered (chaintape/l4 first, then transitions/main as alias)?

Q2. Read `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs::flush_jsonl_record` + `advance_l4e_ref_for_record`. The git2 commit author/committer time uses `submit_id` cast to i64. submit_id is u64. Is there an overflow concern at very large submit_ids (>2^63)? Realistic? Defensible?

Q3. Read `src/state/head_t_witness.rs::reconstruct_from_chaintape_refs`. The cas_root mapping uses `Sha256::new() update("chaintape/cas/oid") update(o.as_bytes())`. Is this domain separator correctly disambiguating from other Hash usages in the codebase? Search src/ for any other Sha256 update with similar prefix that could collide.

Q4. Read `src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs::CasStore::put` (post-A3 R3). The advance_chaintape_cas_to call is `let _ = ...`. If this fails silently mid-batch, does the next put recover? Verify by tracing the recovery path.

Q5. Read `tests/constitution_head_t_c2_multi_ref.rs` all 7 tests. Do they actually fail under their stated kill conditions? Specifically: pick `sg_a3_no_hidden_filesystem_pointer` and identify any forbidden pointer name that should be on the list but isn't (potential coverage hole).

Q6. Read `genesis_payload.toml` for the entries that were rehashed by Stage A3 (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs`, `src/bottom_white/ledger/rejection_evidence.rs`, `src/runtime/mod.rs`). Are the rehash predecessor chains documented correctly? Any hash drift?

Q7. Stage A3 substrate intersects with rejection_evidence.rs (Class-3 per `feedback_class4_cannot_hide_in_class3` analysis: "rejection_evidence.rs is NOT in CLAUDE.md §12 STEP_B restricted list"). Verify: does the A3 R3.5 change to flush_jsonl_record cross any Class-4 line? ## Verdict format

```
Q1: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO - <reason>
Q2: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO - <reason>
Q3: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO - <reason>
Q4: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO - <reason>
Q5: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO - <reason>
Q6: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO - <reason>
Q7: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO - <reason>
Q8: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO - <reason>

## VERDICT: PASS|CHALLENGE|VETO
Conviction: low|medium|high
Recommendation: PROCEED|FIX-THEN-PROCEED|REDESIGN
Remediations:
- <only for CHALLENGE/VETO; actionable and scoped>
```

If any Q is VETO, aggregate verdict must be VETO. If any Q is CHALLENGE (no VETO), aggregate is CHALLENGE.

handover/directives/2026-05-03_TB13_TO_TB17_POST_TB12_ARCHITECT_RULING.md:19:According to a document from 2026-05-03, TB-12 的 shipped summary 显示它已按 NodeMarket Position Index 的窄化目标完成：`WorkTx.stake -> FirstLongPosition`、`ChallengeTx.stake -> ShortPosition`、`VerifyTx.bond != market position`、`NodePosition not Coin holding`，并且用户提供的结果显示 `cargo test --workspace = 759 / 0 / 150`、`8/8 SG-12.x`、`11/11 G-gates`、`14/14 forbidden tokens`、`9/9 immutable-record invariants` 均通过。这个阶段可以接受，但我没有在本轮直接读取到 GitHub commit `fa36eca` 的源码 diff；下面的审计以你提供的 TB-12 summary、已检索到的路线文档和前序宪法/白皮书约束为依据。

handover/audits/CODEX_MICRO_AUDIT_TB_18_PRE_H_VERDICT_2026-05-05.md:17:Q8 (Wire integrity): PASS — Ran `cargo test --workspace --release` in the current workspace; it exited 0. There are no `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, `src`, `experiments`, or `tests` tracked diffs from `0c3a5e1..HEAD`; only `experiments/minif2f_v4/h_vppu_history.json` is untracked under those paths. `cargo test --workspace --release -- --list` counted `1113` tests, and `--ignored --list` counted `150` ignored, so the successful run implies `963/0/150`, matching `handover/ai-direct/TB-18_B_PHASE4_SHIP_STATUS_2026-05-05.md:7` and `:132-134`. Trust-root rehash lineage is present at `genesis_payload.toml:164` for `evaluator.rs` and `genesis_payload.toml:280` for `src/state/typed_tx.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:162:| `cargo test --workspace` PASS | 187 | 204 | 234 | 254 | 261 | 261 | 264 | 265 |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:284:- (Q7.a) Verify `cargo test --workspace` PASS count: 265 / 29 ignored / 0 failed (post-A8e2; +1 over A8e from the new Python-conformance Rust wrapper). Spot-check by re-running.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5109:- A8e2 (post-A8e, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-2 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent): three dual-audit fixes. **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1) `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` wraps `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` in a Rust integration test, making the V3L-27 round-robin conformance gate run on every `cargo test --workspace`. **G2** (Codex R2#2) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 8` audit-requirements paragraph reworded — round-1 fix corrected § 2 but left § 8 still calling the substitution a "strictest plausible bar"; now consistent across the doc. Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5110:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:5159:| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |

Re-hashed in Trust Root. **G3** (Codex R2#3 + Gemini R2#2) `A8_EXIT_PACKET` updated for round-2 reality: § 2 cumulative-tests table extended with A8e/A8e2 columns, § 4 manifest-clean assertion bumped 30→34, § 5 round-1 risk #5 (no FC1-N12 in swarm verify path) removed (closed by F4), § 6 Q7.a/b numbers updated, round-2 outcome + round-3 fixes section appended; TRACE_MATRIX stale `run_corr_id` symbol row replaced; "in CI" softened to "via Rust wrapper post-A8e2". FC-trace: meta governance + V3L-27 mitigation gating (FC1-N7). Trust Root manifest 33 → 34 (`tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs`).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:6855:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:7947:    65	| `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` Rust→Python wrapper (A8e2 fix G1) | `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` | A8 round-2 audit (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1): the round-robin conformance battery in `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` was only manual; tests that don't run automatically are documentation, not gates. G1 wires the Python suite into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust integration test that shells to `python3` and asserts exit 0 + the unittest "OK" trailer. The V3L-27 mitigation (case C-027) is now a recurring conformance gate exercised on every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. | ✅ |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8054:    48	- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:8113:   244	- (Q7.a) Verify `cargo test --workspace` PASS count: 265 / 29 ignored / 0 failed (post-A8e2; +1 over A8e from the new Python-conformance Rust wrapper). Spot-check by re-running.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6505:   318	- **Q2** (Gemini R11#3 — substantive refactor): `make_pput` signature `Option<u64>` → plain `u64` for the 3 affected parameters. All 9 production call sites + 4 test fixtures updated. `wc.elapsed_ms()` returns `Option<u64>` upstream; call sites add `.unwrap_or(0)` at the boundary. `cargo test --workspace`: 267 PASS (was 265 + 2 new boot tests).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6623:   318	- **Q2** (Gemini R11#3 — substantive refactor): `make_pput` signature `Option<u64>` → plain `u64` for the 3 affected parameters. handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6717:   318	- **Q2** (Gemini R11#3 — substantive refactor): `make_pput` signature `Option<u64>` → plain `u64` for the 3 affected parameters. All 9 production call sites + 4 test fixtures updated. `wc.elapsed_ms()` returns `Option<u64>` upstream; call sites add `.unwrap_or(0)` at the boundary. `cargo test --workspace`: 267 PASS (was 265 + 2 new boot tests).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6795:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:27:- A8e (post-A7, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-1 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini VETO): six dual-audit fixes. **F1** (Codex#2 + Gemini Q4) `experiments/minif2f_v4/src/run_id.rs` mints one stable run_id per run, threaded into both `emit_event` and `make_pput` (replaces `run_corr_id` vs internal `run_id` ms drift; oneshot also stops using `oneshot_{problem_file}` placeholder). **F2** (Gemini VETO Q6) `scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` adds 15-test conformance battery — round-robin distributes [2,2,2] across 6 calls, single-key pool degrades to all-zero, two-key pool alternates [2,2]; exercises the V3L-27 mitigation without a live API. (A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:6840:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:318:- **Q2** (Gemini R11#3 — substantive refactor): `make_pput` signature `Option<u64>` → plain `u64` for the 3 affected parameters. All 9 production call sites + 4 test fixtures updated. `wc.elapsed_ms()` returns `Option<u64>` upstream; call sites add `.unwrap_or(0)` at the boundary. `cargo test --workspace`: 267 PASS (was 265 + 2 new boot tests).

(A8e2 G1 wires this into `cargo test --workspace` via a Rust shell-out wrapper — see § 2 below.) **F3** (Codex#4) routing matrix bug fixed — `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` was misrouting to DashScope because `m.startswith("qwen")` won after the slash check; now slash-form ⇒ siliconflow first, bare-qwen ⇒ dashscope second. **F4** (Codex#3) FC1-N12 emit added to all 3 swarm verify sites (`verify_omega_detailed` × 2 + `verify_partial`), each with verdict + elapsed_ms + path stamp; closes the swarm-side gap in the 5-step compile-loop witness. **F5** (Codex#5) `genesis_payload.toml` header + TRACE_MATRIX milestones reconciled to actual 31 entries (round-1 packet undercounted by 1 — agent_models.rs from A3 was already in TR before A1). **F6** (Codex#1) `PREREG_AMENDMENT § 2` wording corrected — `p_0=0.10` substitution is the LEAST-strict admissible ceiling (max tolerated value for `j-RR ≤ p_0`), NOT the strictest possible substitute as round-1 mistakenly claimed; explicit statistical-implications paragraph added (no Type-I inflation since `j-RR` is descriptive; substitution may be less protective than eventual calibrated `p_0 < 0.10`). FC-trace: FC1-N12 (oracle scope swarm coverage) + FC1-N7 (δ provider routing) + Art. V (amendment governance). Trust Root manifest 31 → 33 (run_id.rs + test_llm_proxy.py).
