/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba tests/constitution_closure_3_no_trivial_asserts.rs | sed -n '1,260p'" in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Constitution Landing Gate — Closure #3 (directive §12)
     2	//!
     3	//! `feedback_constitutional_harness_engineering` + CR-C0.1: a test that cannot
     4	//! fail is documentation, not a gate. CONSTITUTION_EXECUTION_MATRIX.md §O #3
     5	//! has sat at "🟡 AMBER (verify on commit)" because no executable enforcement
     6	//! existed — the rule was an editorial norm, not a mechanism.
     7	//!
     8	//! This test mechanically forbids trivially-passing assertions in the
     9	//! `tests/constitution_*.rs` battery (the constitutional gate surface where
    10	//! the kill-condition matters most). It is the mechanism that converts the
    11	//! norm into a gate per `feedback_norm_needs_mechanism`.
    12	//!
    13	//! Forbidden patterns (whitespace-tolerant): `assert!(true)`,
    14	//! `assert!(!false)`, `assert_eq!(1, 1)` (and tautological number/bool/string
    15	//! pairs), `assert_ne!(0, 1)` (and complements), `debug_assert!(true)`.
    16	//! Doc-comments mentioning these patterns are filtered (stripped before the
    17	//! scan). The scanning test itself is skipped to avoid self-reference.
    18	//!
    19	//! `FC-trace: FC1-INV1` — protects "every externalized claim of a passing
    20	//! invariant must be a real claim, not a stub" — the gate-surface analogue
    21	//! of the FC1 tape-visibility rule.
    22	
    23	use std::fs;
    24	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    25	
    26	const TESTS_DIR: &str = "tests";
    27	const SELF_FILE: &str = "constitution_closure_3_no_trivial_asserts.rs";
    28	
    29	/// Trivially-passing assertion shapes after whitespace removal. Each variant
    30	/// is unconditionally true at compile or run time; if a `#[test]` body
    31	/// reduces to one of these, the test is documentation, not enforcement.
    32	const FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS: &[&str] = &[
    33	    "assert!(true)",
    34	    "assert!(true,",
    35	    "assert!(!false)",
    36	    "assert!(!false,",
    37	    "debug_assert!(true)",
    38	    "debug_assert!(true,",
    39	    "debug_assert!(!false)",
    40	    "assert_eq!(1,1)",
    41	    "assert_eq!(0,0)",
    42	    "assert_eq!(true,true)",
    43	    "assert_eq!(false,false)",
    44	    "assert_ne!(0,1)",
    45	    "assert_ne!(1,0)",
    46	    "assert_ne!(true,false)",
    47	    "assert_ne!(false,true)",
    48	];
    49	
    50	fn collect_constitution_test_files() -> Vec<PathBuf> {
    51	    let project_root = env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR");
    52	    let dir = Path::new(project_root).join(TESTS_DIR);
    53	    let mut files = Vec::new();
    54	    let entries = fs::read_dir(&dir)
    55	        .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("cannot read {}: {e}", dir.display()));
    56	    for entry in entries.flatten() {
    57	        let path = entry.path();
    58	        let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {
    59	            Some(n) => n.to_string(),
    60	            None => continue,
    61	        };
    62	        if !name.starts_with("constitution_") || !name.ends_with(".rs") {
    63	            continue;
    64	        }
    65	        if name == SELF_FILE {
    66	            // Skip self — this file lists the patterns as string literals.
    67	            continue;
    68	        }
    69	        files.push(path);
    70	    }
    71	    files
    72	}
    73	
    74	/// Strip Rust line comments and remove all whitespace. The constitution test
    75	/// battery uses `//`, `///`, and `//!` exclusively for comments; no test file
    76	/// embeds `//` inside a string literal. So a line-level prefix split on `//`
    77	/// is sufficient for this surface (the scan only protects
    78	/// `tests/constitution_*.rs`, not arbitrary repo code).
    79	fn strip_comments_and_whitespace(content: &str) -> String {
    80	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(content.len());
    81	    for line in content.lines() {
    82	        let code = match line.find("//") {
    83	            Some(idx) => &line[..idx],
    84	            None => line,
    85	        };
    86	        for ch in code.chars() {
    87	            if !ch.is_whitespace() {
    88	                out.push(ch);
    89	            }
    90	        }
    91	    }
    92	    out
    93	}
    94	
    95	/// Closure #3 — every test in the constitution battery must be capable of
    96	/// failing. Trivially-passing assertion shapes are forbidden.
    97	#[test]
    98	fn constitution_closure_3_no_trivial_asserts_in_constitution_tests() {
    99	    let files = collect_constitution_test_files();
   100	    assert!(
   101	        !files.is_empty(),
   102	        "Closure #3 violation: no `tests/constitution_*.rs` files found — \
   103	         scan would vacuously pass. Did the test directory move?"
   104	    );
   105	
   106	    let mut hits: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   107	    for path in &files {
   108	        let content = fs::read_to_string(path)
   109	            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("cannot read {}: {e}", path.display()));
   110	        let stripped = strip_comments_and_whitespace(&content);
   111	        for pattern in FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS {
   112	            if stripped.contains(pattern) {
   113	                hits.push(format!(
   114	                    "{}: contains trivially-passing assertion `{}`",
   115	                    path.file_name().and_then(|s| s.to_str()).unwrap_or("<?>"),
   116	                    pattern
   117	                ));
   118	            }
   119	        }
   120	    }
   121	
   122	    assert!(
   123	        hits.is_empty(),
   124	        "Closure #3 violation (CR-C0.1): trivially-passing assertion(s) \
   125	         detected in constitution test battery. A test that cannot fail is \
   126	         documentation, not a gate. Replace with a real invariant, OR if the \
   127	         test is a structural placeholder, mark the row 🚫 N/A in \
   128	         `CONSTITUTION_EXECUTION_MATRIX.md` and remove the test. Hits:\n{}",
   129	        hits.join("\n")
   130	    );
   131	}
   132	
   133	/// Self-test of the strip helper — the comment/whitespace pipeline must
   134	/// remove pattern-text appearing inside a `//!` doc-comment so that the
   135	/// existing CR-C0.1 banners across the constitution battery (e.g.,
   136	/// `tests/constitution_fc1_runtime_loop.rs:23://! All tests are real
   137	/// assertions — no \`assert!(true)\` per CR-C0.1.`) do NOT cause this test to
   138	/// false-positive on the very files it protects.
   139	#[test]
   140	fn strip_helper_drops_doc_comment_pattern_text() {
   141	    let sample = "//! banner mentioning assert!(true) per CR-C0.1\n\
   142	                  fn body() { assert_eq!(real, expected); }\n";
   143	    let stripped = strip_comments_and_whitespace(sample);
   144	    assert!(
   145	        !stripped.contains("assert!(true)"),
   146	        "strip helper failed to remove doc-comment text: stripped={stripped:?}"
   147	    );
   148	    assert!(
   149	        stripped.contains("assert_eq!(real,expected)"),
   150	        "strip helper damaged real assertion: stripped={stripped:?}"
   151	    );
   152	}
   153	
   154	/// Self-test of the FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS list — synthesize each pattern in a
   155	/// freshly-built non-self string and confirm the scan would catch it. This
   156	/// proves the test "can fail" (Closure #3 applied to itself).
   157	#[test]
   158	fn forbidden_patterns_list_is_load_bearing() {
   159	    for pattern in FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS {
   160	        // Build a synthetic file body containing the pattern outside any
   161	        // comment. Use whitespace inside to confirm whitespace-tolerance.
   162	        let synthetic = format!("fn t() {{ {} }}\n", spaced(pattern));
   163	        let stripped = strip_comments_and_whitespace(&synthetic);
   164	        assert!(
   165	            stripped.contains(pattern),
   166	            "FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS entry {pattern:?} not detected in \
   167	             synthetic body {synthetic:?} (stripped={stripped:?}) — the \
   168	             scan would silently miss this shape."
   169	        );
   170	    }
   171	}
   172	
   173	/// Insert spaces inside a forbidden pattern to demonstrate whitespace-tolerance:
   174	/// `assert!(true)` → `assert! ( true )`. After strip, both shapes collapse to
   175	/// the same canonical substring.
   176	fn spaced(pattern: &str) -> String {
   177	    pattern
   178	        .chars()
   179	        .flat_map(|c| {
   180	            if c == '(' || c == ')' || c == ',' {
   181	                vec![' ', c, ' ']
   182	            } else {
   183	                vec![c]
   184	            }
   185	        })
   186	        .collect()
   187	}

tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:455:                 **Source**: `tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs::rq3_non_empty_tb13_chaintape_replays_with_state_root_match`\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:456:                 **Trigger**: Codex round-3 RQ3 finding (the existing real-LLM smoke proves the EconomicState 13-sub-field schema round-trips with EMPTY TB-13 maps; non-empty replay determinism was not directly evidenced) + Codex round-4 RQ3 follow-up (the round-5 closure overclaimed state-root equality as cryptographic proof of map equality — fixed in round-6 by adding a direct map-equality assertion via manual `replay_full_transition` re-replay).\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:463:                 - Live `state_root_t` (post-drain): `{live_root}`\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:464:                 - Replay `final_state_root_hex`: `{replay_root}`\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:471:                 2. Pre-shutdown live state has non-empty TB-13 maps (sanity).\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:472:                 3. `verify_chaintape` reconstructs a `QState` from the persisted runtime_repo + cas + initial_q_state.json + agent_pubkeys.json + pinned_pubkeys.json whose `final_state_root_hex` matches the live `state_root_t`. Codex round-4 follow-up clarification: the state-root mutator hashes `domain || prev_root || canonical_tx`, NOT the full QState — so state-root equality on its own proves deterministic tx-chain replay (same initial state + same canonical-encoded txs + same pure dispatcher → same root); it does NOT directly assert byte-equal QState reconstruction.\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:473:                 4. **Round-6 direct map-equality check**: the smoke also runs `replay_full_transition` manually against the persisted artifacts and asserts `replayed_q.economic_state_t.conditional_collateral_t == live_q.economic_state_t.conditional_collateral_t` AND `... .conditional_share_balances_t == ...` AND full `economic_state_t` equality. This is the direct map-equality evidence that closes RQ3 without relying on dispatch-determinism implication.\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:475:                 6. Two-tx state-root chain (initial → mint → redeem) replays deterministically.\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:480:                 - **Resolution mid-test flip**: `task-REDEEM` is pre-seeded as `Finalized` in `initial_q` rather than flipped via a system-emitted `FinalizeReward` / `TaskBankruptcy` mid-test. The state-flip mechanism itself is exercised by TB-8 / TB-11 integration tests; here we focus on the TB-13 mint+redeem chaintape replay determinism.\n\
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:485:                live_root = live_state_root_hex,
tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:486:                replay_root = final_state_root_hex,

codex
Read-only note: I could not create/update a saved audit file in this sandbox. Verbatim audit:

Q1: CHALLENGE - `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` updates `refs/transitions/main` first, then `refs/chaintape/l4`. If the C2 ref update fails, disk has advanced C1 while C2 is stale. The writer returns an error and does not advance in-memory state, but the repo is left divergent. Reorder so `refs/chaintape/l4` is primary and `refs/transitions/main` is a repairable alias, with open-time cross-check.

Q2: PASS - `submit_id as i64` can wrap above `i64::MAX`, producing invalid/negative deterministic git times. That requires more than 2^63 rejected submissions in one run, so it is not realistic for this substrate. Defensible, though a checked conversion would be cleaner.

Q3: PASS - The `cas_root` derivation uses `b"chaintape/cas/oid"` plus the OID bytes. Search found no competing `Sha256` domain with this prefix or a plausible in-code collision path.

Q4: CHALLENGE - `CasStore::put` silently ignores `advance_chaintape_cas_to`. A later distinct `put` advances the latest CAS ref, but the missed object is never represented by the ref. A retry of the same content does not recover because `index.contains_key(&cid)` returns early before ref advancement.

Q5: CHALLENGE - The SG-A3 tests mostly exercise happy paths and direct helpers, not failure gates. `sg_a3_no_hidden_filesystem_pointer` omits at least `LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt`, a known forbidden global-latest pointer pattern elsewhere in the constitution gates.

Q6: PASS - Current file hashes match `genesis_payload.toml` for `transition_ledger.rs`, `cas/store.rs`, `rejection_evidence.rs`, and `runtime/mod.rs`. The predecessor chains are documented correctly. Minor note: `runtime/mod.rs` is marked Stage B3, not Stage A3.

Q7: PASS - The A3 R3.5 `flush_jsonl_record` change stays Class-3. It does not modify `RejectedSubmissionRecord` schema, the `compute_hash` byte stream, or sequencer admission semantics.

Q8: PASS - Mentally, the claimed totals are consistent with the available evidence: `1287 PASS / 0 failed` and `154 GREEN / 0 failed`. I did not execute the suites in this read-only sandbox. No CR-C0.1 trivial-assert vacuity found in the A3 gate, but its coverage holes are captured in Q5.

## VERDICT: CHALLENGE
Conviction: high
Recommendation: FIX-THEN-PROCEED
Remediations:
- Make `refs/chaintape/l4` the canonical write target, then update `refs/transitions/main` as an alias; add open-time divergence detection or repair.
- Stop silently losing CAS ref failures: log/surface the error and add repair from sidecar/open, or convert CAS ref updates into a real commit chain.
- Expand SG-A3 tests with ref-update failure injection, exact `cas_root` digest assertion, real `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH` flush coverage, and hidden-pointer coverage including `LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt`.

Read-only note: I could not create/update a saved audit file in this sandbox. Verbatim audit:

Q1: CHALLENGE - `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` updates `refs/transitions/main` first, then `refs/chaintape/l4`. If the C2 ref update fails, disk has advanced C1 while C2 is stale. The writer returns an error and does not advance in-memory state, but the repo is left divergent. Reorder so `refs/chaintape/l4` is primary and `refs/transitions/main` is a repairable alias, with open-time cross-check.

Q2: PASS - `submit_id as i64` can wrap above `i64::MAX`, producing invalid/negative deterministic git times. That requires more than 2^63 rejected submissions in one run, so it is not realistic for this substrate. Defensible, though a checked conversion would be cleaner.

Q3: PASS - The `cas_root` derivation uses `b"chaintape/cas/oid"` plus the OID bytes. Search found no competing `Sha256` domain with this prefix or a plausible in-code collision path.

Q4: CHALLENGE - `CasStore::put` silently ignores `advance_chaintape_cas_to`. A later distinct `put` advances the latest CAS ref, but the missed object is never represented by the ref. A retry of the same content does not recover because `index.contains_key(&cid)` returns early before ref advancement.

Q5: CHALLENGE - The SG-A3 tests mostly exercise happy paths and direct helpers, not failure gates. `sg_a3_no_hidden_filesystem_pointer` omits at least `LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt`, a known forbidden global-latest pointer pattern elsewhere in the constitution gates.

Q6: PASS - Current file hashes match `genesis_payload.toml` for `transition_ledger.rs`, `cas/store.rs`, `rejection_evidence.rs`, and `runtime/mod.rs`. The predecessor chains are documented correctly. Minor note: `runtime/mod.rs` is marked Stage B3, not Stage A3.

Q7: PASS - The A3 R3.5 `flush_jsonl_record` change stays Class-3. It does not modify `RejectedSubmissionRecord` schema, the `compute_hash` byte stream, or sequencer admission semantics.

Q8: PASS - Mentally, the claimed totals are consistent with the available evidence: `1287 PASS / 0 failed` and `154 GREEN / 0 failed`. I did not execute the suites in this read-only sandbox. No CR-C0.1 trivial-assert vacuity found in the A3 gate, but its coverage holes are captured in Q5.

## VERDICT: CHALLENGE
Conviction: high
Recommendation: FIX-THEN-PROCEED
Remediations:
- Make `refs/chaintape/l4` the canonical write target, then update `refs/transitions/main` as an alias; add open-time divergence detection or repair.
- Stop silently losing CAS ref failures: log/surface the error and add repair from sidecar/open, or convert CAS ref updates into a real commit chain.
- Expand SG-A3 tests with ref-update failure injection, exact `cas_root` digest assertion, real `TURINGOS_CHAINTAPE_PATH` flush coverage, and hidden-pointer coverage including `LATEST_MARKOV_CAPSULE.txt`.
